title: Navy Girl Aerial Demonstration Variant
description: Fighting Magical Girl, Mayflower Shipman, aka Navy Girl has swapped her traditional dress whites for the colors she wears for armed forces day demonstrations. She is accompanied by her Specialized Kinetic Yield, High Altitude Weapon Carrier, or SKYHAWC. (If anyone can find out how to swap a second "K" in there, I'm open to suggestion.)

title: Faculty
description: Guidance counsellor Loki and principle...Virgil. (Yaaayyy! I remember now. ) As I recall, their only issue with their devil horned, forked tailed student, is that she was always late to the bell. I really need to read that script again.

title: Blue Demon
description: Everyone I showed the previous incarnation of this picture was in unanimous agreement that I used entirely too much pink. Here's hoping I didn't over correct. ^^'

title: Magical Girl Diva
description: As far as I can tell, the demon girl Diva finally gets her angelic wish. Really I don't care, so long as I get to draw a magical girl transformation.

title: Evil Diva
description: Here is a proposed cover for a long dead series from the independent studio Angry Viking Press. The comic was Evil Diva, and was about a demon girl who wanted to be an angel. The whole thing took place in a demonic high school, I believe that steeple fingers dude was the principal.

comments.body: was this the girl playing in the snow?
Yes !! Thanks for noticing.

title: Princess Nine Commission
description: Artwork from an old drawing of Izumi and Ryo, from the anime, Princess Nine. I still need to find references for Koshien Stadium. I hope this anime gets a reboot. It was great music, and a compelling story, but the animation got cheaper with every episode. This is one series that deserves modern production values.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1810.01
description: Using this year's Inktober to practice drawing my Streamers characters and machines. Captain Ariel McFly aka Air Force Girl.

title: Anti Tank Girl
description: Army Girl, demonstrates her capabilities as an anti-armor asset, against T.H.R.E.A.T.'s1 fabled Werewolf Brigade during the Battle of Thar. 1.) Transformative Hegemony to Rule the Earth for All Time.

comments.body: "BAD DOGGIE!!!"
Hah! That's perfect!

title: 45.35 Kg MBT
description: While the only Streamer incapable of sustained flight, Army Girl can deadlift 50,000 tons, run at mach 0.75, and survive a 15 kiloton detonation, making her an ideal ground operations specialist. With her speed and toughness, she can negate most T.H.R.E.A.T. mobile armor, effectively putting her in the role of a human, main battle tank. Her chief weapons are high explosive P.O.R.T.I.A.* grenades. (Each one can disable a tank. Their ball gowns fragment into shrapnel.) Although she is capable of carrying a great diversity of heavy ordinance, up to, and beyond a 130mm Anti-Infrastructure Rifle. *Poseable Ordinance Resin Ten Inch Action figures. Pictured above.

comments.body: What's the overall kinetic force that her punches can deliver? (no need for numbers, but what could she potentially punch through?)

Well she can punch an opposing tank with sufficient force to detonate its magazine, and pop off its turret like a cork. Actually, she could punch off the turret without having to detonating anything. She can probably cripple any moving vehicle, if striking the right place, like cracking a submarine, or sinking a battleship.There probably isn't much she couldn't wreck.
And I wager her body's toughness is up to the task for taking punishment as well...

Yes. Air Force Girl once destroyed a hardened, underground T.H.R.E.A.T. installation, by carrying Army Girl into near earth orbit, before descending into an improvised dive bombing run, which would see the ground attack specialist used as an invulnerable bunker buster. Army Girl, who thought she was being being carried on a reconnaissance mission, was not amused by this situation.
Ah, the good old Fastball Special.
Army Girl is just too much fun. I love the gaiters!
Still need to think up a drone for her. Don't know what animal to use. Needs to be something that could go well with tank treads.

title: Hench Ride
description: What you see, hurriedly sketched above is a Ka-50 Black Shark attack helicopter. What ride could be more appropriate for the villainous pilots, mutated into hideous shark men, by T.H.R.E.A.T.1, with their heinous C.I.R.C.E.2 weapon? 1.) the Transformative Hegemony to Rule the Earth for All Time!! 2.) Chromasomal and Intramolecular Re-Combination Emitter.

comments.body: I wonder if there are some victims of the CIRCE weapons that have decided to use their new abilities to fight for good... They are... the M-MEN! (M for Moreau)
Nope. All evil. However some are permanent, and some can be cured. I haven't determined the time period, but after a while the C.I.R.C.E. effect is irreversible.
unfortunate...

Holy cow. It just occurred to me that there could be good C.I.R.C.E. victims. In fact, since vast segments of the population who are transformed, may end up being permanently altered, even after being liberated of T.H.R.E.A.T. mind control, they may be forced to live out their lives, post war, as intelligent animal/human hybrids. I think I may have stumbled upon a backdoor origin to my other concepts.
Heh... welcome to the future. AND IT'S WILD!!!
That is some hardcore work John! We gotta meet up again for arts and chat, was great when ya joined N8dogg5k and I at Otakon a few years back, before they went DC!
I'll be at Otakon again this year. Hopefully with a full comic.
OHhh awesome!

title: Hench Shark
description: Every hero needs good solid punch fodder, to justify their existence. For Coast Guard Girl, it's the mutated animal soldiers, of the Transformative Hegemony to Rule the Earth for All Time. Like the mutated Ka-50 Shark pilot pictured, the altered soldiers retain their skills and training, but their minds are under the thrall of T.H.R.E.A.T..* *I decided to get rid of the second "R".

title: A Girl and Her Drone
description: Coast Guard Girl is assisted in the task of rescuing downed Streamers, and civilians, endangered by the evil forces of T.H.R.R.E.A.T.1 by an artificially intelligent Deep Ocean/Littoral Patrol High-speed INterceptor drone. D.O/L.P.H.IN. can deploy flotation devices, and even serve as a life support pod for deep sea rescue. The drone can also carry a prodigious weapons loadout of torpedoes and depth charges for undersea combat. With its advanced AI, D.O/L.P.H.IN. can also offer tactical advice2 to is operator, which Coast Guard Girl uniformly ignores. 1.)Transformative Hegemony, Regimented to Rule the Earth for All Time. 2.) Said advice is offered in a cute, high pitched voice.

comments.body: ...she's MARINE GIRL, brave, and free...fighting e-vil 'neath the sea...! sorry couldn't resist...her design is very similar to one of my childhood favorite characters...nicely done...!

Finally, someone gets the homage. Also, since every branch of the service gets one, there is a Marine Corps Girl. She doesn't have a drone, though. Just a 100mm Howitzer, modified to be carried as a rifle. Her mission isn't rescue.
nicely done...btw, all i had to do to recognize the reference was be a very old anime nerd...which hardly seems like an accomplishment, but thanks...!
space dolphin?
No, just a robot. It's still aquatic.

title: The Fifth Streamer
description: After many requests, I finally decided to add Coast Guard Girl to the ranks of the Streamers. I'm actually liking how this color scheme came out. The U.S. Coast Guard has some cool looking stuff.

comments.body: When the other streamers need rescue, or there's bystanders in distress, always bet on Orange. One question, that orange vest puff up to serve as a flotation device? Trying to rescue flailing civilians requires some added buoyancy to keep from drowning the survivor.
She is backed up by a Deep Ocean/Littoral Patrol High-speed INterceptor drone, that can deploy flotation devices.
Now that is neat.

title: Swim War 02
description: Yet more antebellum fan art for Fred Perry's Gold Digger. The apocalyptic level water gun battle wages on. Now risen to even more ridiculous levels by Ace. I wanted to ramp this up to a climactic satellite strike, but at that point even my own prodigious suspension of disbelief began to show signs of wear.

comments.body: No it can go further... stealth fighter piloted by Skippy, loaded with heat-seeking water balloon missiles.
I like the way you think.
I'm a old Gold Digger fan... I know how things operate... :3
That was the ultimate attack at the time I came up with this. Now I'm considering aqua-satellite strikes, and water mushroom clouds. H20 Bomb?
Boosted yield dihydrogen oxide cluster munitons launch!!!
R2 peeboo... Oh that's a terrible pun and you should feel bad for making it. LOL - Polecat
Yea I know. Sometimes I just can't help myself.
The bomber is a great touch loved it!
Thank you.

title: Swim War 01
description: More antebellum fan art for Fred Perry's Gold Digger. I'm sorry that I can't find everything to this sequence. Basically a squirt gun battle is raging between the Diggers sisters, and Brianna has chosen to escalate conflict in her own signature style.

comments.body: "Of course you know this means war."

title: Skippy Sketches  02
description: Some more sketches, or rather a protean page of pencils, featuring my favorite character in Fred Perry's voluminous Gold Digger pantheon, Skippy Von Richthofen. Keen eyed viewers will notice that I posted an earlier version of the final panel, posted elsewhere in my gallery, under the title Missing Missiles. In this version, I added the vectored thrust exhaust, as well as the obligatory, anime inspired speed lines. (And yet I still didn't correct that oddly angled nose cone. ) Side Note: I used the Su-35 Terminator as Skippy's jet because I've seen it referred to elsewhere as a "tri-wing" due to its unique combination of a forward canard, in addition to a main wing, and a movable tail. I figure this qualifies it as a perfect vehicle for the the descendant of the Red Baron.

comments.body: The Skipmeister is absurd glorious fun, and an endearing tribute to more aerial combat tropes than I care to reference. It looks like grand & glorious fun.

When my friends ask me, "What character would you like to draw a comic of?" I usually lie, and through out some super hero or another, because I'm fairly certain they've never heard of my real answer--Skippy!!!

I hear you. I have a minor fandom for many supporting characters that I admire, but are never going to be popular. Or maybe they are shows that preceded the Internet era, and so never got instant world-wide exposure.
Okay, THAT is a lot of missiles to dodge. Better hope the anti-air works... - Polecat

Before it was revealed that Skippy could out fly the balance of her Night Flight comrades, she was introduced as the weapons procurement officer. I'm sure she has the best countermeasures mercenary money can buy.

title: Skippy Sketches  01
description: Here are some random sketches and a thunbnail I did, featuring my favorite character in Fred Perry's voluminous Gold Digger pantheon, Skippy Von Richthofen.

comments.body: For some reason the short-haired girl makes me think of Tracer.... - Polecat
Tracer?
Overwatch's cover girl: playoverwatch.com/en-us/heroes... - Polecat

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.31
description: Coming full circle with my final Inktober entry. The U.S.S. Enterprise, no bloody "A", "B", "C", "D", "E", "F", "G", "H", "I", and ESPECIALLY NOT "J"!!! Have you seen the "J"? It looks all spindly and weird. Yuck! Anyway, enjoy the pic. John out.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.30
description: Upon reflection, I probably should have dubbed the last week "Dominion Week", since all of the vessels I've drawn have been ships of the Jem'Hadar or their allies. Today is no different, as I round things out with a chibi-fied Jem'Hadar Heavy Cruiser. Judging from the crescent like, almost chiropteran pylons, sweeping forth from the vessels's primary hull, I would guess that DS9's production designer's followed Matt Jeffries time honored custom of patterning aggressor vehicles after predatory animals; in this case, some form of giant bat. Unfortunately, I always felt the twin warp nacelles, mounted adjacent to its center superstructure, made this warp 9.6, 2,500 Jem'Hadar troop carrying weapons platform more closely resemble some manner of big giant bunny rabbit. But that's just me.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.28
description: Think I'll ratchet up the villainous threat level with today's Inktober chibi. At an astonishing 4265 feet in length, the massive Dreadnaught Carrier is easily the largest, and most powerful of the conventional warships deployed by the major powers during the Dominion War. It's essentially akin to an Imperial Class Star Destroyer, from the Star Wars universe, only with Star Trek weapons;so it can level a planet by itself, and its Jem'Hadar troops don't have to hire out for disintegrations .

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.27
description: Today's chibi villain, the beetle-like fighter that is the mainstay of the Jeh'Hadar space forces. It's just called a fighter. You'd figure they'd have gone with a unique and descriptive moniker, like Scarab or something. I guess the plain name is to reflect the lack of imagination among the genetically engineered troopers.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.24
description: One more week left in Inktober, and my chibi-Star Trek vessel series, so I think I'll shift my attention now to bad guy ships. First on the docket, the distinctive fork tailed, Egyptian ankh inspired, Cardassian Galore Class Warship. With a comparitively modest top speed of warp eight, and a weapons load markedly inferior to the capital ships of three major Alpha Quadrant powers, the Galores, which made up the bulk of the Cardassian fleet, were frequently deployed in groups of three to make up for their shortcomings, and used primarily to enforce the will of the Cardassian Union upon less developed civilizations. Basically, it's a school yard bully with a warp drive.

title: Inktober161023
description: No this isn't the NX-Class U.S.S Enterprise, of the 22nd century. (BTW, could someone explain why the 22nd century w-5 ships weren't simply called the Enterprise Class, since she was the lead vessel in the series?) This is an Akira class ship, which would first appear in "Star Trek: First Contact", and would later be seen, spectacularly burning and exploding in the back ground of many a battle scene on Deep Space Nine. (Seriously, the Akira's were the redshirts of the fleet.) I'm amused by the name of this ship class, as it is yet another nod, by the Star Trek production staff to Japanese anime, just like: -The Enterprise-D's sister ship, U.S.S Yamato which was a reference to the fabled Space Battleship Yamato anime, as was the aliens from the obviously named Gamelan Five. -The Ferenghi access code, "kayee yooree dahtayee", which was a reference to the characters Kei and Yuri of the "Dirty Pair" anime. -The Nausican's, which were both low rent Klingon wannabes, and a shout out the the Hayao Miyazaki's "Nausicaa of the Valley Wind". If you hear a name in a TNG era Star Trek episode, that sounds similar to something you've heard in anime, know that it is not a coincidence.

comments.body: This is the ship that I fly in StarTrek Online. It's a fun ship with the ability to pump out photon torpedoes like nobody's business. The moment I saw the thumbnail, I got warm fuzzies.
I'd like to play this game, but I fear it would sabotage the progress I've made this month toward reestablishing my drawing habit.

This is true, it totally would suck up your free time. But man, it was made for you. Across all my different characters, I fly the modernized versions of the Akira, Olympic, Galaxy-X, Ferengi D'Kora, and Klingon Somraw. It's a lot of fun.

ISTR there was also a mythical element "Keiyurium", also a Dirty Pair ref, and in one early TNG episode, there was some odd fighting dojo they set up in the holodeck, and the walls were decorated with stylized Japanese text with anime character names on it. I think a lot of that traces back to Michael Okuda.
The Tech Manual, which Okuda contributed to, said that keiyurium was an essential part the holodeck tech. Which tells me why it was always malfunctioning. ;-}
Wow, thanks for the info!!! Now can anyone tell me why ships of Archer's era weren't simply called the Enterprise Class?!
Bureaucracy. What I don't get is why they avoided the ultimate Fannish ref by not having Archer say "Oh boy!" after being the first successful human teleportation.
Maybe they shown blow up many times but hell no other Federation ship has that much Torpedo firepower than the Akira!!! 15 torpedo tubes!
True. Would be nice to have seen them kicking bore butt.

Indeed! Weird how they choose this design for the NX class but with alterations to the aft part instead of going to the Daedalus type: www.ditl.org/ship-page.php?Cla... www.ditl.org/ship-pictures.php...

I recall reading that the show runners for Enterprise had never watched The Original Series, and hence lacked the proper fundamental knowledge of its history. One line of dialogue in Balance of Terror pretty much negates the level of technology depicted in Enterprise. SPOCK: "As you may recall from your histories, this conflict was fought, by our standards today, with primitive atomic weapons and in primitive space vessels, which allowed no quarter, no captives. Nor was there even ship-to-ship visual communication. Therefore, no human, Romulan, or ally has ever seen the other." Beam weapons, viewing screens, and tranporters, should not even be in evidence on the NX-01. And I'll stop here before I go down the warm, seductive rabbit hole of Enterprise bashing.

I've only seen a few TOS episodes as it not really arrived to Hungary (TNG, Voyager, DS9 and NX did arrive) but by reading about the lore I agree with you. Though I think the NX series wasn't that bad, I wonder what will bring the new series?

The NX-01's are bad designs, they just have no place in a prequel series. In my opinion a blockier, less sophisticated looking craft, like the Daedalus, you mentioned, would be far more appropriate for the period depicted.
Like some of these designs: www.shipschematics.net/startre... Especially the first the Europa class or the Dreadnought
These are interesting. The design of the Bonaventure here is quite a bit different than how it appears on the animated series. vignette1.wikia.nocookie.net/m...

There are many Bonaventures posted on the site, the one you referring to is looks like this: www.shipschematics.net/startre... Other with this name are these: www.shipschematics.net/startre... www.shipschematics.net/startre... www.shipschematics.net/startre... www.shipschematics.net/startre... www.shipschematics.net/startre... The full list of Federation Star Trek ships of the site: www.shipschematics.net/startre... Many drawings are simple modifications of existing drawings but you can find many nice designs as well with a few quality ones. The site is on since the early 2000's so many designs are quite old now and not of high quality (large resolution)

I considered the animated series designs canon, since some of them would end up in the remastered effects shots, when they updated original series effects for the 40th anniversary. www.ex-astris-scientia.org/sch... www.ex-astris-scientia.org/sch... BTW, the remastering that was done 10 years ago was way better than the "Greedo shot first" Star Wars remastering. They story was never changed, they just added in the ships that were described in dialog on the show, but were unable to film originally, due to production costs.
Indeed. I was surprised to discover that the TOS Star Trek barely had 3 seasons, yet it created such a fan base and culture! That ship is listed on the DITL: www.ditl.org/ship-page.php?Cla...

I am always fascinated when I encounter people who are only now encountering the original series. It's nice to see that new fans, who did not grow up with the show, are still engage by its story telling. It's quite a testament to the quality of its production.

It's not like I encounter it now, I've seen a few episodes some years ago but never seen the entire show nor did it entirely arrived here. While the story is good, the show is way too old it's a bit annoying to see all the props which are made from mostly paper or textile, like the early episodes of TNG, it greatly reduces the immersion feeling in me. Also we (well my parents) seen a similar show which was German made: Space Patrol – The Fantastic Adventures of the Spaceship Orion en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Raumpatr... www.youtube.com/watch?v=vJI4J9... it was dubbed in Hungarian as well: www.youtube.com/watch?v=nEHNl2...

Well I can't fault you if you find the dated props an impediment to a successful suspension of disbelief. While I still find many of the gadgets portrayed in Star Trek well conceived and prophetic of many modern innovations, I grew up looking at them, and have hence developed an invulnerable armor of nostalgia, regarding humbleness of their origins. Personally, I think it best to look at the original Star Trek in comparison to other science fiction programs of the same age, like Lost in Space, or Planet of the Giants. Despite its dated trappings, there is a gravitas to Star Trek that he other, more juvenile programs sorely lacked. I think it was this more mature and dramatic sensibility that unltimately transcends the fact that Doctor McCoy did surgery, using salt shakers as futuristic scalpels. Space Patrol is very interesting. It amuses me how different groups that have no interaction can come up with similar ideas. For instance, I recently learned that the toy company Mego was developing a line of military action figures, based around a fictional anti terrorism strike force, at almost the same time Hasbro was developing its now legendary G.I. Joe reboot.

It's not the gadgets like phaser pistols, tricorders (were they called them in TOS or it had a different name?) communicators, transporters etc that got me alienated on the cotnrary those and the ship designs which caught me, what aliented or what I dislike are the props: stones, alien costumes, caves etc which look like made from paper. Something like the personal shield effect of the first Borg drone beamed aboard the Enterprise though that effect is still much better compared to what we seen in David Lynch's Dune but that is due to the limitations of computer graphics back then! Speaking of which didn't thought of drawing a Borg cube or Tactical cube in super deformed mode? I know there were an earlier Lost in Space movie but this was the first one I've seen and I really like it (Especially the music!): www.youtube.com/watch?v=tsmacm... Indeed it's called something like convergent evolution I've noticed this in real word warship designs as well where two nations who are at war with each other and does not have information on the other's ship designs developed the same kind of vessel!

Between the obvious otaku on the writing staff and the IDW crossovers (Green Lantern, Doctor Who, Aliens), I do not ever want to get crap for writing crossovers that I put obsessive amounts of research into to get them to make sense.

First, have you read the Star Trek/Planet of the Apes crossover, entitled Primate Directive? In my opinion, it's the best of the IDW crossovers to date. Second, you shouldn't have to apologize for doing your research. I have no problem with people who want to get things right.

I am super-behind in reading or watching ANYTHING (other than Trek chronologically instead of air dates) since I'm kind of in a student-mommy hole, so no. But I'm interested in reading the Aliens crossover because my co-writer wrote just such a standalone novel in the late 90s/early 2000s. But the semester is almost over...I wonder how much Netflix and Star Trek Online I can cram into four months. ^_^ And thank you. People might say my OC is a Mary Sue, I say she's too flawed and I write in comic book tropes on a Star Trek playground...not like there's not a precedent for that either.

I did hear about the Aliens crossover. I heard it would be with TNG, which strikes me as odd, since the darker, more strife ridden DS9 would seem a more appropriate setting. I hope it's good, Not every crossover has lived up to the hype. (Star Trek/X-Men, anyone.)
My co-writer did in fact write it in the aftermath of the Dominion War. Secret Dominion base, cut off once the war ended, remote area of a quadrant...Aliens that need ketracel white...^_^

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.21
description: Today's entry, an chibified Big -E. Or perhaps, "The Little 'E'" would be more accurate.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.20
description: Say it with me now. "EXCELSIOR!!!"

comments.body: Here she is in colour: tzoli.deviantart.com/art/NCC-2...
That was fast!
Well it was a simple work Shading would take more time, I will think about it if I should add or not!

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.19
description: Today's Inktober Star Trek vessel is the Peregrine Class Attack Fighter. Although small craft operation is a mainstay in Starfleet officer training, their use as tactical assets was eschewed by the dreadnought oriented organization, which viewed fighter combat as a tactic favored by "developing cultures". This attitude changed when Maquis resistance fighters, many of them, expatriate Starfleet officers, armed Peregrine Class courier ships, and used them in hit and run tactics to hold both Starfleet and the Cardassian Militia capital ships at bay, during the three year post treaty border disputes. After outbreak of war with the Dominion, many of the former resistance members were repatriated into the fleet, bringing with them a rediscovered appreciation for the long neglected art of orbital combat maneuvering. Starfleet was suddenly in the business of building space fighter business.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.17
description: Today's chibi-fied ship of the line is the USS Defiant, otherwise known as Captain Benjamin Lafayette Sisko's Everlovin' Pimp Hand! Class leader in a new generation of "escort vessels", the Defiant was mostly intended to be a test bed for new weapons technologies, that would find use in the larger and more prestigious Sovereign class of starships. Were it not for the emergence of the Dominion threat, it is possible this potent warship would have spent its operational life as a curiosity, forever moored in a geostationary orbit over Utopia Planitia. Given the effectiveness the Defiant class vessels world demonstrate during the ensuing Dominion conflict, I can't help but wonder why Starfleet kept building Galaxy's and Sovereigns, when the resources used for each could have yielded ten of the "tough little ships". If I had my druthers, space would be bleached with angry swarms of these little badasses. But that's just me.

comments.body: The Defiant is one of my favourite, fictional starships. Great art of the Defiant, I think the only reason that more Defiant's weren't built, was because the team wanted it to remain as Sisko's trademark ship.
That makes a good deal of sense.
Chibi Valkyrie Mach-III's then?
Macross, or North American.
Dear lord I don't even think I'm familiar with Macross through anything other than what used to be the FFML...so I'm gonna say North American.

A chibi XB-70 would be cute. Macross is known to most Americans in its translated incarnation, Robotech. It is the story of Earthlings reverse engineering a crashed alien warship, to make a badass outer space aircraft carrier to take the fight to the distant aliens, come to collect their lost property. Macross is credited for inspiring the "real robot" trend, that was prevalent in anime in the 1980's. Prior to Macross machines depicted in anime, where primary colored magic machines, with only the most perfunctory suggestions of functionality. In the hands of aerospace engineer-turned-production-designer Shouji Kawamori the vehicles of Macross took on a look of real, working war machines . (They were based in part upon the F-14 Tomcat.) After Macross, and perhaps even to this day, anime relished in a celebration of technical detail and mechanical verisimilitude, that caught the eye of fans the world over. It's more than old school. It's the damn headmaster.

Wasn't there a Macross spinoff that had to do with singers or music? I do know Robotech a bit better. Watched a likely butchered version on Toonami years ago (decades at this point? Oh dear.) But I have two friends who are VERY into Robotech...collecting the import model kits and putting them together and such. I never got into it because it seemed too big to watch all of it. I THINK my co-writer borrowed parts of it for one of our stories. I might as well admit it, I put a whole planet of animes in the Delta Quadrant. I guess my whole thing is I enjoy the challenge of the "shared universe" concept a little too much, I was writing it before people decided there was a Pixar shared universe. I like your exchange with your friend Phil. We tried to write a friend's Highlander OC into our later novels, but since we lost touch with said friend, she lasted one novel. But playing with reading auras/sensing energy with my OC being telepathic (I SWEAR she's not a Mary Sue, not since I figured out I was writing telepathy as an allegory to Autism!) was kind of a neat scene. But I can see where a lot of dA artists love those details, even if they're not in the Macross fandom.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.16
description: Rather than stumbling upon a theme, I'm just going to declare the next seven days, "Fleet Week", and draw chibified versions of Federation starships, in service during the TNG/DS9/VOY period. First up is the USS Phoenix. This is a ship of the Nebula class, which uses many of the hull assemblies of the fabled Galaxy class, only arranged in such a way as to give the vessel a more compact profile. (The Nebula is to the Galaxy, what the Miranda class is to the Constitution.) Beyond its unique placement of warp nacelles below the saucer section, the most distinguishing feature of Nebula ships are the powerful sensor dishes the carry above and after of their main saucers. Despite their compact profile, Nebula class vessels are extremely potent weapons platforms, as evidenced by Captain Maxwell opening up a can of De''e' pup upon the Cardassian fleet, in the episode, "The Wounded".

comments.body: Though this is the AWACS pod equipped Nebula it is one of my favourite classes in Star Trek together with the Excelsior (Especially the Enterprise-B variant) and Miranda classes
The thumbnails did not show
Try these. johnstaton.deviantart.com/art/... johnstaton.deviantart.com/art/...
Yeah I saw those, will try to colour them if you agree with it
Sure thing.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.15
description: Alright. A Romulan Kestel-Class high speed, diplomatic shuttle. For realsies this time.

comments.body: Angry Warbird?
The sequel.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.14
description: So I guess I've inadvertantly made this into "Shuttlecraft Week". Today's Inktober entry is a Surak-type, Federation high speed courier/scout shuttle. I've always had a soft spot for this design, even it it only appeared briefly in Star Trek: The Motion Picture, to facilitate Spock's grand entrance. The idea of a standard shuttle pod, mated with a warp nacelle module to increase it's speed and range, is just plain neat. Plus, it just looks cool.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.13
description: As indicated by his earlier sketches, Matt Jeffries (Star Trek production designer, and reason all service crawlways on Starfleet vessels and installations are referred to as "Jeffries Tubes".) had originally intended the Class F "Galileo" Shuttlecraft to be more aerodynamic. His initial design for the craft featured a teardrop shaped main hull, and prominent gull wings. With the production techniques available at the time of the original series, the Aeroshuttle, with its wealth of curved surfaces, proved too complex to build on a limited television production schedule. The Galileo was given a more television production friendly redesign by Gene Winfield, a designer, working for a subsidiary of Aluminum Model Toys, who funded to the shuttle's construction. Winfield took his design cues, from automobiles, specifically, the 1962 Studebaker Avanti, which is likely why the resultant design bears that "orbital minivan" aesthetic.

comments.body: love the little poots
And to think I almost forgot them this time.
The forward door is an interesting design touch.
One on each side. Just following Jeffries' designs.
Dang, really loving the ship design and work that you've got going on John!
I appreciate it! After hanging with you and Nate at this past Otakon, it really hit me that I need to get back in the habit of hitting the drawing board again. Inktober is a godsend foe that.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.12
description: A mercifully simple to draw Class F, "Galileo" Shuttlecraft, deploys its recessed landing skids, and makes it's final approach, just in time for today's Inktober entry. As with the the D-7 Battlecruiser, the construction for the effects models for the Class F Shuttles was funded by Aliminum Model Toys, in exchange for the merchandising rights. Lucrative product placement was yet another technology predicted by Star Trek.

comments.body: I don't recall them ever using the landing gear in the show, just setting down on the nacelles.... - Polecat

They're really hard to spot, due to the angles at which the full size model was often shot, as well as the resolution of the television sets the original series was usually viewed on. I didn't notice them myself, (Especially the recessed nacelle struts.) until I looked at the remastered edition of the show. sta.sh/01cro76q9v42
Huh. Well I'll be damned... - Polecat
In the picture with Kirk and Spock, the two of them are about to carry out a bunch of scantily clad hippy chicks. NO ONE who saw that scene was looking fr the landing struts.
Interesting, I knew it had front struts, but I didn't realize they fit into the bottom of the nacelles. (I also thought it was white, not grey. This my alter my plans for a painted microvan.)
*LOL* Point. - Polecat

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.10
description: Today's chibi starship is the Klingon Vor'cha class battlecruiser. Combining the traditional manta-ray silhouette of the Klingon battlecruisers of antiquity, with the tell tale crimson glow of Federation style Bussard Collectors on the leading edge of each engine nacelle, the Vor'cha was a showcase of the technical exchange between to two former rivals in the years following the Khitomer peace accords. Vor'cha's would be a common sight in Alpha Quadrant space from the 2360's to well into the twenty-fifth century, serving with distinction in every conflict from the Klingo-Cardassian Conflict to the Dominion War. That said, I still think the nose looks like someone stuck the Millennium Falcon on the front of the ship.

comments.body: Never let it be said that Trek didn't have a variety of ships. - Polecat

After finally adopting CGI for fleet battle sequences, DS9's production design department comes up with a wealth of new ship designs to pad out the Dominion War era Starfleet. Most of them become the orbiting equivalent of redshirts. sta.sh/0elw9cnul7t
Admittedly, playing "Star Trek Online" has given me access to a LOT of ships that were likely just used in the backgrounds. - Polecat

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.09
description: Today's chibi Star Trek starship picture is of a Romulan Kestrel-class shuttle. The Kestrel is the Romulan Empire's answer to Starfleet's Danube Class Runabout, and boasts similar size, crew capacity, and speed. The Kestrel-class does boast many features not available on its Federation counterpart, such as cloaking technology, and far more luxurious accommodations in its VIP variant. In fact, this variant is the preferred mode of interstellar transit for many Romulan officials, such as members of the esteemed Tal Shiar, and of course, the senate*. * In case anyone doesn't get the reference: www.youtube.com/watch?v=StF9jr…

comments.body: Unfortunately no witty comments from me here. I didn't get to see much of DS9, and maybe all of 2 episodes of Voyager, so I don't know those events very well. So my entire knowledge about these things comes from playing too much STO (Star Trek Online). - Polecat

I highly recommend DS9. It is the best overall of the Star Trek spinoffs, and the only one with an overarching storyline throughout. In what is arguably the best episode of the series, Garak, who is basically alien James Bond, aids the Federation by doing the reprehensible act of assassinating a Romulan official, and implicating another government. His act is horrible, but serves a greater good; showcasing the show's moral complexity.
I watched the clip, yeah. Tho I gotta admit it felt very out of character for Sisqo to me, but I assume I also hadn't seen his evolution through much of the series either. - Polecat

Sisko, by the time depicted here, is a lot more of a commanding presence than he was at the beginning of the series. At the beginning he was calm and diplomatic, perhaps in a attempt to engage viewers, more familiar with Patrick Stewart's calm and urbane portrayal of Picard. Over the years, as war breaks out, we see Sisko become harder edged and more assertive. In continuity, this is a reaction to the pressures of commanding a fleet at war. (With all of the fleet actions her commands, many fans wonder why he was never made an admiral.) Sisko has to shoulder the burdens of sending thousands of men to their deaths for the cause of galactic freedom, not just the occasional handful of security men. Outside of the continuity, I think the producers just decide to "Let Avery Brooks be Avery Brooks" and let the actor tap into the wellspring of badassery that served him so well in the hard boiled detective shows, "Spencer for Hire" and "A Man Called Hawk", perhaps attracting the viewers of those shows as well. The reason Sisko enlists the services of "Space James Bond" here is because Betazed, a charter Federation world, (And home to TNG's Counselor Troi.) had just fallen to the enemy, signalling that the UFP was loosing the conflict. Motivated by this event, Sisko put his most amoral man on the task of securing a new ally, not realizing the depths Garak would plunge to achieve his objective.

yeah, I was in the Navy at the time these were coming out, and seeing TV at all was a spotty proposition. Maybe I'll inherit my mother's Star Trek DVD collection someday (a day I'm in no rush to reach) and binge watch the entire thing. I only saw about the first half of the TNG run, a few episodes of early DS9 (a scattering within the first 2 seasons), and maybe 2 episodes of Voyager's first season. So I'll admit I'm not the Star Trek fan my mother is. - Polecat

Your mother sounds incredibly cool. I should point out that every Star Trek series and spinoff is available to stream on Netflix. You would have to bother with DVD's to watch everything. Also, in the case of both TNG and DS9, both shows really found their footing in their third seasons. You've seen the worst both shows had to offer, so your reticence to continue viewing does not surprise me, but I recommend you do.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.07
description: "From heck's heart, I poke at thee. For hate's sake, I hock my last loogie at theee!" USS Reliant, from The Wrath of Khan.

comments.body: Hehehe. My favorite Starfleet design (and referencing the movie with my favorite variant of the Starfleet Uniforms). Win/win! - Polecat

Yea, those "monster maroons", as the actors called them, were crisp as hell. I can't help but wonder if they were in any way inspired by the landing party jackets from "The Cage"/"the Menagerie". The "Cage" jacket was meant to be worn over the duty shirt, featured a wide collar to reveal the department color collar the aforementioned duty shirt, and had a double breasted fold over Velcro enclosure. These are features the older jackets would share with the "Wrath of Khan" uniforms. sta.sh/01zg99mz2tj2 Too many similarities to be a coincidence, in my opinion. Besides, it would be cool if the looked back to the older design.

Admittedly I remember hearing one of the designers/production guys talking about some of the intent of the design, and they said they took some cues from actual military uniforms for those, drawing from the pea-coat design the Navy uses as the base for the jacket. Which also bears a passing resemblance to the fugly jackets they used in '"the Cage" too, probably where that similarity comes from. Tho I'll admit I think they would have paired well with leather skirts for the ladies and some of the old fashioned knee high boots for one killer uniform (for the ladies). Anyway, yeah. Given my military background, it makes sense I'd gravitate towards the most militaristic looking uniform the Star Trek continuity used, ne? - Polecat

What would be cool is if Star Trek: Discovery, which is set around the Pike era, would have the blueish grey jackets, and cut them with the sleek lines and dress shoulders of the monster maroons. This would spark an instant visual connection between these eras. Won't ever happen, but it would be nice.
They'd have to REALLY clean up that jacket to reach that. That thing fits like a poorly made sweater from my Grandmother's knitting experiments.... - Polecat
Really, I was thinking of them just using the maroon jakets, only in the Pike era blue gray. I can't help bet think that's what they would have gone with if they had more time and money.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.06
description: Today's entry in the Inktober, chibi-fied Star Trek series is a Klingon K'Tinga class battlecruiser.

comments.body: "I'm coming to EAT you!"
I made the torpedo tube bigger than on the D-7 to add to the overall chibi-ness of the image. Yea, it does look like a hungry mouth.
Foreshortening helps.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.04
description: Just sliding in October 4th's Inktober entry under the wire, with half an hour of this day left. Today's picture: Angry Warbird.

comments.body: Ha ha. That birb. Any chance of an SD U.S.S. Benjamin Sisko's Mothafuggin Pimp Hand in the future? Or is it already too kinda SD to be of any real difference?
There is a Very good changes that you'll be seeing it this month.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.03
description: Today's Inktober contribution is a super deformed Klingon D-7 Battlecruiser. A little trivia about the D-7; Star Trek's producers had never intended to build a filming model of a Klingon ship. They had bluffed their way out of showing one for two seasons by having the vessel always be out of visual range, or appear only as a sensor blip. Budget reductions for season three seemed to preclude the design and construction of a new alien vessel. Fortunately, Aluminum Model Toys, who had made millions with their licensed models of the USS Enterprise, and the Galilio Shuttlecraft model kits, stepped in and funded the construction themselves, in exchange for another lucrative model license. Network execs seemed to be oblivious, but the veteran toy company was keenly aware of Star Trek's potential as a merchandising opportunity. I haven't decided if I'm going to continue the theme of drawing SD Star Trek vessels for the entire month. Through my own purchasing of the many successors to AMT's legacy, I have enough references to pull it off, although by Halloween I'll probably be hauling out some obscure ships.

comments.body: The D-7... my favorite of the Klingon designs. ^_^ - Polecat
Easily.
Always loved the D-7, it just has such a retro look to it

"Retro" You mean it looked like something from golden age science fiction like Flash Gordon? Very interesting. The Klingons themselves are very much a throwback to such villains as Ming the Merciless.
Yes, that far back "retro"

Zoom! This one just makes me imagine the sound effects. It's pretty cool, and even cooler to learn that little tidbit about Trek models. That's pretty genius. The underside is a little bare, but I assume that's the design.

Yes. The old models didn't have the intricate texture, or "tech-sture" ("Trek-sture"? ) of the later, motion picture/ TNG era, ones. AMT may have been footing the bill, but they still weren't exactly ILM.

title: Inktober: Stardate 1610.02
description: Created in 2009 by artist Jake Parker, the Inktober Initiative is a challenge intended to promote and develop positive drawing habits in artists, by tasking them to complete one drawing every day during the month of October. I did not complete a drawing yesterday, to my eternal shame :icononionfail:, but since I've been running my mouth about the initiative, I thought it would be better to start late than never. I'm starting simple, a quick picture of a super-deformed USS Enterprise-A. It may interest folks to that the windows near the belly of the ship, which are visible in every "pre-jumping-into-warp" money shot, are view ports for the ships botanical garden. (According to Mr. Scott's Guide to the Enterprise, anyway.) This section of the vessel is also just aft of the matter/antimatter containment tanks. So in the instance of a magnetic containment failure, the first crewmen to be VAPORIZED will be anyone taking a stroll in the arboretum. Serves them right.

comments.body: It's a neat idea to keep artists drawing, and I'm obviously elated to see you giving it a shot. And while we will surely log your failure forever in the annals of the internet... this one is still real cool. The Enterprise is such an iconic ship, having osmosed from a single TV show to popular culture and general consciousness. Everyone in my family can identify it as "the one spaceship" even if they can't quite note that it's the best damn ship in Starfleet. Very cool.
"And while we will surely log your failure forever in the annals of the internet... this one is still real cool." Uh...thanks?
Haha, just a joke about you missing a day. I think that this whole series more than makes up for that tiny omission.

title: Car and Driver
description: I've had this picture sitting in my deviant stash for a good long while now, and figure it's high time I posted it.

comments.body: Some fine arting there, John.
Thanks, Joe!!!
I know this is a silly question, but why does a parking patrol officer need a riot shotgun? (Also, clearly Natsumi and Miyuki approved.)
Well, actually it's a Thug Zapper, a sort of wireless taser, that fires focused pulses of ball lightning. Still, your question is a valid one.
I am in awe.
Maybe from a different department as America Jones ?
From the same department, actually. :thumb108199524:

title: Snow Fall
description: Okay, back to cute devil girls, in the unspeakable act of making snow angels. Normally, I like to work on pages in order, but in this case I decided to go ahead and work on the subsequent pages since I had problems composing Diva's falling sequence. It was a major help, when a friend of mine recommended that I simply flip over my initial composition. (That's what I get for reading too much manga, I suppose. ) I had fun with the snowman. Rather than settling for the obligatory top hat and coal eyes approach, (What kid, post 1918, has access to coal anyway?) I decided to use carrots and apples to give the snow golem features more appropriate for this unique community.

comments.body: The Snow Golem will run off with Diva's mom. XD
I'm sure Super Mode Diva will have something to say about that.
HAHA wow you just did this now? Man thats fast!

title: From Out of the Sun
description: Just about finished with the Big Planet Comics exclusive variant cover for Princeless: Pirate Princess #1. I made Sparky's wings lighter, in a vain attempt at translucence in order to bring out Bedeli, whose outfit I discovered to my consternation pretty much blends in camoflage-like to the dragons coloring. Anyway, I hope folks enjoy it.

comments.body: "It's a big planet. Somebody's got to save it."
As opposed to Tron, where saving the world involves PKZip and a whole lotta floppies.
Epic looking.
Thanks!

title: Princeless Progress
description: Now all the girls. (Yes, that's a girl dragon.) are colored in. Still need to wok on tones and highlights, while my obsolescent computer protests with every step. Going to have to buy a new one soon.

comments.body: It looks very bright and cheerful and calling out to adventure. Dropping in from above is a de rigeur pose these days.

I have seen it quite a bit since I laid this out. Of course I'm probably prone to notice it since I am working on this. Still, I did at least 30 sketches before I arrived at this composition, so I'm satisfied I didn't simply phone it in and go with the fashionable trend.
Fashionable and popular sometimes trend because they are good, something the hipsters sometimes forget.

title: PrinclessThree-Color01
description: Now starting the colors for the Big Planet Comics exclusive variant cover for Princeless: The Pirate Princess #1, shipping on January 25, 2015. Of course Adrienne Ashe gets colored in first. She is the star after all. Supporting cast has to wait their turn.

comments.body: For all her armor's flashiness, I hope they didn't skimp on the practical application (read: it really is armor) part!
Somebody needs to read the first volume. They go into great detail on how Adrienne's armor is far more useful than that of other fantasy heroines. But then, it would almost have to be.
Speaking of practical application, another artist has weighed in separately on the idea of practical armor. zeonista.deviantart.com/art/Po...
Adrienne really is a knight in shining armor!! If that white color is not enamel it is a mirror finish; mad stylin' with the brass work.
I didn't fully understand the term "white armor" until I actually saw a full plate armor with a full polish. When sunlight hit at the right angle the plates had a brilliant white sheen.

title: Dragonatomy
description: Pencils for Sparky the Dragon, faithful aerial steed of Princess Adrienne Ashe. There was quite a lot to puzzle out about drawing Sparky. From her powerful upper body, to her incongruous kangaroo legs, to her horse-like snout, and her freaky bat-wings, ol' Sparks was a quite a mythological conundrum. So much so, I opted to work out her bizarre "dragonatomy", on a separate piece of paper, later to be reunited with the Princesses Three through the miracle of Photoshop. I am forced to admit, drawing helicopters is a breeze by comparison. On a profoundly trivial note, nitpicking keen eyes will notice that I drew English saddles hitched around Sparky's midriff, as opposed to the Western-style saddles, she is usually drawn wearing. I know it's a fantasy kingdom and all, but I just don't see medieval-esque fairy tale knights making use of American saddles.

comments.body: To be honest, seatbelts would probably make more sense than saddlehorns anyways.

Flying steeds never have seat belts. From Appa in Avatar: The Last Air Bender, to the flying dog-thing in Never Ending Story, to Aladdin's magic carpet, to the bridge of the USS Enterprise, no fantasy hero ever seems to place any value in passive restraint.
So, yeah, English saddlery makes perfect sense. lol

title: Pirate Princeless
description: I'm taking a brief interlude from the wintery mother daughter shenanigans of Evil Diva to create a variant cover for the first issue of the third volume of the highly popular fantasy series, Princeless: The Pirate Princess. The #1 one issue will ship January 28th, and this variant will be a exclusive for Big Planet Comics. Featured here are the heroine, Adrienne Ashe, the sword wielding woman warrior, who chooses to be a knight rather than waiting for one to rescue her, Bedelia Smith, a freakishly tall (For a dwarf.) dwarven blacksmith whom, after forging Adrienne's unprecedented suit of non midriff showing armor, was inspired to join the young knight's quest, and Raven Xingtao the latest addition to their band. Above is a rudimentary sketch of what will become Adrienne's faithful steed, Sparky the dragon--promise.

comments.body: I love the silly-cartoonish look your pre-design sketch has for the dragon. LOL. As for the girls, look pretty good, tho that pose means that non-midriff baring top is baring her midriff. - Polecat

I suppose it's hard to see without color, but actually, no part of her body is left exposed by this design. 40.media.tumblr.com/79c85cd44c... She even has a helmet, which I omitted to better show her face.
Isn't that design even more sexist? She looks like she's wearing a steel corset.... - Polecat
It's a reinforced leather sash. Check the diagram. Even if it were a corset, that would still provide coverage and some degree of protection, more so than a bare middy, anyway.
That would depend on if you account for the reduction in mobility a corset would cause... That said, I'm joking as well. It was that particular artist's style, it's just how it struck me. - Polecat

I'd read that comic, and so would my niece. (So would my nephew, he's not afraid of cooties.) Action girls rule. Cheesecake armor has been over-done, as a RPG gamer and Rennie I appreciate you sending Adrienne out to play in full safety gear.
www.amazon.com/Princeless-Vol-... You really should check it out. I think you and your niece will get a big kick out of it.
Thanks

title: Caught with Ink
description: Partial tone guide, over inks for Snow Devil comic. Colors coming soon.

comments.body: I was gonna mention that there should be snow in the middle of the legs, but it appears you caught it.
Verily! Know that nary a mote of minutiae escapes the alert attentions of the mighty Mecha Master!!! But seriously, thanks for looking out.

title: Color Notes
description: Inks of the previous page, or rather a copy of said inks that I've randomly scribbled all over, as a guide to imminent coloring efforts. Perhaps one day I will be able to simply speak into the air, "Computer, color one page", and go back to vegging out in front of the American Heroes Channel* secure in the knowledge that the job will be done amidst an ecstasy of electronic beeps. Until then, I have to figure things out the old fashioned way. The coloring computer would probably mess it up anyway, as it was probably spending too much processing power on plotting the overthrow of humanity.

comments.body: Oh my goodness. Now this is absolutely sublime. That contrast upon seeing Ms. Beelze is so to die for. I love it.
Thank you very much for the kind words.

title: Devil Inks
description: Inks of the "Not angry, but deeply disappointed" Mrs. Endira Maria Beelze. Name provided by wise and gracious publisher.

comments.body: "That's Ms. Beelze to you, bub."
XD
Now that's pretty awesome, love the outfit
Thanks! It was a kit-bash of varying Google Images web search results. I'm glad, and more than a little relieved, that folks are responding to it positively.
You can almost hear her going "Whatever am I going to do with you, child?" - Polecat

Curiously enough, she was initially supposed off-panel. Diva was just supposed to be looking guilty as she hears Endira calling her. I figured it would make for clearer story telling if I included her in the panel. That, and I was taken by the MILF'y potential of Endira's jaded socialite character, and was moved to invent and excuse to draw her. Sadly, the publisher turned down my, "Kidnapped by snow golems" idea.
" Sadly, the publisher turned down my, "Kidnapped by snow golems" idea. " Wait what? XD !?!?!
I was just joking of course. But then I figured, what the heck.
XD hahah

I can certainly agree with that. Not only is that a fantastic outfit there, but she really has the look that she's smokin' hot no matter what she wears (regardless of how little or how much she has on). - Polecat

title: Caught White Handed
description: More drawings of Diva Beelze, and her disapproving mother, er--"Mom" Beelze. (Haven't been able to find out her first name. Does she have one. ) More proof to my theory that whatever you are, when your children hit adolescence, they'll rebel against it.

comments.body: Absolutely beautiful. Can't wait to see the finished piece.
I have no idea who these characters are, but it makes me laugh anyway. Rebellious demons always pretending to be innocent and pure. When you grow up, then what'll you do? - Polecat
Well Diva isn't pretending. Its her nature.

Well, the girl is Diva Beelze who is a demon with the hear and disposition of an angel. Endira is her long sufferingly, surprising hawt, corporate attorney* mother, who wonders just where she went wrong. Keep you eye on this gallery, you'll definitely see more on them over the next few months. *I was going to say demon corporate attorney, but there was no sense in being redundant.
Sigh Diva Beelze.. Whatever are we going to do with you. :3 BTW Mom's name is Endira Maria Beelze

Thanks for the info. In the script, and on her character entry on the comic strips web site, she is only identified as "Mom". I was beginning to think that was an intentional thing, like with how Penny from Big Bang Theory has no last name.
Well we only find out her real name when Diva accidently breaks into her moms desk and finds her Diary of her time as a Teen ANGEL! =O DUN DUN DUNNNNN!

title: Devil
description: Inks and flat colors on disapproving devil lady panel. Image inverted for your convenience.

title: Caught in the Act
description: In case you were wondering what the previous sketches were for, here is the final penciled page containing them. Much to her profound disappointment, Mother Beelze discovers her daughter's shameful habit.

comments.body: "I will raise an ARMY of snow angels to defeat you!"
I see nothing wrong with playing in snow.. I do miss snow sometimes.. making snow angels and snow forts and the list shall go on.

For you and me, there's nothing wrong with making snow angels, but for a little devil girl, it's sort of taboo. Snow forts would probably be acceptable; along with arsenals of snow balls with rocks inside them.
Making Snow Angels is all sorts of fun.. Even Little Devils love it, just unfortunately their moms aren't so pleased... XD
adorable!!!!
Thanks.

title: Snow Devil Sketch
description: Another panel sketch. As I post this I a reminded that folks keep telling me i won't be long before it starts snowing again. Ugh.

title: Devil Woman
description: Mrs. Beelze from the Evil Diva comic. You can just tell she's evil, right? Its because she's a lawyer. Sadly much of this image will be cropped off in the final piece, but I had fun penciling it nonetheless.

title: Snow Devil Close-Up
description: Pencils for a project I am working on for Angry Viking Press. This is a head shot of Diva Beelze the titular character in the comic Evil Diva. Hope you like her, you'll see more of her soon.

comments.body: Very cool! Is she going in for a hug? I'm not familiar with the webcomic, but I gave it a look-over; your rendition is an extremely cool take on her design. I hope to read more of Evil Diva and see some more of her in your style soon!
Nope, not going for a hug. I'll keep the mystery alive for now.
And with your latest pieces, the mystery is revealed.
That's her look before a large snowball buries her face!
That would kind of rock actually.

title: Chiroines 08: Chikasa
description: My last chibified offering for Anime USA, and this one's an actual heroine for a change, she of the awesome scarf: Mikasa Ackerman from Attack on Titan.

comments.body: Break away utility blade swords?
Yes, they actually have them in the anime. Actually, AoT isn't the first anime to feature break away utility knives in combat. Remember the prgressive knives, used in Neon Genesis Evangelion?
I couldn't make it very far into NGE... It's a terrible show with terrible characters and no development. But now that you mention it, I think I remember something like that on the red Eva.

title: Chiroines 07: ChibiQuinn
description: When I scanned this one, I couldn't help but slather on some rudimentary color. BTW, to me this is the only Harley costume I recognize. While normally I support any and all acts of pandering and fan service, I always felt the costume designs for Dr. Quinzel, featured in the Arkham series of games were just unnecessary and desperate. Seriously, aren't red and black latex tights sexy enough?

title: Chiroines 06: ChiBaroness
description: My day's visit to the artist alley at this year's Anime USA afforded me an opportunity to add to my long promised, but oft-neglected series of chibi-fied heroine drawings. Today's picture features Baroness Anastasia De Cobray from GI Joe, a character which I've been meaning to draw for the longest time. I know it isn't perfect, but I'm proud how it came out, particularly the HISS tank, which I drew from memory. Of course that probably really only means I've been watching waaaay to much GI Joe, but then you probably already knew that.

title: Pin-Emp 2.0
description: Thought I'd try a another drawing of Empowered, (The titular* heroine of Empowered Special #4: Animal Style--coming to a comic store near you this coming June 5th!) carrying that darned dragster engine. This time I thought I'd forgo the gratuitous rear shot of my previous attempt. At the very least, our heroine would probably appreciate it. *Hee-hee. I said "Titular".

comments.body: that's cool
"Hey Guys Look what I found!!! I goes Vroom Vroom!... but not any more.. "
"That's odd. It was "vrooming" like crazy when it was in that shiny wheeled thing where I found it."

Enjoyed seeing this, John...great work! No worries about the pitch...know that you've sold at least two! I pre-ordered a couple weeks ago to show my support for another child of the 80's who grew up on arena rock, anime on TV after school (Robotech Macross, Voltron, Battle of the Planets) and GI Joe. Happy to support your work because I so appreciate your El Hazard, Bubble Gum Crisis and Ninja High School pieces here. I go back a few years in the fandom myself, as I gladly bought Warren's earlier work; I have copies of Dirty Pair: Dangerous Acquaintances and BGC: Grand Mal. These days I have about as much hair left on my head as Destro! Heh. I'm sure Asrial from NHS could find a chassis in short order that power plant would look right at home in. Engines I work on don't have as much horsepower but a LOT more torque, with cylinders I can fit my whole head into! MJE

Thanks. I'm glad that there is still someone out there who appreciates the apex of anime as an art form. When I first posted on DA, I got a lot of compliments for being "retro", which prompted me to check out a lot of the new offerings in anime and animation in general, in the hopes of updating my "look". (Of course DVD and the internet make such research far easier than back in the days of shady, back room, 4th generation VHS tapes. ) While technically more advanced, I find myself not as inspired by a lot of modern anime, with its never ending glut of emo boys, loli girls, excorcisms, zombies, and infinitely repeating ki-battles , complete with color commentary. "Oh no Obinowashi has just struck Toji with his spiraling fist! No one can withstand that much ki at one time." Worry not, Lilli! Toji will prevail. He has yet to show his TRUE POWER!" I miss the bold decisive characters. I miss the guitar riffs, and I miss the all important robots and mecha that look like they could actually be built. I honestly don't know if I'll find a modern audience, but as I work to get published more and more, I hope to do stories reminiscent of the things that inspired me so many years ago. And I'll bet your Destro look kicks ass.

Certainly know where you're coming from, John. My first experience with Bubble Gum Crisis was on a fifth-generation fansub with about as much snow on the tape as a look outside my dorm window on a winter's day (and I went to college in Michigan's Upper Peninsula!) circa NINETEEN EIGHTY NINE. (Yes, I did like BGC 2040 but I'm talking about the original here from 1987!) AND WE LIKED IT! Kids these days don't know how good they've got it. And one of my recent favorites that harkens back a lot to all of the old school virtues you extoll here is Gurren Lagann. It's what got me back on the wagon, in fact. And in my nearly thirty years in the fandom, it's the one that got me hands-down the most emotionally worked up. It is that good! Very powerful piece that I strongly recommend with a surprising relevant message during these trying times. MJE
When you ink, boy do you ink.
I'm hoping that's a good thing.
When have I not sung your praises about the subtle levels of detail in your work? You wound me, good sir! I demand satisfaction! Pistols at dawn!
"Subtle?" Hey now, there's no call for harsh language here.
Awesome work!
Thanks!
My pleasure!
I got this from you while at College Park on FCBD, it was great meeting you! -Gary

Hey Gary! Thanks for dropping by on FCBD and double thanks for checking out my site and leaving a comment. If you see anything in my gallery you have a question about, or just want to leave a general comment please feel free.

title: Post Apocalyptic Homeys
description: One more element in my ongoing campaign of floggery for the upcoming Empowered Special #4: Animal Style coming this June 5th. This is a group shot of a Mad Max-esque, alternate-universe band of Super Homeys, hinted at earlier by the existence of Auntie Spooky, and the Homie Hauler. Since I was already pulling the mad Max riff with Spooky, Adam decided it would be fun to have the rest of the Homey's be all Mad Maxified, so I had all kinds of fun making Mind**** into the Warrior Woman, Major Havoc into the Feral Kid, and Captain Rivet into Lord Humongous. Actally, Adam left it to me between which male Homey would be which MM character, so I basically had to decide who would look funnier in a hockey mask. (I actually did a sketch of Rivet in an feral wig. I'll have to see if I can track that down. )

comments.body: Very cool. Wonderful grouping!
Thanks. I appreciate the kind words.
that's cool
Thanks. Always good to hear from you, dewd.
Just finished reading Animal Style and I love Lord Rivet's mask. That's just beautiful.
Yea, I was debating over who, between him and Havoc would be the Humongous analog, but the idea of Rivet with the mask was just soooo much funiier.
dang i went to the book store and forgot the title. The funny thing is i did not look for your name. argggg.....im totally getting this tomorrow
Remember, Empowered: Animal Style. And please, let me know what you think of it.
they didnt have it in stock...... i will try to get it thought.

title: Pin-Emp
description: Empowered in a pin-up-y show of strength, as she prances around with a 8,000 hp top fuel dragster engine. I just started this out as a sketch in preparation for drawing Adam Warren's titillating titular character, for my work in the Empowered Special #4:Animal Style, coming to a comic store near you this coming June 5th. (Gotta get that floggery in, folks. ) In part, this drawing was inspired by an old Six Million Dollar Man action figure, I had as a kid. To demonstrate bionic strength, the figure came with a scale engine block, for it to lift when you pressed the orange button on its back. I resisted the urge to put Emp in a red track suit.

comments.body: It's nice to see a happy Emp. At least no one has asked her to be a car model...no doubt she wouldn't feel all smiley having to pose on a hood of some guy's ride.

I was thinking about doing something like that, with Emp dressed as a racing queen with an umbrella. I can't decide whether to put her in a short little racing queen outfit with her mask so folks will recognize her, or just her costume.

You could do both. Do one of Emp as the college aged woman earning extra money modeling that has her on the hood of a "real" looking car. She would be undercover of course. Then do a mirror image one of her as Emp on a slightly more "heroic" and unrealistic car.
Hmmm, I am actually considering drawing her in her college age hoodie. [link]
Aww, sweet! Superchargers make everything better. Also, congrats on landing the gig!
Thanks!
Oh God, I remember that toy. You could see through its eye.
Indeed. In retrospect, I'm surprised that 8 year old me didn't find that toy more creepy, with that hole in its head.
Partially colored my friend. Wife in hospital again and hopefully we get answers and a solution.
Talk about your good news and bad news. How is your wife doing? I hope it's nothing life threatening.
it looks like she's wearing her costume from when she had that job cosplaying as herself...
Yea, I can totally see that. This is one of the first drawings I ever did of Emp; long before I got the hang of the whole wrinkle-free aspect of the outfit.
Awesome inkwork
Thanks!

title: Animal Style Designs 05: Maul Bunny
description: More sketches and floggery to promote Empowered Special #4: Animal Style, which will be in your local comic book store this coming June 5th. (Order it avariciously!!) Since it's Easter, I thought it would be fun to post the Animal Style gang's command-and-control Landmate1, the festively designed Maul Bunny. Essentially, a titanium bathysphere, mounted with powerful hydraulic legs and armored manipulators, the Maul Bunny does not pack the offensive punch of other Animal Style mecha2. It is designed primarily move rapidly around a battle space, observe positions and movements of all combatants, and relay such data to the more heavily armed mecha of its squadron. It carries out this function with a prodigious COTTONTAIL3 sensor array mounted on the aft of its bathysphere module. The COTTONTAIL can track and up to 1,000 separate targets, and lock onto them, allowing every friendly mecha in a battle space to address enemies outside of their individual detection spheres. Although the Maul Bunny features twelve retracting tungsten carbide blades, housed in its manipulator "paws", it possesses no other ranged or offensive weapons. It's the machine's ability to maximize the combat efficiency of entire groups of combat mecha, that makes it stand out--or "hop" out, as the case my be, 1.) Landmate: Big honkin' robot, driven by a human operator, usually seated in the torso. Featured prominently in the manga, Appleseed, by the divine Masamune Shirow. 2.)Mecha: Generic term for any internally piloted, anthropomorphic machine--of which I am the master. (Or so it says in the ad copy. ) 3.) COTTONTAIL: COordination, Target Telemetry and Overview Nodule, for TActical Information and Logistics.

comments.body: Looks Big, Emp will need a realy big monty python holy hand grenade!
Too bad there's no room for one in her suit.
It just got the reference! King Arthur's knights used it to vanquish the rabbit with the "really pointy teeth!"
i dont think there is a copyright for that weapon, you can use it XD
But does he scoop up field mice and bop 'em on the head?

No, he feeds their coordinates to a nearby Terror-Pin unit, who then showers them with Shellfire mortars. The mortars airburst over the targets, releasing hundreds of anti-personnal bomblets--and it's those that bop the field MICE1 on the head. 1.)MICE Mecha Intended for Close quarter Engagement.

title: Animal Style Designs 04: Zodiac One--Point One
description: More floggery1 for Empowered: Animal Style. This is the first of many, many, many designs for the larcenous Animal Style gang's transport/car carrier/getaway vehicle--Zodiac One. It was quite a challenge, coming up with a vehicle that could perform all there of these tasks. In this initial iteration, I had intended to design something that could carry all of the Hard Suits2 and Land Mates3 internally. Unfortunately, I discovered there would be little room on-panel for the stolen cars, which were sort of the point of the story, with the size of this particular beast, so other options had to be pursued. With this design, I tried in incorporate design elements from the sporty, 1980's Steinwinter truck concept of The Highwayman4 fame. I also tried to give it sort of a croccidile/dragon motif on it's forward section. Since it didn't make into AS, I'll probably use this design somewhere else. Something tells me it will look really spiffy in green. 1.) Floggery: "To advertise and promote a product or service on personal websites or blogs." Term is actually a portmanteau, combining the words Flash: "to display obtrusively and ostentatiously." and Blog: "a Web site that contains an online personal journal with reflections, comments, and often hyperlinks provided by the writer." Still weird to say, "I'm flogging myself." and have that be a good thing. 2.) Hard Suit: Roughly man-sized powered armor that augments the strength of the wearer. 3.)Landmate: Big Honkin' robot driven by a human operator, usually riding in the torso. 4.) The Highwayman 1980's action show, in which a poor man's Mad Max rides the post apocalyptic road ways dispensing justice in the worlds most badass truck. Mad Max meets Knight Rider

comments.body: And The Highwayman had a really badass gun, too. I mean, exploding bullets are old hat these days, but back then, in my early teens mindset, I was "A gun that fires exploding bullets? COOL!" (The truck cab also turned into a helicopter, but we try not to talk about that....) I was wondering why the front of Zodiac One looked familiar! I had thought it was because of the resemblance to Damnation Alley' Landmasters, but only because I'd forgotten about The Highwayman until now!

Actually, I would use the Landmaster as a basis for the Homey Hauler, the ride of the alternate post apocalyptic version of the Super Homey's. That, and a Striker APC. And I remember the 20mm handgun. Actually, your observation on the foresight on that gun reminds me of an episode of Get Smart, in which Agent 86 and a Kaos agent are meeting for a temporary truce. To demonstrate their good will, the two spies discard their weapons, which are revealed to be machine pistols. At the time, the notion of a concealable, fully automatic weapon was so absurd as to spark a giggle from the laugh track. Twenty years later they were relatively commonplace. Amazing.

title: Animal Style Designs 03: Monkey See, Monkey Doom
description: Another villainous mech design I did for the upcoming Empowered: Animal Style one-shot, due out on June 5th. This is the Brass Monkey, one of two Hardsuits1, fielded by the larcenous Animal Style gang. This simian designed suit is designed for close combat behind enemy lines. As such, it lighter, faster, and far more nimble than the ponderous Terror-Pin, although nowhere near as thoroughly armored. Just for giggles, I decided to pay homage to an old school friend of mine by using a familiar logo as the suit's roundel. 1.)Hard Suit: Roughly man-sized powered armor that augments the strength of the wearer. Term originally used to describe Knight Saber armors, in the seminal anime, Bubblegum Crisis.

comments.body: Love the Frank Cho monkey you threw in the body. That's great.
Thanks a lot man. I'm glad somebody finally noticed that.

title: Animal Style Designs 02: Fear the Mecha!
description: Another villainous mecha design for the Empowered: Animal Style one-shot for which I performed the guest artist duties. This mecha is, Terror-Pin a heavy, Landmate1 style mecha which carries a sizable array of Shellfire2 mortars in its rear "shell" assembly. This mecha is designed primarily to serve as artillery back-up to the close-quarter combat Hard Suits3, by showering targets with indirect fire from a stand-off position. (Really, there was no actual need to determine how a giant turtle-shaped robot would be fielded in a combat setting. I just like coming up with that sort of stuff. ) When coming up with the line-up for the mechanized car thief gang, Adam wavered momentarily on whether or not to include ol' Terror-Pin, but I campaigned passionately for it, myself being a refugee of the University of Maryland, whose mascot is of course the Terrapin. U of M Representin'! Also, this gives me an opportunity to make a more appropriately fearful version of the aforementioned mascot than was required by my prior commission on the subject. *1.)Landmate: Big Honkin' robot driven by a human operator, usually riding in the torso. Term coined by Masamune Shirow. 2.)Shellfire: Actually an alternate name for Terror-Pin itself, coined by the inimitable Mr. Warren. Much better than my own alternate idea of "Die-Mondback". 3.)Hard Suit: Roughly man-sized powered armor that augments the strength of the wearer. (Think Iron Man.)

comments.body: i can definitely see april o neal in this somewhere. teenage robo ninja
If I was doing TMNT, they would definitely have a mecha!!!

Hrm, I wonder, it looks like it might be able to transform, or least clench up or turn into something, least I think that is what it might be able to do. Other then that, I think it might be interesting to see further designs of these things. I might be taking a class on Character design and drawing, whom knows I might start to draw again.

Sorry, it can't transform. It's interesting, I've often been asked if jets I've drawn have "Battloid Modes", and now I'm asking if an actual robot has a vehicle mode. I suppose this is why the Transformers franchise continues to endure.
Transformers bah. Robotech / Macross. That is where I see this from. Though I love the designs of the turtle, monkey, and I see where it comes from kinda neat.

What I meant is that Transformers still has animated series, toy lines and major motion pictures made in its honor. It remains an active and vital franchise, whereas all we Robotech fans have is the 2007 DVD release of The Shadow Chronicles which was well enough animated, but so poorly written and voice acted as to be physically painful to watch. Somebody need to kidnap Andrea Romano, the voice director for Avatar: The Last Air Bender, Batman: The Animated Series, Justice league, Justice League Unimited, Motor City, and Teen Titans. Imagine what she could do with Robotech.

title: Animal Style Designs 01: Paladindrome
description: Thought I'd start posting some of the various and sundry designs and sketches I generated while working on the Empowered: Animal Style one shot, which is due to ship on June 5th. Above is a design for the Purple Paladin's Paladindrome, the sweet sweet ride of a "knight-in-shining-armor" themed emp-verse super-hero. At least I think he's "knight-in-shining-armor" themed, since I don't believe he's been depicted in Empowered canon as yet, but in any case, this gave me the freedom to perform such visual experiments as using the Dark Horse logo as the vehicle's badge. I also thought it would be fun to use the signature crusader knight's cruciform as a theme for the machine, so I decided to give it entirely too many engines, all arranged in a cross configuration--and cross hubcaps. BTW, I strongly believe that every super-hero, everywhere should have some kind of souped-up automobile. (If anything it would add one more marketable accessory to marketers looking to franchise the characters.) I don't care if the hero's own power-set provides him with an alternate means of transit. eventually, everyone loses their powers, even it is that once in a blue moon, temporary instance in which said hero has to prove how tough/brave they are without the advantage of their powers and abilities.
