Would be nice to have a ride home after the bare-knuckled duel of honor. Think of it...the Emp-mobile...hmmmmm.

comments.body: Yeah, but the Emp-mobile's body would strip right off the frame, the moment it got a scrape. Though it would give entirely new perspective on the term "fender-bender."
And it would probably end up always getting booted, towed away, and impounded.

title: Chi-Roines 06
description: Well it's the start of the new year and I, like you, have made an innumerable host of hideously doomed resolutions. Chief among mine are to post more pictures in this long neglected gallery, and to finish my even longer neglected series of chibified fictional heroine drawings. So, kicking off a little rust, here is the sixth picture in the series. Chibi-fied Viper Girl. Initially I was going to make this Sheba, but I totally got the hair wrong, and in my haste, I drew a Galactia bird on her helmet instead of a Pegasus--er--pegasus. So I guess this will be an anonymous female Viper pilot for the present. The composition of a female pilot beside her fighter as the Cylons bear down on her is a shaky homage to a publicity painting done by Frank Frazetta for the episode, Lost Planet of the Gods. Between you, me and the keyboard, I was always a little bothered by how far the master of fantasy art went off-model with the mecha; but I suppose given my screw-up with the hair, I'm not one to talk.

comments.body: That is adorable! As always, I just love you technically drawing skills. So clean and crisp
Thanks, good to hear from you dude.
wooo new art! Glad to see you working again.
Oh, I'm always working. Posting however, in another matter entirely, but I'll see what I can do about that.
cool Hope to see more (And really want to see DMV! XD)
Oh boy , look at the chibi Cylon , with those angry eyes , ha ha . This is a reat art , well done . and I remember very well that frazetta art .
Thanks. Yea, the angry eyes just felt right.
Ops... , What I wanted to say was "GREAT" art. Now , lets see this art in Colors!
Happy New Year, John! Hope to see more from you.
Thanks, I hope to see more from me too.
excellent work. I do love the composition.
Thanks.
This is too dam cute. Make sme wanna see a chibi Cylon (old style) to go with that adorable Cylon ship.... - Polecat
Of course old style. I still think that stuff's cool. RDM's Galactica was cool starting out, but it really went off the rails. It ended up like Lost, only with spaceships.
Admittedly I never even saw much of the BSG reboot Syfy did, so I'm really only familiar with the old stuff. - Polecat

At first the reboot was impeccable. The atmosphere really communicated a palpable sense of loss, as humanity came to terms with the end of civilization. They were very good about showing the unthinkable choices and sacrifices that had to be made in a survival situation. They paid attention to critical details. Injuries suffered in one episode kept people out of the action in later stories, things that got broken stayed that way and thought was given to situations and tactics. For a show based on a spaceship, it was very grounded. But over time, the show would be hijacked by plot devices and increasing nonsensical metaphysical hogwash. By the end, it was a sad jumble of deus ex machinas, fan pandering, and pretension. It had utterly lost the verisimilitude that marked its first season, and had become a shadow of its former glory. I credit classic BSG for never pretending it was anything other than a highly entertaining mash-up of Bonanza, Midway and The Ten Commandments--a literal wagon train to the stars.

Cool chibi...SD BSG styling! Reminds of that great classic Walt Simonson Marvel Comics BSG #5 "Ambush" cover! Man, this really brings me back to when my parents got me that actual issue as a kid when it came out!

Heh, I have every issue of the Marvel Comics run. Walt Simonson stood out to me because he was the only artist who drew Vipers on model. And I remember that cover. I believe the issue debuted a new class of Cylon.

Simonsom rocks! That's cool you have the entire Marvel BSG run. Yeah, it got a little too weird for me after the TV adaptation run...just like the Star Wars stuff, heh! I lost the original issue ages ago but managed to buy a copy in much better condition couple years ago on a nostalgia run.
Very nice job here. Would you mind to much if I tried to color this piece?
Go for it.
Alright. Thank you so much.
Cool; reminds me of "Scramble"by Frank frazzetta
Exactly. That's the publicity piece I was referring to in my artist's comments. (Note the link. )
Good to see you're still producing; Gives me a little encouragement.

title: Paper Terp
description: Paper doll of Terry Terrapin. The publisher of the coloring book didn't go for this idea, so I figured I may as well get a DA posting out of it. Enjoy folks.

title: Terp Twins
description: Meet Tom and Terry Terrapin. This is a cover I did for a coloring book promoting businesses in the area of College Park, Maryland. Being near the university, I figured that the characters in the book should be terps. Enjoy the cuteness.

comments.body: Very cute, good to see this up here
Thanks. I'm think about coloring some of the other images to post here as well.
No probs mate. Aha well cool - it'd be good to see them here.
Marvelous! And very Extreeeeme!

Thank you, but I don't know if I'd really categorize it as extreme. But you've got me thinking, maybe I could put modular weapons and armor on my next paper doll, like the old Centurions cartoon. "POWER EXTREEEMME!!" Another cool cartoon opening. [link] Unrelated to the previous topic, but I still love the cheesey hair-metal guitar riffs in this one. They should totally update this like they're doing Thundercats and Voltron. Zombies are in noadays.
Very extreme. I like it a lot. Well, you know, young kids, BMX bikes, EXTREME jump rope... That sort of thing. Never saw the cartoon - but I am always interesting to see re imaginations.

title: Steampunk Airship
description: My attempt at a steampunk aerial pirate ship for an online comic project, conceived by the feverish imagination of KrazyKrow. I confess that this design was a bit of a challenge for me, as I'm not the biggest fan of Victorian-era aesthetics. (The iconic firearms are sufficiently eye-catching, but I could never warm up to the cat-house filigree that creeps upon every background element like gold inlaid fungus. ) To keep things interesting, I went for a sleeker looking profile, and incorporated a steam-based reaction control system with "Harrier-jet style" thrust vectors. The strip never materialized unfortunately, but upon reflection, I feel it was a good experience, dabbling outside my technological comfort zone.

comments.body: Say, wasn't the Victorian era also responsible for Alphonse Mucha? (*Don't* correct me on this, please. ) That Jolly Roger variant makes me think this is some unusual combination of One Piece and Space Pirate Captain Harlock, perhaps with some Pirates of the Caribbean thrown in for good measure. Always a treat to see more stuff from you. Great, now I'm imagining a WWI version of Ace Combat, what with your infusion of "Harrier-jet style" thrust vectors to the steampunk genre. How's the Chinese New Year treating you so far, John?
Hmmm, I keep forgetting about Mucha. In fact I'm overdue to draw a Mucha influenced pin-up. All artist have to do one at some point. [link] [link] [link] [link] I think it's a rule somewhere.
I always thought of the old airships as being quite romantic I think your design came out rather nice.

Well airships and Zeppelins do have their appeal. There is something about a huge vessel that hangs in mid air, unbeholden to the influence of gravity. Probably why I like the SHIELD Helicarrier so very much. Now that they have movie's with live action versions of Nick Fury, Iron Man, Thor, Captain America who ride around in said Helicarrier, maybe they'll have a live action version of it as well.

I'm not that familiar with the Helicarrier...but doesn't stay up in the air by mechanical means? Meaning, if the power turns off it would slam into the city below? Or does it have something that would keep it afloat?

Yes, as the name suggests, it is held aloft by rotor blades, and a power outage would send it into the ground below (Which has happened OFTEN in the comics. In many ways it is the Lt. Worf of fictional vehicles. ) But when the power is on and everything is working right, it hangs in the air, cutting a large and imposing silhouette--like a Zeppelin. A possible design for the one to appear in this summers Thor movie. Now I have to see it.
Dang..that thing is huge! And I mean the ship
Steam vectors! White misty VTOLs in your face!
Why does that sound dirty.
Replace "misty" with milky and voila! Innuendo!

You must re-watch Wild Wild West (Will Smith). People generally hate that movie but I loved it. giant mechaspider with a hot latina strapped inside it, and a black cowboy with his professor sidekick giving chase on horses!

Well I've never heard a remotely positive review for the movie before. This is a new experience. This reminds me of my experience with the Speed Racer movie. It was an unqualified flop at the box office, and most reviewers are united in their vituperation, but I alone enjoyed it. The whole father son dynamic between Pops, Rex and Speed reminded me of the relationship between my brothers, my father and myself. It's no surprise to be that two siblings wrote the screenplay--it all just rang very true. But I seem to be the only one to see this merit in the film.
You're not alone. I love it too. But PLEASE DO NOT WATCH THE NEW CARTOON SERIES. It's made of Flash and FAIL. Ugh. (but the villain girl racer is cute tho)
Are you talking about that computer animated travesty? Wretched.
Glorious, good sir! *pops monocle in* A most ingenious airship design, perfect for exploring the aetheric winds of the upper stratosphere!
LOL.

John, wonderful depth. Great geometry. Can't wait to see this fully rendered. You are a great talent. sir! And please enjoy this: [link] Upon seeing the video, I instantly thought you would enjoy it. Be well! Jose
Jose, that was completely insane!!!! I definitely mean that in a good way.
This is sooo amazing!!! I wish I was that good at drawing in perspective T-T

If you're looking to sharpen your perspective, I highly recommend ordering this book. [link] It breaks down perspective into clear digestable techniques. Sadly, there's an even better book from the same publisher, but it's long out of print Anyway, check this out.
Awesome! Thanks so much!

Being absolutely honest - I love this. The rest of your stuff I'm not that keen on. It is all too cartoon-ish and I honestly believe you should set your talents toward creating something more individual and unique to you. Your anime stuff is very competent but just too run-of-the mill and ordinary. You have real talent but I think you are wasting it on a childlike pseudo-artform.
It is never a waste to do the work that inspires you.

You are quite correct. Inspiration is what you must follow. My criticism is only because I have a personal distain for the anime genre, it seems such a waste of your talent but that's because I just don't like anime. I think you have real talent though.

title: Terp Valk
description: This is my end of a holiday gift exchange with my good friend, Phil Moyer. Normally, as he is also a photoshop aficionado, Phil likes to color other artists' line art himself, but as his end of the exchange was the complete DVD set of the saintly, perfect Galaxy Rangers animated series , I felt obliged to put in an extra effort. I'm hoping you recognize the type of jet in the picture. (Heck with it, if you don't recognize the type of jet in the picture, you're just not worth talking to. ) The character is actually Phil's OC,Aerianne Conner, who is both a bad ass fighter pilot, and a "Highlander" style immortal. (I'm thinking she's going to win that duel with the Kurgan, packin' this sort of heat. ) I'm probably doing all manner of injustice to the character in my description, best you visit Phil's site for more pertinent information. Oh, the markings and roundels for the craft belong to the Terrapin Corps, an elite squad of intergalactic, heroic bad asses, of which Aerianne is a member in good standing. Did I mention that my friend is a graduate of the University of Maryland? I should probably mention, this wasn't the final version of the character as accepted by my friend. He wanted Aerianne's hair to be straighter, and her bangs to be shorter, so the final drawing differs slightly from what you see here. However, I liked this version so much I just felt it shouldn't go to waste. Aerrianne Conner & Terrapin Corps are © Philip J. Moyer

comments.body: Not Only I recognise the jet , as I have 3 of them on my shelf. Very nice art . how you made it ?

I only have two, one of which is a Transformers Jetfire, that I bought at a Kaybee Toys, way back in 1987. (There were a whole shelf of them that they were clearing out for half off!! Why oh why did I buy only the one? ) I lost the armor for the Jetfire sometime in the intervening near quarter century. I still keep the plane on my light table. That was one well built toy.
Awesome work here
LOL, I swear RedwingsDragon I begin to think you just copy and paste the "Awesome work here" line wherever you leave a comment!
... Crap he's on to me.... *calls hitman*
Check my last Dev ID I am the Hitman!
Oh... Well can you hit yourself?
You couldn't afford that.
Don't sweat it, man. Saying stuff is awesome is a good thing, no matter how many times you do it.
You'd be surprised how many people I've pissed off with my comment.
VF1-J and I almost exclaimed MILIA STERLING before reading your description

VF-1S Super Valkyrie actually. The J model has only two laser cannons under the cockpit area that become the head in battroid mode, and this one has the 4 that indicate the S model. Also it would be Millia Jenius, not Sterling in this case. The cockpit detail is from the Do You Remember Love movie rather than the original Macross TV series, so no Robotech names for this artwork. Either way, Millia has green hair, not red.

Oh man, my memory must be rusty as heck. Thank you very much. I always refer them as Veritechs since we have Robotechs rerun here in my country. Also, mentioning Mospeada's LEGIOSS as Veritech would further anger macross fanbois Harmony Gold just clumps the animes together into one big series
"Fanbois." I like that spelling.

Good, good. This is the kind of thing I was looking for. Many I've shown this picture to in RL, just issue the admittedly well-meaning "Neat airplane, did you make that up?" While I'm often tempted to respond, "No Shōji Kawamori made it up, you philistine!!!" to such astounding ignorance, my wrath is blunted by the knowledge that they were paying me a compliment.

GawdDAYUM this is an awesome New Year's gift from you, even if it wasn't meant for me personally. But I saw this in my inbox and suddenly my inner fanboi was dancing around rocking old school. I'll admit, I don't remember all the details of a VF, but I hope you don't mind if I point some things out: a) That ain't a standard UN Spacy™ helmet. If you'll look at the opening credits animation (Macross/Robotech, doesn't matter), you'll see that Hikaru/Rick's helmet's got a visor and "blast shield lenses" that he can lower and raise at will, and every other UN Spacy/RDF pilot's helmet has the same design. b) The chain gun's colored orange? Typically it was just colored black or a very dark gunmetal gray. Besides, wouldn't continuous/sustained rapid fire from that sucker discolor the muzzle? But then again, Terrapin Corps sounds like a private military army, the M/R equivalent of the mercenaries from the Area 88 series. Supporting this concept, IMO, is the Corps' logo on the floor of the hangar bay. In either M or R, I can never recall a similar logo. Looks like we've got ourselves a group of fighter aces with very, dare I say it, hot-blooded personalities. Crud, I just troped myself. I type the phrase "hot-blooded" and all of a sudden I imagine these pilots pulling of hairpin turns all the while kicking reason to the curb and going beyond the impossible, roboteching and macross missile massacre-ing all the way. (Sorry, never bothered going to the dA account you linked >.<)

a.) That's the back of the helmet, my friend. b.) When in robot mode this particular fighter uses a a weapon like Galvatron used in the animated Transformers movie. In this case, it certainly isn't standard equipment. (You'll have to ask Phil about that particular modification.)

Actually, Phil sent me a more complete answer to relay to you: ------------------------------------------- To InsolentWelp: a) Actually, it IS a standard UN Spacy Helmet -- we're just looking at it from the back. (I was confused at first as well, seeing this draft. The finished version is clearer in that regard.) b) That isn't a chain gun. It's a fusion cannon. (3 guesses who it's from, first two don't count). (For what it's worth, Aerianne is well-trained in the use of the GU-11 gunpod. It's just, as a commander and squadron leader, she has the option to choose more specialized armaments.) c) The Terrapin Corps is a cross-dimensional peacekeeping force, having adventures across the multiverse, trying to keep people from screwing up their homeworlds too badly, putting the smack down on people who deserve it, while cruising around in their giant 4-kilometer-long terrapin-shaped flagship, the USS Testudo. (Whaddya want? I came up with the concept back in 1994...) ---- Philip Moyer ----------------------------------------- Hope that answers everything.
Wow Great piece !
Wow. Nice

You know, looking at this picture and the VF-1J sitting up on the shelf I just got... Wow.... You did a very very good job at replicating it even though mine only has 2 cannons on the head where as this one is the four. I still say wow you did a good job. Very very detailed as if you had the model yourself.
Yes, and a Shoji Kawamori sketchbook. One that shows there are multiple variants to the head module--but I'm sure you already knew that from watching the show.

WOOT! Maryland! XD I totally got the joke...if it wasn't supposed to be then it was definitely sorta like an ironic pun o.o I loved watching Galaxy Rangers. Nowadays if you mention them, people confuse them with the Galaxy Rangers from the Power Rangers franchise. And that's sad <__< I loved the theme song to Galaxy Rangers. *hums* No guts no glory~
Yea, I hate doing a search on GR and coming up Power Rangers, or some such nonsense. That was the ultimate 80's cartoon. And yes, the Terrapin Corps refers to UMD. Really, what else would it be for?
Ummm...a species of turtle? XD *brofist* Totally. I also watched Godzilla, the cartoon series. I also liked the TMNT original cartoon series too.
Yes, but who else would even think to use a species of turtle as a mascot?
.....Kentucky? O_O They have alot of turtles there.
I usually think of horses when I think of Kentucky. And chicken. And meth labs--but that's because I watch too much Justified.

Nah. have you ever heard of the show "Call of the Wild Man?" on Animal Planet? THAT'S what makes me think of turtles. Mmmmm...Kentucky Fried Chicken~ <3 o.o Hahahaha. Watched that show once...lost interest due to short attention span.
You need to work on lengthening that attention span. Justified really is a terrific show. It's full of marshal gunning down bad guy goodness.

*sweatdrops* ADHD and asperger's syndrome are my worst enemy right now. Having those two make life difficult for me, including staying on topic and keeping attention where it should. I'll watch some later after I watch Doctor Who and Torchwood. Whenever I see Jack Harkness I squeal XD

Again, late to the party on this one... Always great to see classic mecha piloted by red-heads! One of things I liked about Roy Fokker's VF-1S Super Valkyrie in the Do You Remember Love? movie was double beam cannon mounted on the starboard booster pod. Pity that a certain "back-stabber" of a Zentraedi (literally) got the drop on him before he could use it. Out of curiosity, any future plans for some SPACE BATTLESHIP YAMATO 2199 mecha? [link]
Yes!!! As well as Yamato 2199 WOMEN!!!! The brainy girl in the blue uniform is hawt. And the nurse!!!

Yes, at least these gals are not being put into "suspended animation" for the rest of the voyage after the "last call to Earth" episode, as it was retroactively explained for the original 1974 series... I guess I shouldn't been surprised how quickly the hentai arts of the new gals showed up on PIXIV right after the character designs were publically released earlier this year...

There's hentai art for Starscream, the robot who turns into a jet. I'm not surprised that folks would draw actual human women the first chance they got. I suppose the suspended animation excuse is the only way they can explain why there were several women in the episode, but none anyplace else. Really creepy though.

And let's not forget the yaoi art of Admiral Okita and Chief Tokugawa... I was at Summer Comiket (Comic Market in Tokyo) last year, and one of my expat friends pointed out a really nice Desslar dojinshi for me... Passed on it because I recognized the artist from PIXIV and I already knew was inside... *Brrrr* I did meet the people who do the Galaxy Navy art books [link] and picked up EDF and Garuman-Gamilus art books... I still wish the EDF Arizona / Pennsylvania Galaxy Navy book was still availible... I really liked that ship design.
The gunpod looks like Galvatron's cannon...hmm
Does it now?

Just one quibble, and mildly surprised nobody has picked it in the years previous: The VF in the booster configuration has a nose down stance when sitting on its wheels. This image shows it sitting level. I also echo the question about why she has Galvatron's cannon.
The Galvatron gun was at the request of the commissioner. The landing gear was just an oversight. Only had reference for VF-1A landing gear.
Truly awesome and beautiful! The girl looks pretty good too.
Thank you. I've gotta get to drawing more. Looking at this reminds me of how good it felt to really go all in an on a drawing.

Hmmm, sounds like something to keep in mind. "And he saw the magistrate takin' Every dollar, and leavin' five cents. He said 'You can't do that to my people!' 'Gonna show you how it feels!' And so he strapped of his hat And in five second flat, Stole everything that Higgins had to steal..." Oh, did you pick up the one shot about Shepherd Book's past, that came in this past Wednesday?

It's so evocative, so high-tech... and yet so bureaucratic! I bet you invested quite a bit of time on this design, huh? It really shows! What's that little bulb up on the top of the weapon? Next to that red line.

It's funny that you should use the term "bureaucratic." I have a good friend who is particularly anti-authoritarian. I plan on using that quote as a cover blurb.

I'd like to request a sidearm be made that fires high velocity Koosh balls.
A sidearm? No. Such a weapon should be vehicle mounted!!
A friend was browsing the Weapon Designers group and posted this on Facebook. I got a great chuckle upon seeing it.
I'd be curious to see the link, if you please.
Hm? What do you mean? It was just a post on Facebook about a bunch of the weapons in the group .
I'm just curious to see a comment on my work in another venue. You should know by now what a feedback whore I am.

title: Winged Pony Car
description: The only thing more astounding than the discovery of the miracle fossil fuel, BOA®1 beneath the surface of Mars2 was the subsequent discovery that polymers made from it, repelled gravity when exposed to electrical current. Though practical antigravity, or Antiweight® will have been in use for three decades by the time of America Jones' "NTDDCF" 3, it is only now ever so slowly making its way into the hands of an awaiting public. To date, FT's, or Floating Transports have only been used by heavily trained and licensed services. Such as Heavy FT's, or "HeFT's" for construction and freight hauling; and Light FT's or the "LiFT's" that have become infamous through their use by the DMV. The Medium FT's, or "MeFT's" are potentially the most diverse breed of these vehicles, as they could represent the realizations of science fiction's long welshed-upon promise of "flying automobiles", but they are the last to be fully developed due to the vast multitude of safety concerns they raise. Even so, for years automotive manufacturers have fought legal battles to enable them to build more than the occasional tow truck, or playthings for the astoundingly wealthy. After decades of war with various levels of legislature, as well as the insurance industry, it looks like the first practical, affordable MeFT will finally find its way into the open arms of the common driver. That vehicle is the Pegasus 4. One more headache for the Meter Maids. 1.) Blood Of Ares. Legal wranglings with the Wells estate, prevented the use of the name "Cavarite". 2.) As every schoolchild knows today, the discovery of BOA® was made in 1976 by the Viking lander, and withheld from public knowledge, until regular travel was established in 2045 3.) "Not-to-Distant-Dystopian-Cyberpunk-Future." Gotta come up with a more convenient anagram of acronym for that. 4.) Yes, it's painfully obvious homage to the Mustang the first high-performance sports car, truly affordable to the common driver--or "Pony car" as it were.

comments.body: Neat! As an old-car fetishist and flying-car fan from childhood, this one's a winner as far as I'm concerned. I dig the front bumper/grille/fender treatment, I think I have something similar in a sketchbook somewhere, but yours is much better. I also dig what appears to be directional vanes in place of the signature vertical bars in the taillight bezels (am I right?). Keep it up!

You made my day, friend. I always figured, that ever since the jet age first kicked off, automobile manufacturers had made tail lights to mock jet exhaust, why not return the favor with a flying car's exhaust? But it was really one of those things I did for my own self edification--I totally did not expect anyone to notice it.

Ah, interesting! I'm surprised to find someone else who'd pondered the thought of "aircraft styling turned into automotive styling turned back into aircraft styling." The comic story I'd like to do eventually involves a spacecraft styled after a 1960 Buick, which in turn was heavily styled after a B-52. At least I know I'm not the only one!
I'd love to see the Quad Coupe.
You will.
looks like some supercharge muscle car to me. Mars huh? reminds me of biker mice from mars. being meter maids, i dont think they will be caring any chalks sticks from your under arrest.
Chalk sticks? Not quite.

title: Chiriones 06
description: Sure its another Marilith (And her hostage/sidekick--whose name tragically escapes my memory.), but it's all chibi-fied, so I might as well add it to the "series." Marilith is ©Sean Lindsey.

title: Sniper Girl
description: An old convention sketch I did of the character Marilith back in the day. I know most of you are surprised to see a picture of a girl with a gun in this gallery, but I assure my loyal fan that I'll be back to my usual pictures of puppies and flowers on my next post. Marilith is ©Sean Lindsey.

comments.body: Ironsights? Very leet. (this commenter ignores the posterior and straightaway notices the gun. His orientation is now questionable)
If its any consolation, your anal retentiveness was never in doubt.
Gorgeous. I hope her hair doesn't get in the way of her sniping. I know it can't get in the way of her looking awesome.
It's anime girl hair. It never gets in the way, or goes flat, or gets split ends, or has a bad hair day.
So true. Not to mention its gravity-defying properties.
It's certainly not the only thing about anime girls that defies gravity. And that's why we love them.

You have the right idea, you do. Anime characters are always so bubbly, so fun to look at... when animted by a studio or drawn by lovely artists like yourself. Real life gets a little more... tenuous? [link]

title: Repeat
description: Quick sketch of another character from the "not-too-distant-dystopian-cyberpunk-future. As you can tell from the uniform--another Meter Maid. Miriam Bradley is an inexperienced Parking Enforcement Officer, still growing out her hair from her strict academy bowl-cut. She idolizes America Jones almost like one would a popular upperclassman, following the seasoned veteran around like a lost puppy, and obsessing over her arrest record like the stats of a popular athlete. The younger officer aspires to one day to have an comparable citation record, instill as much respect and fear among the various Commuter Tribes, and have a similarly curvy figure as her more experienced counterpart. (At her present rate of development, only two of her three goals are most likely possible. ) Miriam has acquired the nickname "Repeat" due to her preference of carrying two PTD's-- one on each hip. Repeat tends to be over eager, and often barrages offenders, frequently even coating them with tickets. (I am actually considering ginving her two semi-automatic PTD's, whose larger magazines hold 1,000 tickets each. ) At present she is a far cry from AJs cool, "one shot per customer" demeanor, but due her habit of wallpapering her opponents, she is well on her way to being feared by the Tribes.

comments.body: Curves or not, she's adorable.
Cool. I was going for adorable.
I like her hair style. Very nice

choosing the hair color was surprisingly difficult (Since in an anime-inspired story, I have all manner of outlandish, biologically impossible options to choose from.), but I finally decided to choose one that reflected her level of experience.
It was a good choice. I like it.

Thanks again. I'm glad people seem to be reacting well to the character, and her design. When I wrote that she envied AJ's figure, I was a little afraid some would object to the idea of using "body image" as a character motivation, particularly in the context of a female character desiring a body more in line with the "male fantasy ideal." I suppose I'm just thinking about this too much.
I honestly didn't think anything of it. People will always be envious of others, it's in our nature so it makes sense that she'd be envious of AJ. But ya, you're over thinking it
"Offender! Stop!" SPT SPT SPTTATATAT
Actually, the onamatopeia I was going to use was "SPAK!", but you were pretty close.
Spak - that sounds too familiar...
I'm almost scared to hear why.
Adam Warren's Dirty Pair =3. 'Spak' is usually sound of bullets hitting flesh
Well its more innocuous here. Even though firearms will be used with dizzying frequency, almost no one will ever get hurt or die. You, know, like in the old G.I. Joe cartoon.
hmm, The linework is different than the way you usually do it. Love the guns in action too.

That's because I didn't actually ink this one. I scanned the finished pencils, copied them over themselves, and multiplied the top layer to darken them. Me being lazy, I guess. Lazier than normal anyway. Thanks for the good words.
Nice colors.
Thanks!
You're welcome.

title: PTD: Parking Ticket Dispenser
description: A Samurai has his Katana. A Jedi has his Light Sabre. In the "not to distant dystopian cyberpunk future", Meter Maids will have Parking Ticket Dispensers as their chief instrument of dispensing justice. Since the initials, "PTD", are very similar to Philip K Dick's initials--which are used for the eponymous PKD pistol of Blade Runner fame-- and since DMV is more or less, a cyberpunk story, I took a lot of cues from the iconic weapon. such as the clear polyethylene grips--except that I decided to go with a more "policeman like" palette for their coloring. Initially, I wanted to use the same general proportions from a Colt Army Single Action revolver for this retro-futuristic weapon, but I found that a Colt Anaconda worked better for my purposes. (Many thanks to my good friend and retired postal worker Ken Cox for the gift of the Complete World Encyclopedia of Guns. ) A little pseudo-technical information: A standard PTD carries 250 blank parking tickets in the form of compactly folded sheets of interwoven carbon nanotubes. Each ticket is accelerated to approximately 300 meters per second by a series of 110 wafer thin magnetic field accelerators, lining the interior of the dispenser's barrel, and is programmed to unfold within seconds of attaching itself, nigh-irremoveably, to a target. With a PTD, every Parking Enforcement Officer holds the power of life and debt in her hands.

comments.body: I am astounded by this design, your tropospheric levels of geekery, and your witty play of words. We are not worthy!
Thanks for the kind words. I don't want to see the day where one can't seriously bring the geek when writing a Comic Book.
Even though you are arming and promoting one of the lowest forms of life on Earth, I will forgive you on the basis that it's a freakin' awesome design .
"Arming and promoting"? My friend, you have no idea.
Woah , if that's the next generation of traffic wardens, I'm going to be parking illegally quite often .
Looks so badass great work I am looking forward to seeing more of youre work ^^
you,re very welcome ^^
Is it meant for two hands? Cool design though
No, the heroine handles it with one hand, shooting from the hip, like a frontier gun fighter. I'm curious, what about the design suggests to you a two handed weapon?

Not so much the design, as to how your heroine has to fire it. Whenever I have had to hold a gun (and yes, I've fired guns and rifles before...I am a West Virginian after all..lol), with a gun I usually had to use both hands to aim fairly well. I thought the handle looked too small for a two handed grip which I would need at least to fire well. Maybe your heroine has more arm strength than me

I've fired guns once or twice myself, and yes I recognize that real world physics really put a crimp in one handed shooting and lookin' all cool, shooting from the hip. I could say that a PTD's linear accelerators has significantly less recoil than that of a traditional gas-operated weapon, allowing single handed accuracy, but, truth be told, while I put a lot of time and research trying to give the guns themselves a sense of verisimilitude; the way they'll be used won't have much to do with recognizable gun etiqette. I guess I've watched too many of this type of film.
Woah awesome design, looks almost like done with a 3D software, and I mean it in a very good way of course
I appreciate it as always.
Man, this is too funny! I love this.
that description is so bad it's awesome.
Thanks--I think.
its just so full of puns it is awesome.
... You put alot of thought and time into this. I am impressed... but seriously dude, you don't want to give them to much more power. They're already hyper with sadism and ink as it is!
"hyper with sadism and ink" Nice.
You hae some how magaed to drain even more dignity out of the Meter Maids. Congrads.
Oh, I'm just gettin' started.

title: Tiffany and Charlotte 03
description: More of 's Tiffany and Charlotte, deuling with BMX bikes versus bio-engineered wings. Sorry folks, this is as far as I got. I figured their adventuring should lead to some manner of trouble, but I hadn't figured out what. What would be the suburban neiborhood equivalent of Draconian Hatchet Fighters?

comments.body: i dont know what kinda comic this is, but i love the panels. great job.
Thanks.
Chainsaw Wielding Maniacs. - Polecat

Your answer makes me realize how the words "Draconian Hatchet Fighter" can be taken out of context. The words do suggest musclebound, axe-wielding thugs, to which Chainsaw Weilding Mainiacs would be an appropriate analog. However, in the Buck Rogers show, a Hatchet Fighter was a sleek, single-occupant spacecraft, optimized for high-speed, ship-to-ship combat. [link] It was the main fighting spacecraft of the Draconian Empire and the tactical equivalent of the Earth Defense Directorate Starfighter. [link] So what would be the equivalent of a Draconian fighter plane? I'm thinking something more along the lines of bullying older kids with their own bikes. Chainsaw Weilding Maniacs are more the purview of Tiffany's mother, or Charlotte's caretaker.

If you're trying to analog them with bikes, there's 2 possibilities that come to mind then. 1: A stripped down "courier" type bike, Y'know the sort where they stripped off the brakes? Or 2: Those really nimble BMX stunt-bikes. And yes, that's the way I took it. I obviously don't know Buck Rogers as well as I know Star Wars. >.< - Polecat

A little trivia. Artist and general design bad ass, Ralph McQuarrie designed the ships for Star Wars, Battlestar Galactica, and Buck Rogers! In fact, the Buck Rogers EDD Starfighter was actually McQuarrie's rejected first draft design for the Colonial Viper. [link] [link]
I did not know that!! Explains the "cool" factor of all 3 in the ships tho. No one ever quite managed that style again IMHO. - Polecat
Obviously the Hatchet Fighters are in-line roller skaters playing street hockey.
You might have something there. Or perhaps, rebellious skateboarders.

title: Tiffany and Charlotte 02
description: More on 's insanely adorable cat-girl and bird-girl, Tiffany and Charlotte. As you may or may not recall, I was mystified this two never played Buck Rogers vs. Hawk in their BR inspired, Calvin and Hobbesesque, fantasy, bicycle dogfights. So mystified was I that I scribbled some panels into my sketch book on the matter. Sadly my curiosity was not strong enough to get me to finish this enterprise , but I figured folks wouldn't be offended if I posted what I had. I hope folks can follow what's going on here.

comments.body: I recognize the Buck Rodgers fighter... Not sure where the "Hawk" fighter is from. - Polecat

The Warhawk is from Buck Rogers. [link] In the premier of season two, Buck meant the only pilot in the show that was his equal*--a half bird humanoid, named Hawk. The two started out at odds, resulting in THE BEST dogfight of the series. But eventually, the two united against a common foe, and became fast friends. (Never seen that plot before, have we.? ) *Aside from the mysterious unnamed old bad-ass played by Buster Crabbe (Same actor who played the 1930's Buck Rogers ) who saved Buck's bacon in the second half of "Planet of the Slave Girls."--Gawd, I loved that show.

Heh. I don't think I saw those episodes. The local station was notorious for playing the same handful of episodes over and over and over again.... I only saw like 3 episodes of the second season at all, they always showed the first. - Polecat
I hate when they do that. Reminds me of how the Boomerang network will only show the same two episodes of Teen Titans.
That's why I have all 5 seasons of the Teen Titans on DVD. ^_^ - Polecat
Holy cow, there's some old memories for you. I remember that show. VERY nice!
Thanks. I was beginning to think I was the only one.

title: Tiffany and Charlotte 01
description: An old sketch of a pair of characters from one of my favorite comics, Gold Digger, by . For the uninitiated, the duo depicted here are Tiffany and Charlotte, a cute li'l were-cheetah girl, and a genetically engineered bird-girl with the body of a woman, and the mind of a pre-teen, respectively. What is of interest to me is the fact that Tiffany, on occasion, pretends to be Buck Rogers*, while riding around on her bike in Bill Wattersonesque fantasies, yet even though she has a "bestest friend" with her own working pair of wings, she has yet to replay the second season opening dogfight between Buck and Hawk. This omission is a continuing source of mystery to me. * I mean of course, the 1979 Glen Larson produced, Erin Gray in spandex, Gary Coleman as president, incarnation of Buck Rogers. Which as you all know, is the only one that mattered.

comments.body: Interesting Duo. I stopped following GD a long LONG time ago, so I have no clue who the characters are honestly. Still, it's an interesting look you gave 'em. - Polecat
I gave Charlotte, the bird girl, a feminine version of Hawk's armor. [link] Looks better on her in my opinion.
OH! Heh, I completely forgot about him. They didn't show the season he was in very much locally, only the first season over and over and over.... - Polecat

Probably with good reason. I'm sorry to say, after the first two episodes with the Buck/Hawk showdown, they got rid of the "James Bond in Space" angle, put the characters on a ship called the "Searcher", and tried to ape off of Star Trek. There were still some good episodes, like one where Buck was accused of starting the world war that took Earth 500 years to recover from. There was some Hawk badassery in that one, as he helped Buck bust out of EDD custody to clear his name. There was also an infamous episode in which telekinetic space dwarves came aboard the ship, and used their powers to try to "Off-Think" Erin Gray's uniform. (I $#!+ you not! ) But for the most part season two just didn't have the crazy, campy energy of season one. Oh, and Mel Blanc stopped providing the voice for Twiki.
That last part I DO remember, because I always went "WTF" with what happened to Twiki (I thought it was Twiggi?) 's voice. - Polecat

I believe the brought Mel Back toward the end, but it was probably "too little, too late." I wonder why they changed the show's format from espionage adventures with all the spandex clad, pretty girls your eyes could stand, all in perils that could only be resolved by either a laser beam gun battle, or an outer space dogfight. That's televison GOLD right there. Why would anyone change that?
No idea. I'd say to try and emulate Star Trek.... and maybe try to prop up sagging ratings? I don't know. - Polecat

Ah yes, the telekinetic space dwarves. What I remember most about them is that they all proudly introduced themselves as generals and wore fancy uniforms, except for the sole African American of the group, who introduced himself as a private and wore olive drab. For all its faults, I do give season two credit for introducing a robot that was a real robot and not just a man in a suit.
Noticed that too did'ja. Why was the only black dwarf, the one takin' orders? I mean all the other dwarves were generals!
DAAAAAAAAAMN! I still love how both your inks and pencils just seem to come off so cool!
Thanks for the nice words.

Just a statement of fact! I do look forward to seeing more of your art (on DA), for it does make me rather jealous/envious of such a wonderful style (especially the inks), but it still doesn't stop the admiration Still recall your stuff (a while back) on Marilith(?) which is still impressive Needless to say, I do like what I see! Last time I recall seeing you was in Otakon and had a blast! Hopefully, you're doing some cons!
I LOVED the episode with Gary Coleman.
That was fun. I love his hot secret secret service babe, who kept a huge blaster in her purse.

title: Maid and Machine
description: Another DMV picture. America Jones once again, this time with a slightly altered uniform. Seeing how actual meter maids were day-glo vests at present, I thought I'd try one out on our heroine. The low belt, bike pants were suggested by so I thought I'd try them for a spin. It's been a while since I designed AJ's uniform, so I thought I might try to freshen up the look. You'll note here AJ's PTD is in transit between her hand and its holster. I always loved the old Galaxy Ranger cartoon and how the straps on their holsters would pop open on command. I thought it would be cool if the entire holster opened up and somehow "magleved" the PTD to its user. On more note, to facilitate this unhinging action I made a slit running down the side of the holster, similar to the ones the Colonial Warriors used in the original Battlestar Galactica. Yea, I know--I'm putting wayyy too much thought into this. The robot in front of AJ is a Roboot, and semi-autonomous device that attaches itself to the tires of cars with delinquent parking tickets. This little critter is one of two, carried in integrated housing in AJ's Light Floating Transport or LiFT. Though suited to fill the role of spunky sci-fi robot, Roboots are regarded with unqualified dread by the average commuter.

comments.body: Now if only they could find and shoot the programmer who made the roboots say "I looove you" as they hug themselves tightly around a tire.
Actually they sing "I'm Never Gonna Let You Go." by Sergio Mendes.
"From now on, the only rolling this car is going to do is Rick-rolling!"
Nice "hero gloat."
Stop me before a Filk a song about parking boots and tickets to that Rick Astly tune....
I'm sorry. What's a "filk"?

Science Fiction Folk song. (Actually, they aren't necessarily Folk, but the term was born of a Typo in a WorldCon program book many moons ago.) More famous examples would be that Star Wars song that Weird Al did, but any SF Con worth it's salt has a Filking circle late at night. So if I re-did the lyrics to that song and made it about robotic parking boots, that would be a Filk. Singing it would be Filking, and it would make me a Filker. (Oddly, Irish music is also popular among filkers. Must be all the blood and battles, and Elves and Fae folk.)
Very nice design. I like the robot (err, Roboot) design a lot, the way it stands, looks like I can imagine it in some CG movies

Thanks. I was going to have the Roboot scuttle around on all fours like a crab (Or a really fast turtle. ), and it still can--but for the purposes of this pin-up, it would've ended up just looking like a footstool. Rearing the thing up on its hind legs allowed me to give it some degree of personality. Movies huh? Hmmm, the girl who played the kid sister in "Fresh Prince of Bel Air" should be just about the right age now. [link]

Oh hey cool! I'm not the ONLY person in the world who remembers the old Galaxy Rangers!! Still end up humming the danged theme song these days... >.< As for the holster, that is a wicked idea, tho it reminds me a little of the Patlabor robots design, where the leg panel would open up and lift up the pistol right into the hand (Tho more mechanical). As for the bot there... is it just me or does the disk shape remind you of the "saucer" section of the Enterprise? O.o - Polecat

Galaxy Rangers was possibly the best adventure cartoon ever. Certainly the best of the 1980's. It was basically "Firefly: The Animated Series". Zachary Fox was like Mal. Telepathic Niko () was like River, and Doc Hartford was their Book. (Actually, he's more like how I'd imagine Zoe and Wash's son turning out, now that I think of it. And River had more than a little of genetic super-weapon Shane thrown in, but you get the picture. ) Of course to me, Rangers corrects what in my mind is Firefly's greatest flaw--lack of bad ass space fighters. Both show even got canceled after one season. Too cool for TV I guess. You really seem hung up on detail. Let me ask you. Are you saying that the sources you are citing give you the impression that fundamental skills like anatomy and perspective are unimportant? You say that they look for detail first. Do you believe that they don't look for the fundamentals at all?

Unfortunately I never caught Firefly when it was out, but I managed to get hold of the collected series on DVD< so I plan to correct that in the near future. However I agree. Plus you gotta admit that intro was just awesome. Makes me wish that the animation in the episodes was as good as the stuff in the opening. Plus they seemed to set up in the first episode or two a serious over-arching storyline... then it was like someone said that was too serious for a kiddie show and made them lighten the tone way up. Rangers had a lot of potential, but it was like the developers and the producers had different visions of what direction it was going to develop in. It would have been interesting if they'd been willing to take the risk and keep it slightly more mature methinks. Maybe then we'd have figured out exactly what Goose was... or not. O.o - Polecat

There were two episodes that had animation on par with the opening: Psychocrypt and Sundancer. I'm always disappointed by Sun Dancer, because it was the second half of a two-parter and all the cool violence happened in part one. Part two was about a horse race. An awesomely animated horse race, but still... At least we'll always have Psychocrypt.

I must've missed those. But then the local station as playing them at an obscene time and in no particular order (Which means they kept showing the same couple over and over again). Always wished that series had lived up to it's potential... But then, Firefly is a sort of spiritual successor I suppose of the whole "Cowboys in space" theme. - Polecat
You should get the DVD. Psychocrypt is in the volume entitled "Phoenix". I don't think Sundancer has been collected, but I think you can find it on Youtube. [link] [link]
I plan to eventually, but I'll admit the DVDs aren't high on the ol' priority list right now. Got some other crap pulling for my cash at present. - Polecat
I think she turned out well The low rise shorts are definitely an improvement on the 90's belly waist lol

I'm always impressed by your ability to connect an article of clothing with a certain era. I've think we've discussed before that I can do that with cars, but I'm lost with women's fashion. Between you, me, the keyboard, and probably a lurker or two, the primary focus of my attention were women's fashion is concerned is what part of the body is shown off.

The premise of the Roboot is hilarious. I can just imagine motorists kneeling by their parked cars and pleading with the Roboot to let them go just this once. Can they curl up and roll like destroyer droids?
Hmmmm.
cool - robocop like gun holster...
Dang. I forgot about Robocop's holster. Well I suppose I could bluff and count that among the references I'm paying homage to as well.
that's what I would do... Where will this comic be featured, online? in print?
I'll get back to you one that.

Actually,I love it when artists put a lot of thought and art aspects into the stuff they're working on,at least if they cared enough to explain them like you do. C: Lets me learn a lot of things.Love the holster concept.

Gotta admit, I love that too. Half the fun of appreciating a good work of art is in learning precisely how the artist arrived at the particular decisions--why'd they use a certain angle? What compositions they tried and discarded, and why? What previous works or unrelated objects influenced their vision. Learning the process whose result is a favored image is oftentimes as enjoyable as the image itself. I can't stand it when artists put ellipses in their artists comments. If I were running things, a deviation would not post until an artist typed a minimum of 25 words in their artist comments. Of course, if one forces the issue, folks'll probably do passive/aggressive junk like typing "One, two, three, four..." and so on. Oh well.
maid an machine, power extreme
It looks so modern. Unlike so many other designs, this one actually looks plausibly futuristic. ...yes, even the bike shorts.
Actually, the bike shorts were a suggestion by a another female fan.
Well, they fit her. Very cute. I wonder if those are regulation, too?
Yes, AJ is nothing if not a stickler for regulations.
I trust that she keeps her ticket gun well-lubricated and ready to fire.
Well..yea, naturally. But the way you put it sounds naughty.
I try.
Maid and Machine, Power Extreme.
maid and machine, power extreme
Actually, that would probably work better for this picture. fav.me/d1qtfc8

title: Rifle Maid
description: Here's a sketch of America Jones, the "not-too-distant-cyberpunk-dystopian-future's" toughest Parking Enforcemnet Officer! (AJ, for short. ) The weapon she has slung over her shoulder is a Palomino 61M Assault PTD-- the inevitable rifle version of her trusty sidearm, the PTD (Parking Ticket Dispenser.*) The Assault PTD dispenses the same 5.5'' x 8.5" parking ticket as the pistol type, but at a significantly higher rate of fire. The larger barrel beneath the primary muzzle fires larger 6' x 9' tickets, which can paper an average man to a wall from as much as 250 yards. APTD's are largely used for crowd control situations. *Yes, the PTD is a mild tribute to the eponymous PKD of "Blade Runner" fame. When I realized how the initials came out, I almost felt obligated.

comments.body: She's got those shorts hiked WAY up. No wonder she's such a tough meter maid, her panties MUST be in a bunch.

Damn, I can not debate that. *Redraws hips* Take another look, let me know what you think. And let me know what you think of the rifle concept, ya gearhead. What's the use of having a visitor who builds his own robots, if he doesn't comment on the tech?!

Definite improvement. And now I'm going to scrutinize for proper belt buckle orientation.... oops. The gun's a little tricky in terms of figuring out which tube the round comes out of. But the idea of papering a guy to the wall reminds me of an idea I had once for an Unreal mod and a tape gun that would shoot 3' segments of duct tape that would wrap, bolo-like around the target, the idea being that you hosed the target up and down until they were mummified. I also thought her head might be a little small, but it's too nice to mess with.

You know what? Scrutinize away, my friend. I just did a side-by-side comparison between the modified and unmodified jpegs, and found myself aghast that I allowed myself to post the first version. If I can't take criticism, especially when it's accurate, then what the hell good am I? Also, thanks for the comments on the gun, and sorry for begging for them like some kind of art diva. I've gotten so accustomed to the abundant flow of feedback from other sources that I've gotten a little spoiled. If I want that kind of dialogue in this gallery I'm going to have to work for it here, and not think I'm in any position to demand it. Thanks again.
I took a photo a long time ago for a first pass at the Buckle tutorial, I really need to finish it off.
The subtle hint I was getting at was that the tongue of the buckle should point the opposite way from the free end of the belt.

I had it right in the freakish original drawing, but as I was making corrections, I had the thought, "Hey wait a minute, men and women open their coats in the opposite direction. Wouldn't that hold true for the direction of their belts?" And all I had to do was look in a magazine. That's the trouble with the things you think you know. You tend to do less research.
Interesting idea... tho her hips seem to be very long, or she's really short-waisted... O.o - Polecat

As I recall, I was trying to capture that skinny Sonoda-esque look in the figure. Don't know why, just experimenting, and not all experiments succeed. Oh well. That's why we have sketchbooks. Any thoughts on the rifle concept?

Lord don't I know. My sketchbooks get filled with pics I don't feel are "good enough"... or false starts on pics I do like and finish. As for the rifle, it seems very effective. Tho admittedly my mind comes up with alternative uses not well suited for the character. But it probably makes catching the occasional perp she comes across far easier.... And the idea of her getting so annoyed at a repeat offender that she completely wraps their car in tickets is hysterical. - Polecat

Well, since the picture was still at the pencil stage, I decided to fix the problem. The "granny shorts" were beginning to bother me as well. Yea, sorry no "alternate uses" here. Besides, perps are by no means "occasional" here. In fact, there will be need for bigger weapons in the future. The "not-to-distant-dystopic-cyberpunk-future" future.
Aye, the classic generic cyberpunk future... - Polecat

Hmmm that might work better than "Not-too-distant-dystopian-cyberpunk-future", which doesn't even lend itself to am acronym. (NTDDCF? See what I mean?) Spaceflight, flying cars, light-up umbrella handles, and all of the street-crime and political corruption of home.
Just like the old RPG I played back in my days in the Navy. - Polecat

Ah man. You have altered my perception of service life. I really bought into the bad ass recuitment advertisements in which Keith David says, in an appropriately bad ass voice, "If someone wrote a book about your life, would anyone want to read it!" Transposed with a whole montage of bad ass dudes jumping out of Blackhawks, and other equally bad ass dudes skipping the surf in a Rigid Hull Inflatable Craft, with their M-4's at the ready. Now you've put the image of some guy like me saying, "Roll to save against mystical damage" In a circle of dudes with pencils, graph paper and twenty-sided dice. If I had known the Navy was more like the latter--I would've enlisted!

I'm ex-NAVY. What else are we gonna do when we're in a floating tin can for months at a time?!? It's not the mainstream, but you'd be amazed how many people picked up RPGs that weren't in the usual "geek crowd" from High School. Hell, one guy there was trying to make one based on the Highlander movies.... Tho the most popular one when I was in was RIFTS. I wasn't a fan, but that was the big game in the mid 90s.... - Polecat

That is so cool, but not altogether surprising when I give it more than a quarter second of thought. It reminds me of an episode of "Mail Call" I saw in which I spotted a Cobra symbol spray painted discreetly on the turret of self propelled artillery piece. We geeks are everywhere!
And we're usually the techies that keep the stuff working!! I mean seriously, there's a reason Tasha is a techie geek, because her PLAYER worked on radar systems for the Navy!! - Polecat

I love it as usual minus one thing. The 80's/early 90's super high waist band that is now reserved only for little old ladies. I love it in the same way I adore late 80's/early 90's anime, but hate it in the way that it makes me cringe as a current fashion distaster. But other than that I adore her

Heh, that was more a drawing flub than a fashion statement. Check the drawing now. I've redrawn the shorts. I suppose she still won't be fashionable, but hopefully she looks a little less freakish now. I am giving some thought to changing her uniform. I've noticed that meter maids nowadays wear day-glo vests--sort of like what deer hunters wear, only yellow as opposed to orange. I was considering giving AJ, and her less tough comrades, a bullet-proof version of such a garment. I suppose, since this is the "not-too-distant-dystopian-cyberpunk-future*", the vests could be self illuminating as well as proof against small arms fire. Don't know what to do about the shorts though. *I really should come up with an acrinym for that. It's becoming increasingly annoying, typing that out. Hmmm. NTDDCF?
Muuuuch better now Even NTDDCF is a hand ful o.0 The Notoedydycyfi?

I guess I'll just have to copy and paste the phrase a lot. Also, since you can link a garment to a decade, (Something that wouldn't have occurred to me. Like most guys, I use automobile model years to measure time. "Hey that's a Lebaron convertible, this film is set in 1983!") I'm guessing you might have an eye for fashion. Any suggestions for a uniform update?
yupp I just took her into photoshop for a minute for an idea of what *I'd* change, but I'm no fashionista so it may suck. I can email it to you?
Got me all curious now. I'll send you a note with my addy.
haha well don't get your hopes up, it's nothing special
Well thank your for taking time to give me your input like this. I'm sure it will be very helpful. I have faith in the wonders a fresh point of view.
Not a problem (And to make you feel better, I can only tell the date of a car if it's pre 67' haha)

It's not too hard to recognize cars from subsequent eras. Cars after 1967 got progressively more tank-like, until the fuel crisis could no longer be ignored in the late 1970's, and since that time cars have been looking steadily more like used bars of soap, with a brief flirtation with tank-likeness again during the Clinton years when everyone had money.
I dunno, my mom's latest Toyota looks a hell of a lot like a tank, and so did her Nitro before that lol. But before that was the PT, which I swear looks like a dog...

Yea, but the biggest, "tankiest" SUV, stills lacks the raw intimidation factor of a 1970's Cadillac sedan. A Venza looks like its taking a family on vacation. A Fleetwood looks like it has five bullet riddled bodies in the trunk--And probably does. Actually, I learned to drive in such a beast. After that, there is nothing I can not parallel park.
Fine work as usual sir. How goes progress in the DMV world?
Thanks, Joe. Regarding progress, as Ferdinand would say, " SS,DD." (Actually, he'd say, "SS, Deeds.", but you get my drift. )
well except for the hair, she kinda reminds me of Cellas Victoria from Hellsing. =]

Huh. I know who you're talking about, but I always thought is was Seras? Hmmm, I see you get the same images fro either spelling on google images. It's amusing watching how the "L's" and "R's" get transposed in anime translation. For instance, I heard that if you'd ask Sonoda he'd tell you it was supposed to be Larry Vincent But there are other differences. Seras/Cellas wear a skirt. And AJ has dark skin, and purple hair. (You can't tell that from here, of course. )

yeah man there are really those two versions of her name, guess i just made a mix of both. and yeah i just googled her and yeap she really used a mini-skirt, but the rest of the costume looks like hers. man can you give me a link of a picture of her in her series, or a artwork? now im interest to see her look.

Well for now, I just have a few images from my own gallery. [link] [link] , [link] [link] There are also a few strips I drew of the character, but I don't want to overload you. With hope, there will be other venues for you to see my intrepid maid of meters. For now, I hope you enjoy my humble scratches.

wow man i saw all of the ones you linked, and i must say your style is very impressive. you do a great job with colors, ur able to make a very colorfull art and it still doesnt seem just too colofull, like a pain in the eyes. also u make a simple but very effective shading work, pretty similar to some good animations. and last, you make some futuristic vechiles very well, like the bike shes riding in one of the drawings. and yeah, she doesnt look like ceras victoria anymore. T.T

Thanks, I'm glad you enjoyed what you saw. With hopes, in the next few weeks and months you'll have more to see. I do take my influences from animations. I worship at the altar of Kenichi Sonoda and Bubblegum Crisis. Of course, I know that makes me all Old Sküle now, but I like what I like.

NTDDCF = "Noted, Chief" Close as I can get with your acronym dilemma. Welcome back, always a treat to see your work, and I like AJ's non-visored look. So that's the rifle you mentioned before, when I said I was afraid what she'd whip out when you parked illegally on the "disabled" space. Great, now I want to see a parking sniper rifle, and a big effing gun like an anti-tank/recoiless rifle. I bet she's got a Tackleberry-like coworker who has a gatling/vulcan dispenser.

Hmmm. "Noted, Chief?" That's actually cute. Certainly more than I was able to come up with. ;XD: Yes there are Sniper PTD's. ('Course now, I can't name the character who carries it, Yoko. ) And I have come up with a vehicle that can mount even heavier PTD's, although I need to come up with a design for the actual mounted weapon. (As well as a name for the 6' x 9' tickets--- "SIXTY-NINES"!!! ) A Tackleberry-esque meter maid? That's a new one. As is a Gatling PTD. I'm thinking the latter is more likely, but who knows?
Just don't forget Callahan.
I know of Dirty Harry Callahan, but no Callahan that would elicit a " " emoticon.
Hmm, I must (re-) introduce you to Police Academy­™, then, and to Debbie Callahan in particular.
Wow, I certainly hope I don't live long enough to see that kind of law enforcement becoming necessary. Even if the ones dispensing such an interesting brand of justice are cute.

The bad news is, this future might be closer than you think. The worse news is, cute meter maids occur only in this gallery, and the occasional movie. (I loved that scene with Elizabeth Hurley in "Bedazzled". , Come to think of it, I loved every scene with Elizabeth Hurley in "Bedazzled". )
...*shudder* thanks for the mental image.
Anytime.
No mirrored sunglasses?

No, but there's another character who wears them. Actually, she's a meter maid AJ encountered in her past. [link] My working name for the character is "WiNiE", (Woman with No Eyes) which is a pretentious reference to a character from the movie "Cool Hand Luke", in which Paul Newman plays a man who is sentenced to a chain gang for vandalizing parking meters.
If that's a traffic warden, I'd hate to see the SWAT teams.

"Traffic Warden?" That actually sounds cooler than "Meter Maid". Perhaps I should have set the story in England. (Then I could name the heroine, "Rita". ) I'm afraid you might be a tad disappointed by the NTCCDF's Special Combat Weapons and Armor Deivision, or "SCWAD". Sure, they have a breathtaking assortment of visionary weapons, equipment and vehicles, and bad ass storm troopery armored uniforms in a fashionable klien blue, but whenever they get a call, a meter maid has resolved the matter by the time the arrive on the scene. They are the Maytag™ repairmen of the future.
That is disappointing. Now I'm actually starting to waonder what the K-9 units look like.

All the duties once performed by dogs are done by spunky l'il robots in the NTDDCF1. The exposure of animals to harmful situations like explosives detection is considered barbaric by the more evolved denizens of the future. The "K-9's" employed are more like Doctor Who's old robotic sidekick--only, you know, called something else, since I don't have any rights to that name. 1.) Not To Distant Dystopian Cyberpunk Future, in case you were wondering.
Glad that's cleared the future police system up.
Hey Man May I upload this to the AVPress Facebook page?
Sure, knock yerself out. In the coming weeks I should have a few color items you can use. Nothing big, vehicle, architectural, and weapons designs, but they should be of some use.
nice!

title: Bunnies for Hire
description: Now that you've met Daniel Dukke and Ferdinand Fox, let me introduce you to two of their chief villains--The Hench-Bunnies. This halter-topped and miniskirted pair of miscreants expertly employ all matter of weapons to create mayhem and destruction, sometimes for pay, and sometimes for kicks. Skilled enough to survive multiple encounters with both the Dukke of Death and the Teenage Killing Machine, The Hench Bunnies present a unique threat due to the usually deadly Ferdinand's critical awkwardness around members of the fairer sex. One bat of an eye, and the slayer of 1,000 ninjas is out of the fight. It's tragic really. The Hench Bunnies, as well as Deeds and Ferdy are totally © John H. Staton.

comments.body: What, no prison stripes?
Couldn't find that picture.
Should I fire up my scanner? *evil grin*
Go for it, I'd be curious to see what you have.
I think it's mostly from around the "Game Show" where the turtle was put on the spot naming the color of the bunny's eyes. ("They're black! It's a black and white comic!")
Oh yea, the one hosted be Metrosexual Ferdinand. Hah, good times.
Especially when the bunny (forgot her name) was tied up and kidnapped by the henchbunnies. An early indication of your love of mummification.
I know not of what you speak.
Heh, wrong account....
>.< Oh no, you really need to look up fashion lingo. Neither are in halter tops and only one is in a mini skirt Haha, besides that! I want to see these two colored!

Don't I get any credit for being able to distinguish a M1915 Vickers from an M1910 Maxim? I'll look into halter tops, (Big Chore that.) but how are the both not wearing miniskirts? Their hem lines are well above their knees. What am I missing? You too deserve a sketch for nailing me on this technical flub. Name your character.

Of course you get credit for that But it's a wonderful thing to master all the elements, isn't it? (not that I can tell a Magnum from an airsoft gun...) You got me on one of the hem lines. A miniskirt can only be a miniskirt if it is separate from the top. So the bunny in the front is wearing a miniskirt, but the bunny in the back is wearing a dress, so it would simply be a lowback mini. So I don't know if I deserve a sketch still or not haha! But I do love your artwork, so a sketch of *me* would be wonderful! (Am I egotistically or just not creative? Possibly a bit of both!)

A sketch of you eh? That quite a bold thing to propose after all this talk of halter tops and miniskirts. (Or "butt tourniguets", as I occasionally like to call them.) I find myself tempted to use this as an opportunity to correct my previous fashion faux pas. You'd get a machine gun too, of course.

Oh! Oh! Oh! How about this gun? [link]istol&toggle=tp&breadcrumbseries=LF1 HAHA. When I first saw this gun my brother in law commented on how badass it was, then I told him "You're kidding right? It looks like something a pimp white suite wearing black guy would carry around while getting his nails done" and I've been attached to it ever since And as for clothing...considering what I'm KNOWN for wearing...I don't think it could be that bad. [link] [link] [link] [link] [link] [link] [link] I'd say those say it all 7,000 words in fact.

Could you try sending the link for the gun again? When I clicked on it, I got a site, but no picture of any specific firearm came up, and I must confess your description has certainly whetted my interest. When I look at your message here I see an emoticon and some gibberish immediately following the link. I figure that some DA protocol probably confused part of your original link. Very curious to see your other links.
Hmmm lets try a few links. 1. [link] it's the 100 .40 S&W PISTOL WITH GOLD ACCENTS AND MOTHER OF PEARL GRIPS [link]istol&toggle=tp&breadcrumbseries=LF1 [link] ONE of these should work o.0

Hee hee hee. Pimped out thought this may be, this is actually a fairly common gun. Or at the very least, a replica of a popular firearm. This is a Taurus PT92, which, despite a few minor details, is pretty much a licensed reproduction of the infamous Beretta Model 92. [link] The Model 92 was Bruce Willis' sidearm in "Die Hard" [link] (Remember the climactic scene where he taped a pistol to his back? That was a '92.) It was also Mel Gibson's sidearm in Lethal Weapon [link] , and has been the standard issue sidearm for soldiers in the United States Army, Airforce, and Marine Corps since 1985, under the designation M9. [link] You sure you wouldn't want something more exotic? Beretta did make a machine-gun version of the Model 92, called the 93R [link] . Looks a little bit like Megatron and "pimping it out" would be a simple matter of photoshop. Just a thought.

I more or less just meant the tacky pearl and gold for the aesthetics of the gun. I've never been a fan of the "big" guns, despite me always taking the machine guns in Resident Evil. I'd prefer a drop leg holster with two beretta's haha

Do you mean "big" as in "physically large firearm", or do you mean "big" as in "widely used"? Oh well, no big deal, I see here that the overall "pimpage" is the main factor. A drop leg rig is a good suggestion, as it would go well with a nice, shiny butt tourniquet ; I think a shoulder rig would also be good, cause it would show off yer--er--halter top! (Don't worry, I'll do my fashion research this time.)

Big as in Physically large I have very bad tendinitis, too much weight on my hands/arms/wrists and I wouldn't be able to lift it after a few minutes Don't know how well a shoulder rig would work with a halter top, it would probably pull it down. (No funny stuff! haha)
On a side note, I was actually dreaming last night that I was drawing a fan art of your bunnies. I like the pose my sleepy brain came up with so I might give it a shot later haha!
Let me know when you post it.
jeez now I have to get on it >.<

Just need to take off the coat. Like the glasses. They make you look all crazy.

Is that a Maxim or a Vickers?

Its a M1915 Vickers. It's been a while since I researched the references for this drawing, but I think the parallel groves along outer housing of the cooling apparatus are one of the details that allows one to better distinguish it from the Maxim. You deserve some kind of reward for even being able to ask that question. How about a pencil sketch--Name your character.

That's kind of you, thank you. Hmm, I'm a fan of the Wolfenstein games, so mayhaps you could do a pic of one of the enemies from Return to Castle Wolfenstein; The Venom Chaingun Trooper, standing at rest with the weapon hanging at his side?

Actually I was expecting something more along the lines of Wonder Woman, or Power Girl. Or Wonder Woman pinning Power Girl in a wrestling match--that sort of thing. I can do this Venom Guy from this game I've never heard of certainly, but as you can probably guess, I'm going to really need some references to do it right. If you have links to any jpegs, feel free to send me a note.
So again you said you did a book with these characters? I'd love to see it.

Just send me your address in a note, and I'll fire you off a copy. Personally, I think the humor still holds up, (Although, admittedly, I'm not the most objective source.) but I should warn you in advance that I was in the middle of learning how to draw during the early parts of the book. I'll be curious to see what you think of it.

title: The Original Dukke of Death
description: Get ready for a trip down memory lane. This positively archeological Berol Prismacolor™ marker illustration depicts my first ever original characters in all their glory. Holding the Desert Eagle, sporting the double-breasted Armani suit, and posing in front of the red Lamboghini Countach, is none other than Daniel Dukke ( Pronounced "duck" . As you can no doubt guess, this character is the inspiration for my bizarre screen name. ) Daniel Dukke is an independently wealthy, sophisticated jet-setter who travels the world finding evil where he can, and extinguishing its practitioners in the simultaneously coolest and grisliest ways possible. I never decided whether or not he was a plain-clothes policeman, an undercover detective, a private investigator, a secret agent, or simply an overdressed vigilante, although at one point or another during his existence, he's exhibited signs of being all of the above. In all honesty, I really didn't care about such particulars so long as the criminal body count was sufficiently high. As I look at this picture now, the profound impact of the television program Miami Vice™ upon my impressionable young psyche, becomes readily apparent. As I recall, I even owned one of those ridiculous Don Johnson "Pastel Ice Cream Man" suits back when the show aired. (I hope to God no one ever photographed me in that get up. ) Looming large behind Mr. Dukke, is his equally bad ass1 side-kick, Ferdinand Fox, an aspiring crime-fighter who learned martial arts by watching the films of L. Bruce Preslei2 set on fast forward! Acquiring preternatural fighting skills in this manner, the newly minted Teenage Killing Machine sought out the world's foremost slayer of evil-doers, Mr. Dukke, to learn from him how to become a great adventurer. When he thinks of it, Daniel will occasionally say something wise and "mentory" to Ferdinand, but for the most part, he just points gifted fighter at the inevitable, incoming cloud of blood-thirsty Ninja Cats and lets him go wild. Foxes, as it turns out, make excellent human shields. During my college days, I inflicted the adventures of these characters upon the hapless University of Maryland campus. I probably have a few copies of the collection I made still gathering dust in my attic. Every now and again, I consider having DD & FF take place in the same "nobody looks twice at a five-foot-two-inch mosuegirl" universe as Most Dangerous Bookstore, and having Daniel be a former associate/ex-boyfriend of Cassandra Cat. In this manner I could explain the bookstore cat's uncanny acumen with dispatching ninja-pirate-zombies. 1.) I made up these characters when I was eight, so it's quite a rush to be able to use the phrase "bad ass" without fear of reprisal. 2.)FYI, L. Bruce Preslei was a recurring character in the strip. He was a rock star who acted in a host of mediocre kung-fu/musicals before disappearing mysteriously in 1975. He is also © John Staton, as are Daniel Dukke, and Ferdinand Fox.

comments.body: Oooh, there was a collection? I have a few of the strips laying around from when my sister went to UMD. I simply HAD to save the ones with the Henchbunnies. I seem to recall Preslei made an appearance and caught a mouthful of exploding bullets.

Yea there was/is a collection. Heh, I totally wasn't expecting to hear any interest at this point. I was all braced to hear how rickety, antiquated, and 80's everything looks here. The next battery of commets will no doubt be along those lines, so for now its good to hear from someone who remembers. Send me a note, let's see what we can do about getting you a copy or two.
Well, yeah, I was kinda saving it as blackmail material. *grin* I'll send you a note.

The only thing you could possibly blackmail me with would be pictures of me in my old white "Sonny Crockett" suit, complete with sockless deck shoes. Actually, there are probably a great many things one could blackmail me with, but that one sticks out in my mind. Look forward to your note.

You say that now... Maybe someday I'll put up my best Cosplay pic. I did Russ Post from Those Annoying Post Bros, a comic that I was apparently the only person to read. Only the second time in my life that I shaved my mustache.
"Human" shield? Looks more like a walking duck man shield. Love this drawing

I'm actually quite tickled that you like this antiquated drawing. I don't know why, but I really appreciate it. Thanks. I occasionally think about resurrecting these characters; of course with hilariously superficial changes like giving Daniel a red Ford GT and getting rid of Ferdinand's parachute pants. It's fun to think I might have one or two more stories left in me featuring these two. "Human" shield is just a generic term. it would still be Ferdinand charging into the face of all the dangerous, flying objects. Remember Mr. Dukke, the duck, is the smart one.
No! You MUST keep the parachute pants! *cough* sorry, I have a not so hidden love for parachute pants You need to carry on with all of your stories, time to start producing them!

You make a great point. It IS high time I do something with all this crap I have sparking between my ears. I guess that's why I appreciate DA so much. Putting out concepts before potential readers in this manner helps convince me they won't be blown off as the ravings of a old nut. But still, I do need to generate the nerve to expose my work to a larger forum. On another matter. I was just remembering how much research I had to do to get the right views of a Countach. When I was mentioning moments ago about giving "Deeds" a new GT, I just "google imaged" more views than my last trip to the library yeilded of Lamborghinis! Times they are a changin.'
