In this particular future genetic research of this kind is in violation of American law, as it is frowned upon by the religious right. However, after other nations were able to establish a permanent presence on the moon, and repulse America's initial attempts to enforce it's Lunar Territorial Claim (i.e. Neil Armstrong's flag.) through the use of Genetically Augmented Combat Astronauts; The US invested in a big (Albeit secret.) way into catching up in this type of research. No one is even supposed to know Sabrina and Tabitha exist until the foreigners have been kicked off the moon.

Aha okay. Ah yeah, good point (the whole "we are the way we are, made by god and we have to stay like this" thing). Was just asking cause for some reason her design kinda reminded me of the Sisters of Battle. Aha, now that sounds cool
Well they do all have white hair, but the sculptors could never really get a lot of sex appeal out of those tiny grimacing faces. Glad you like the sound of the concept.
Yeah, lol yeah that's very true. Models on that scale do have problems with achieving sex appeal. Heh yeah, what's not to like about it ^^

I like your concept here, where the mech is attached to a giant frame that grants it flight (and speeds faster than what it can achieve by itself if already can fly on its own). Reminds me of that 70's anime UFO Robot Grandizer.

Well how do you like that. Not being familiar with Grandizer, I had to look it up on old faithful Wikipedia. While the mecha are radically different, the composition of the poster with the profile of a mecha head is uncannily reminiscent of my illustration. Boy there really is nothing hew under the sun.

Ah, don't you worry about it, I remember making a knight's face out of that paper "prediction" game/toy, and was totally shocked when I first saw the old school anime Space Knight Tekkaman; looked exactly like Tekkaman's head! My conspiracy theory is that ideas and dreams are shared by all people because said ideas and dreams all exist in another dimension which our subconscious can somehow tap into. So it's not unusual for someone to come up with something and see that someone else already published/manufactured it. It's just a matter of time and resources to see who gets to the said idea first and makes something about it. ^_^

It's funny that you should say that, because your conspiracy theory sounds a little like Carl Gustav Jung's theory of the "Collective Unconscious". The idea was floating around out there. In this one case, a pioneering Swiss psychologist stumbled upon it first. The stinkin' bum.

How old is this, btw? It's just that the style is totally different from your current stuff... Not much differentiation between foreground and background (inking issue, as you say) but otherwise not so bad... In fact, I kinda like the composition! Is the mech in the front in mid-transformation?

Good observation sir. This piece is so old, I don't exactly remember how old, but it is at least 10 years old! And you're very keen in pointing out the lack of differentiation between foreground and background here. I've probably mentioned before that I had to have that deficiency pointed out to me by another artist. Before then, I thought everything was about how much detail one could pack into each square inch of drawing. (One of the many pitfalls of learning how to draw in the 1990's.) About the mecha. That's it's final form actually, and it's perfectly justified to think it's in mid-transition between two forms as this particular design didn't really have a governing theme. I wanted the mechs to look roughly humanoid with wings that would serve as hard points for missiles and bombs. In this version I had the wings attach to the legs which looks silly to me now. In the colored pencil drawing, [link] I had the wings in a folding assembly that deployed from the humanoid mechas back. In that version, I was trying to make American combat mecha look more or less like caped superheroes, but the folded wings made them look more like angels instead, which I'm not sure is a bad thing.

Well, it looks great! I suppose when you draw technical stuff like mecha and planes, detail is inevitable... You must have an excellent reference library because your tech is very believable/realistic... The fourth wall remains unbreached!

"Reference Library" I like the sound of it. I have a politically minded room mate who calls it "Mission Creep" a term Colin Powel coined for an unintended spreading out of resources. I have a female friend who calls it "a big giant mess." And a fellow cartoonist friend of mine calls it "John's machine-porn collection." "Reference Library" has a much less pajorative ring.
Nice work here.
Thank you.
You're welcome.
Looks great!! I've been trying and failing to draw more robots, machines and weapons. have any tips?

I hope you wont yell at me for saying so, but after reviewing you art, I first want to give you some help with backgrounds. I don't see a lot of them in your gallery. That may be because you have the skills to address them and simply haven't found the need to draw them, but it's been my personal experience that a young aspiring artist avoids perspective because he/she isn't comfortable with it. I recommend you get a copy of How To Draw Manga Volume 29: Putting Things In Perspective. Really any book of linear perspective will help you, but HTDM29 is intended for readers of various ages and levels of expertise, so it explains its techniques clearly, in language that's easy to understand. It's full of good tips and easy ways to achieve breathtaking and complex environmental effects. Check it out. Now about those robots. I've been drawing machines and robots since I was a kid, so it's hard for me to explain exactly how. I can recommend that you get pictures of robots and machines--cars, airplanes, motorcycles--and draw them so you get accustomed to how mechanical devices are laid out. Since I have my other window opn to Amazon.com, I can also make you another book recommendation, Draw Manga Volume 12: Giant Robots. Now I haven't read this one, so I can't vouch for it personally, but since its from the same publisher as Putting This in Perspective, I'm sure it will have some good tips. Hope that helps.

Thanks, it really does help alot! About backgrounds I know that I should be drawing more of them and during this past summer I have though nothing to be too proud of, and when I have a picture in mind that needs some sort machine I copy a picture off the internet, which also helps get the fell of it. I'll check out the books for sure. Tanks again.
I still have an installment of night of 100 stars, as well as the prints I bought from you at Anime east(Damn, I'm old!). As with Mme America, It'd be a diggable concept if it were further developed.

It's funny. The reason I gave for there being one hundred stars on the American flag, in the future depicted in this tale, is that a terrorist attack on US soil would spark a war that would have the US taking over nations in the middle east. In my notes I was fond of one sentence, "And the morality of the period would be hotly debated over Big Mac's at the McDonald's adjacent to the the University of Bagdad." A friend of mine I read my notes too laughed and went on at length about how unrealistic that part of the premise was. I wonder if he's on Facebook?

Fascinating.You were and continue to be an excellent hand at story concepts in particular, speculative fiction which in this bankrupt culture of laser-firing nipples with finger-activated cervical firing mechanisms, is a dying art esthetically as well as intellectually.
Don't forget the ninjas. The endless, overwhelming glut of effin' ninjas.

Yeaassss...! bloody ninjas! sprouting like chancres on hooker's client list. An endless drone of unimaginative,yak of black pajamas and ginsus. Now everything's zombies and vampires. Perhaps twilight of the zombie ninja hogwart's assault?

"like chancres on hooker's client list" Heh, nice simile. But yes, for practitioners of a centuries old tradition of stealth, ninja's are criminally over-exposed. To be honest, they sour my enjoyment of GI Joe. Just when you think you're going to see some decent team work, or the development of some other character. any other character, suddenly Snake Eyes shows up, and has an three issue long sword dance with Storm Shadow. Frankly I think those two belong in another comic entirely. Give me my tank battles!!

Actually, this is part and parcel as to why I only read what comes out of Europe and Japan.The dissolution of our roots and the loss of our mythology has produced an insipid fare of re-fried tropes and idioms further emasculated by political correctness, pop psychobabble and trial lawyers.

title: Colonial Bomber
description: One thing that always bothered me about Battlestar Galactica was the lack of variety in the spacecraft deployed by Colonial Forces. I mean your average Supercarrier has fighter aircraft, interceptor/fleet defense aircraft, attack/ bomber aircraft, close air support aircraft, electronic warfare aircraft, anti-submarine vehicles, rescue helicopters, tankers, reconnaissance aircraft, and cargo planes to bring in the mail. (And I'm sure I'm missing a few. ) While the mighty Galactica with a minimum of four times the carrying capacity of a Nimitz Class Carrier has only Vipers and Shuttles. That in mind, I tried to design a Colonial Bomber, a larger fighting craft with longer range, and the ability to deliver more ordinance onto Cylon targets. Not as nimble as Vipers these would be used primarily for attack missions against hardened targets like planetary encampments and Base Stars. Vipers would probably still have to be used as escort on such attacks, but it would still amount to a better situation than wasting a Battlestar in direct confrontation. I have no idea what snake or reptile to name this after.

comments.body: Nothing like getting geeked out before an epic episode of BSG tonight. For some reason, this craft looks a bit similar to the ones in the game Homeworld 2.

Keep in mind that I own no gaming systems. (Well that's not entirely true, I do own a joystick, that I bought at CVS that has 8 or so Atari 2600 games on it.) So any clarification you can provide would be greatly appreciated.
Homeworld 2 was a PC RTS developed by Relic Entertainment. Here is some info [link] about it. One of the factions had a spacecraft similar to that.
Wow, there are a lot of games out there.
Awesome, totally awesome How about calling it a Monitor?
Is that a type of snake or reptile? (Heh, I'll bet that's what Cobra Commander always asks when he's presented with a new gadget.)
A monitor is a type of lizard, think Komodo Dragon

Good enough, although it sounds more appropriate a name for an electronic countermeasure/warfare craft like the Raptor on the new show. If I had my druthers, I'd name the new BGC craft a "Monitor" and call my design "Raptor." Hmmm, "Dragon" sounds promising though.
Aha okay. Heh, Dragon works Yeah Monitor probably fits something bigger and slower, but infinitely more powerful (the Komodo being the biggest lizard alive today and all). Happy to help

Even then, "Komodo" seems a better name. I know America's first iron-clad warship was named Monitor and, as such, the name is steeped in heritage and tradition, but when I hear the name "Monitor" I can't help but think of what I'm staring into as I type.
Yeah true, has a powerful sound Yep, it was indeed. First ship in the world equipped with a turret. LOL yeah that's very true.

How about, Mamba? That sounds dangerous yet sleek. Or perhaps Adder? Depending on how big it is, you can go with Anaconda too. Some other neat snake names: Asp Boa Copperhead Cottonmouth Diamondback Keelback Moccasin Python Rattler Sidewinder Stiletto
Cool names, Of course Rattler's taken. Hmmm. I like the sound of Python. Colonial Python it is. Mamba could be a stealth/recon craft.

The Colonial might be a multiuse ship that can do bombing and or anti-ship missions. Though, I'm no BSG fan, I like rethinking/thinking of these sort of (plot?)holes. I do see in many episodes that if the FTL is hit and many fighters launch their missiles at vulnerable areas(think sub-light engines that seem to use a type of fuel) and or support from say, Raptors equipped as 'missile trucks' mounting many more than the average Viper has. This tactic seems to have worked against both the Resurrection ship and the Resurrection hub as well. It seems fighters in BSG are there in numbers to screen and or target enemy ships for other bigger ships to destroy(gunstars?), we do see the newer Battlestar Pegasus destroy a Base Star with its forward cannons. Also during this second cylon war there was a 'stealth' fighter built, the "Blackbird" which was invisible to DARDIS. It seems the fighter is/was King in the Colonial Fleet and specialization was left to the bigger ships.

I wish that the actual show thought more along these lines nowadays. I know that the present writing staff wants to explore Cylon Metaphysics, but I honestly don't think that such literary indulgences would preclude the interjection of logical tactical thought.

Seems standard TV show/Sci-Fi show logic nowadays. I mean: 1.)First have villain that seems impossible to beat(Wraith, Cylons, Xindi, and The Empire). 2.)Then find ways to win fights against them... 3.)...leading to actually wining big battles against 'The Enemy'. 4.)Then, in order to make it seem easier introduce a counter faction in opposition to 'The Enemy' more than likely from within their mist. 5.)Introduce more sides of either 'The Enemy' or the 'Good Guys' be them for good or not(like Cylon Metaphysics, Xindi Factions, Corruption, etc...). 6.)Above all else: FORGET ABOUT "REALITY", "PHYSICS", Previous Storylines/Show History, and or LOGIC in general.... Have I forgotten anything(on purpose, anyways)?

You pretty much covered it all. Thats what they do when the are contracted to mek endless shows with more plot holes than episodes -_- I actually like short and well-made tv shows! Look to avatar: the last airbender! That was an awesome show!

Just so you know, I was referring to the original Battlestar Galactica, in which the eponymous ship was the bleeding edge flagship of the Colonial Fleet, and supposedly armed with the best weapons and equipment the colonies had to offer. I deliberately used more angular lines and a less swept wing to suggest the look of the more classic incarnation of the show. But since you bring up the new Galactica, I find their lack of technical diversity equally frustrating. Galactica in the updated continuity was being decommissioned, but it was still conducting flight operations, and maintained a squadron of Viper Mark VII's and Raptors. The outdated fighters were museum pieces, pressed into service only when Galactica's original flight of Mark VII's fell victim to the Cylon virus. The ship had to improvise at the opening of the war, but is did originally carry first rate fighters, and would have until the moment of it's decommissioning. Two types of spacecraft simply weren't enough. But even if we grant that Galactica was so minimally equipped, that still does not explain why Pegasus which was a bleeding edge, top of the line flagship, with facilities onboard to make new spacecraft only had Vipers and Raptors. I appreciate your suggestion that the Mark VII was an attempt to deepen the pool of spacecraft Colonial planners could draw from, but since in the continuity, they replaced to obsolete Mark II's by five full generations, we were still being asked to believe that the Colonial Fleet, with 120 Battlestars at the height of its strength before the attack, relied on two types of fighting spacecraft, those being the Viper and the Raptor. With the first Cylon War causing humanity to distance itself from the technical sophistication that would breed a single do-all spacecraft, it becomes more logical than ever that Battlestars would boast multitudes of specialized spacecraft for different mission profiles. However, in the end, I am forced to concede that a proper depiction of a military build-up is precluded in both shows by the frustrating realities of television production.

lol... the entire time I was reading this I was thinking your last comment... Every sci-fi show out there has this problem. There is a clear disconnect between what SHOULD be depicted as the logical reality... and what CAN be depicted due to television or film production...

The effects, which are the first televised use of CGI, are dated, but they are beautifully thought out, and the space battles are well staged. Beyond that, the writing of the show is VASTLY superior to updated Galactica. B5 was a well planned five year story arc, whose end was devised before the first scene was filmed. (In B5, when someone says they have a plan--they actually have a plan!!!!) In contrast, Galactica was brilliant for one and one half seasons, but for the remainder of is run, it became increasingly obvious that they were making things up along the way.

title: Ifurita in Marker
description: As the deviantion title suggests, a Berol Prismacolor Marker drawing of Ifurita, my absolutely favoritest thermonuclear blast shooting wind-up goddess from back in the day. Back when I drew this, I pretty much used markers for everything. I probably would've brushed my teeth with them if could figure out how. Oh, the legions of brain cells I willingly sacrificed to their pungent fumes. Good times. Since my discovery of photoshop however, I'm afraid I've abandoned my beloved tools like a starter wife after her first crow's foot. They sit now, in my studio inside a once fancy, multi-shelved cart on casters that now serves as a dusty pedestal to an old MoBat toy. As a mater of fact, I threw in a photoshop gradiant to this piece because I didn't like the spartan white background it had initially. One last insult I guess. Maybe one day, for old time's sake, I'll do another marker picture. 'Course by now, my old pens are probably bone dry.

A fallen god of markers But reborn as a photoshop god ^_^

The El Hazard OVA was that exceedingly rare anime whose conclusion actually provided closure and, more importantly--made sense!!! I consider this quite an achievement when you consider that it had an enigmatic, über-powerful, glowing goddess, and time travel. Either one of these two elements usually spells total disaster for the plot cohesion of a Japanese cartoon. The fact that El Hazard avoided this pitfall with both can almost be considered historic. But then they had to ruin it by continuing it.

Suddenly! In 18th century industrial revolution London! I was disappointed. Why not continue in the first gleaming arabian realm? :< But the OVA has been inspiring me since. I wish there was a remake. Just like Top O Nerae
this one is unique

title: DMV: Ka Bume
description: Pencils for a comic page: Heroic meter maid AJ, and her significantly less than heroic partner Drew are narrowly missed by a stray ray blast. Somewhere, there is a Pool Party* going down. AJ of course, rushes o the scene. An unenthusiastic Drew is quick to point out that they are heading toward the source of a 100mm H.O.G.** blast. AJ chides, "What do you recommend we do, partner? Call the police?" AJ accelerates away, leaving Drew to fume. And plot his passive/aggressive revenge. *Pool Party: A violent armed dispute between rival car pools, usually over heavily contested parking lot territory. *Heavy Optic Gun. Compact megawatt yield laser, roughly the size of a contemporary squad automatic weapon, serving a similar function, only possessing far greater endurance and anti-material capabilty than it's slug-throwing predecessor. I chose to give it a 100mm emiter lens so I could humorously call it a "Decimater." but since I live in a country that couldn't give a rat's ass about the metric system, nobody ever got the joke.

comments.body: wow, thats one hefty punishment for not coining up the meter.
eheh. That's not quite what's going on here. Most likely, my visual storyboarding is at fault. Apologies.
well thats the first impression i got

Well, now that you've read the artist comments, do you have any suggestions on how I could make the pictures match the events I've written? Or at least how to make it clear that the meter maids are not the ones responsible for the explosion? Please don't say captions. This is a storyboarding exercise. The visuals should convey the message. Besides, it's good practice.

hemm, it looks like them racing for leadership or teritory(you explain that). of course Kosuke Fujishima did the same story in ah my goddess. i can see the resemblance i know im still new at this, but you should put arrows showing the action and movements if its a storyboard. thats how all storyboard artist do it. Of course, this is up to the artist of course. Just something i learn from class. no biggie.

Storyboarding and cartooning have a great many similarities, but there are differences. Storyboards can use arrows because they are more less blueprints for a director or cinematographer who needs directions regarding movement and placement of his camera. A comic page is supposed to lead the eye of the reader solely with its arrangement of images with as little aid from captions and symbols as possible. The cartoonist is supposed to be invisble when the reader is reading, and arrow call attention to his presence.(At least that what Will Eisner* says. ) There are many, many things that your storyboarding classes will give you that will be a great aid to you in cartooning. Trust me, it's been my experience that directional arrows are not one of them. So I guess, I'm asking how can I best rearrange my panels, or the images within them to rescue this trainwreck of a page. (Panel borders probably would have helped my cause immensely.) Sorry if I sound like a crumugian, I do appreciate the spirit in which your suggestion is offered, and hope you feel free to offer more. *Author of The Spirit and the cartoonist who coined the phrase Sequential Art.
i think the set up is ok, besides that it looks combined.
Heh, panel borders it is then.
Decimater, that's a good one.

Thanks guy. *Checks nationality* Ah, Austrailia! No wonder you actually get metric humor. I really need to travel. I'm tired of no one laughing when I say things like "Real American British Engineering Units!" Someone besides me has got to think this stuff is funny.
Real American British Engineering Units, definitely remembering that one since my dad can do measurements in Imperial and Metric so that might get a laugh out of him.
Nice.
I can actually tell where that "round" came from. I think you should darken or thicken the lines surrounding that shot some more. Or maybe use some Kirby dots­™.
"Kirby Dots" Awesome. Awesome because of the suggestion, and awesome because you said "Kirby Dots."

Kirby dots were revolutionary for their time. I'm pretty sure black and white comics had a hard time trying to come up with unique energy signatures before its introduction, and artists and writers were sick of electricity-based effects; it was done to death since the inception science fiction and fantasy genre comics.
Yea, the depth and impact of Kirby's genius upon the comic medium can not be overestimated.

I've always though your storyboarding was excellent John, though in this case the nature of the angled frames makes me want to naturally read it backwards, like a manga. Since it's my nature to read top to bottom, the higher frames logically come before the lower ones, making the upper right frame the first one. It seems like Drew gets mad at what AJ says in the middle panel and then AJ scoots off past him. Where what's really going on is she goes past and *then* Drew gets mad. The last frame in the sequence being higher than the former is what throws it off. If the entire thing was angled the other direction it would work perfectly. Drew's arm in the middle panel runs into AJ's hand in the final, which is a little confusing to the viewer. It looks like Drew has a strangely deformed arm instead of them being separate panels altogether. You do paneling leagues ahead of anything I could ever manage, but since you seem like you're open to feedback on this one, I decided to give some.
Hmmm, A slant going downwards from left to right would feel more natural to the English reading eye. This is a very insightful observation. Thanks.
Actually, I just noticed something. When did Drew get a fu man chu mustache? I don't recall seeing him with that in any of the other drawings of DMV.

Believe it or not, I orignally designed Drew with a fu man chu 'stache. I was going for a whimsical Erol Flynn look, until I realized that Flynn was actually cool, something that AJ's hapless sidekick only achieves on extremely rare instances. So as time wore on (And I learned to properly compose panels.) I decided to have him be clean shaven. Actually, for a while, I've also been weighing the option of replacing him with a female, since as he stands, I fear Drew might be a little too reminiscent of another bumbling male sidekick. Just a thought

Well, having him be male gives you more opportunities to play out the usual love interest angle with his partner which can make the story more interesting. If Drew was female instead, that would be a lot more fun in the fanservice dept, but you'd lose any possible gender based misunderstandings or character flaws. Needing to look macho, getting embarrassed around sex talk, inappropriate comments around mixed company, all sorts of differences they could have. I think without any real inkling of a personal plot for AJ, it's hard to tell which would be better. You have a good concept and a setting, but no visible story so far. Just a world with lot of potential to make stories within.
Hmmm, These are excellent comments. I have much to consider here.

title: Cho Mama
description: Brandy Carter* from Liberty Meadows* done in my faltering approximation of a manga style. She is posing here on a Motoslave from Bubblegum Crisis. (Hmmmm. the fifth Knight Sabre?) I was always impressed by the cleverness inherent in Frank's granting of Brandy's last name. Everybody already knows she is based on the legendary Lynda Carter, but the surname "Carter" also allows him to pay homage to Edgar Rice Burrows by simply naming her father John. I always thought that was neat. But then I've always been easy to impress. Inks by Frank Cho. *Brandy Carter and Liberty Meadows are © F. Duk Cho.

comments.body: oh i saw this in sketch mag way back when, neet-o
Hah! So you're the one who read that article.
great as always...^^ Who is Linda Carter...?

Sorry my friend. My first two responses to this question where laden with sarcasm at your expense. I sometimes forget that cultural phenoms we take for granted in the US aren't always circulated worldwide. Lynda Carter is an incredibley beautiful television actress who played Wonder Woman in a phenomenally popular television series that aired from 1975 to 1979. [link] Beyond filling out the costume well Lynda Carter had a charismatic screen presence that exemplified all aspects of the amazing amazon's character so perfectly that she is strongly associated to the character to this day. Any boy in the United States alive between 1975 and 1979 worships Lynda Carter. Heh, I remember after enthusiastically recounting a episode to a friend on the playground unfortunate enough to have missed it, a nearby girl, in an obvious fit of jealously over our doting on Ms. Carter sniped, "Why don't you just marry Wonder Woman?" I don't think the fact that my friend and I soon engaged into a dusty, wrestly kid-fight over who gets to marry Wonder Woman did anything to assuage her jealousy. Since here, Wonder Woman's iconic status is far, FAR greater than that of Storm's, I assumed that you would be familiar with the character, her history, and her incarnations in the various media, such as the famed television show. This in mind, I assumed that you were joking with your question, and responded in kind. Sorry about that. I also remembered that you are from Germany. It is entirely possible that the show may not even have aired in your country, since the first season took place during WWII, and Ms. Carter spent much of her time stage-punching bad Hollywood actors who where engaging in even worse negative German stereotypes. During the first half of the second season, the show switched its setting to present day, (Taking full advantage of the character's immortality to save the production costs of recreating a bygone era.) however, I doubt her battles with Neo-Nazis and a Hitler clone would have made her any more a desirable option for modern German broadcasting. Excuse my insensitivity, and I hope this all answers your question.

Yes it does... I really think I should have red the name again! I didn'tw nat to sound like a fan geek.. I really didn't know who she is! Sry That is soo cool of you, telling me who she was and show me, how awesome she was!!! I mean, in such a terrible time like the second WW... to fight against german stereotypes... :] Makes me like her a lot! Even NOWADAYS... I sometimes get hate from strangers just because I am german... Even if I am not born in germany Steroetypes are stupid... Again: It was just a question! Maybe I sounded a bit geeky...

No worries. And never apologize for geekiness. Now I do feel I should clarify one thing. Lynda Carter didn't "fight" stereotypes like an activist, promoting a positive image of Germans, or anything like that. She played opposite people portraying stereotypical characters, and her character was locked in mortal combat with theirs. So when I say she "fought stereotypes", I mean she wore an American flag leotard with a golden eagle for a bra and pretended beat the stuffing out of wicked trolls in SS uniforms who spoke English with crummy accents. Sadly, if anything, the Wonder Woman show may well have reinforced the very stereotypes you dislike. This could be why the show didn't air in your country. Sorry, I'd like you to like Lynda Carter as much as me and my friends, but I don't feel right having you assign her a merit she didn't possess. I'm not saying she's even remotely a Teutophobe, but neither was she an activist. She was a competent actress who was built like a brick ship-house, and that is primarily why we love her so.

title: More American Streamers
description: Another maker colored group shot of the winsome super soldiers I created. This was actually created earlier than the picture I posted previously. The design for Air Force Girl here was, I felt, too similar to AJ from my DMV concept. I don't know why I should concern myself with the similarities between two characters of my own creation, particularly when neither has seen the light of day publicationwise; but I decided to design Air Force Girl anyway to how she appears in the aforementioned previous post. The big rockety thing she's strapped into is an Orbital Rig, allowing her to do anti-satellite warfare. It was inspired more that a little by some of Ace McCloud's gear from the cartoon Centurians. (Man the 1980's truly were the golden age of cartoons where stuff blowed up.)

comments.body: Its neat seeing your older works. Would you ever redraw them now?

Probably. It would be fun to revisit some of these concepts. Perhaps even redesign some elements. Marine Girl for instance, I was considering dressing in a body-hugging, cropped-top version of USMC's trademark dress blues. Although that probably would make her look too much like one of the Strike Witches, despite having human legs. And without all the decorative ribbons flowing from their costumes, there would be little point in calling them Streamers. Still, the idea of super-heroines going toe-to-toe with heavy Warsaw Pact arms and equipment, is simply to appealing for me to indefinitely abandon. It would be fun to at least generate a pin-up or two in my contemporary style.
Awesome.
It's always nice to hear from folks.
I love how old school this looks X3

There's that phrase again. Actually its a fairly old drawing, so its "Old schoolness" is the product of the "Old School" anime that inspired it. Although people seem to enjoy the accidental retro-osity of the picture. I'll definitely have to draw these characters again soon, although I wonder how much of the old school look I'll be able to recreate.

Holy memory lane,Bat-dude! This was the first piece I'd ever seen of yours! The effect it had on my own stuff was nothing short of a personal epiphany! It remains one of my all time faves from your body of work.

Wow, I'm honored to learn that this picture had sucn an effect on another artist. Whenever I look at this picture, I want to do more stuff with the Streamers, and one day, I definitely will. For now I want to focus on America Jones. Coming up with ideas has never been a problem for me. I suppose I should consider myself lucky in that respect. My challenge has always been focusing on one thing.

title: Ka POW
description: A little fan-boy wish fulfillment from one of my favorite old animes. To those of you who recall Neon Genesis Evangelion, remember the episode when Shinji got swallowed uo by that Angel that kind of looked like a Rover?* Remember the scene when Asuka was talkin' all kinds of heartless smack about Shinji, as he suffered in the bottomless sea of who knows where? Remember when an obviously pissed Rei got up in her face? This is what I really would like to have seen happen next! Anyway, this is yet another ancient marker drawing that I unearthed. Please excuse the overall cringeworthiness of this old piece. As an anime fan, as well as the artist responsible, I kind of felt a duty to post it. * Prisoner reference. R.I.P. Patrick McGoohan.

comments.body: Pwnage! Absolute pure pwnage!!

LOL not overly familiar with the Anime - found it, sadly like most series, too slow and filled with a few too many biblical references. That's pry just me . But do know a bit about their history so great to see Rei give Asuka what she deserves

I enjoyed NGE because its biblical refences weren't gratuitous as was the case in the multitudes of shameless imitators it would spawn. They were integral to the story, and a delight for anyone whose ever had to study the book of Revelations. However, if such references aren't your cup of tea on principle, I can see where NGE wouldn't be your thing. Not everything is for everyone. For instance, gothic vampire anime just turn me off. It would have to be particularly well done for to get me to endure yet another tale of tortured soul Fabio in a poofy pirate shirt.

Aha, okay. Never really watched it, as much for the slowness of the show as the references. Well yeah I think for me it's often a case of principle. Each to their own. Yeah I can totally understand that, the conflicted Vampire theme has been done not quote to death but certainly a lot.

Yea, NGE is not without its pacing problems. Especially at the end. Structurally Evangellion is patterned after Patrick McGoohan's famed 1960's psychedelic adventure show The Prisoner. There are even many references sprinkled throughout the anime: such as the punching of Shinji Ikari's ID card in exactly the same manner as McGoohan's character's in the opening of the Prisoner. Both show's also follow a pattern of a decreasingly linear narrative in which the story literally flies apart into a flurry of indecipherable stream of consciousness at the end, featuring, in both cases, scenes of apocalyptic destruction, to the tune of chirpy Beatles' music. The funny thing is that, also in both cases, the writers had to go into hiding when their respective viewerships flew into incandescant rages at the avante guard nature of each conclusion. Considering that he would obviously be aware of the negative reaction to McGoohan's work 30 years earlier, you'd figure Hideki Anno would have known better. Or a least have taken better precautions.

I think it also didn't help that I only sorta looked at NGE when I was in my early to mid teens so I didn't have as much patience as I do now. So it just felt too slow. ...Course I'm sure you can guess why I looked at it at that age Aha I see, well I've never seen that show so I'll have to take your word for it. title: American Streamers Pencils 01
description: I really like the old Mil Mi-24 attack helicopter. Few machines serve better as a visual cue, announcing "Here come the bad guys!" than this ass-ugly, flying crocodile. (I'm actually surprised and disappointed that Hasbro™ never co-opted the design for use in their G.I.Joe™ toy line--It would make a perfect Cobra™ vehicle!) Anyway, the purpose of this sequence was to demonstrate how woefully inadequate conventional weapons are against Tactical Combat Maidens, or Streamers. I believe Marine Girl fatally headbutts the hapless pilot on her way through the fuselage of the doomed chopper. I really need to find that page.

comments.body: OH this is awesome!
Wow that was quick. Thank you very, very much.
May I ask how big the original is?

I drew this small. It was only 6 9/16" x 10" live area. Really, it was meant as more of a layout page. I was going to blow it up later, transfer it to comic art board and add real detail. It's just one of those things I didn't get around to finishing.
I was wondering what your normal size was, thanks

Absolutely awesome work You're quite right about the Mil Mi-24 having a real presence to it. It's something only some aircraft seem to have such a presence about them (F-14, AH-64, B-17, Avro Lancaster). Excellent job in capturing that feel to the helicopter
Man, gotta say the F-14 is THE sexiest fighter ever--period. I'm sure the FA-18 and the F-35 are more technically advanced and all that but man, they just don't look anywhere near as cool.

I totally agree with you on the F-14, there is something very special and appealing to its design and appearance (maybe it's the whole swing wing thing). I think the F-15 runs a close second. Yeah, I don't find the FA-18 (either the original our Airforce operates or the Super Hornet) visually appealing at all. I don't mind the look of the F-35 and F-22, but they definitely don't have a classic or overly appealing look to them either.

It frightens me to say this, but the sexiest looking jets are coming out of Russia. The Su-37 has all the seductive curves that the "paper airplane" stealth vehicles have sacrificed in the name of technical advancement. Makes me sad.

Well yeah the thing is right now America and a lot of countries are totally into this whole "stealth" thing, so the classic lines of a fighter go because they produce a larger radar signature. Personally I think while you can create a stealth jet that's gotta impact on its performance, and once it's in visual range that stealth stuff counts for a lot less... The Russians seem more interested in producing high performance jets, so they're keeping the seductive lines and such.

title: Ms. Claus
description: Yet another old drawing. The real irony is, I found it when putting away the Christmas tree. (That's right, I said "putting away" as in "artificial" Don't you judge me, man! ) And yes, it is too late to put up a holiday themed drawing, but after cutting myself on a splinter getting this out of a cold cramped basement, I'm not in the mood to wait 347 days to post it. Of the drawing itself, its of a young nubile daughter of jolly St. Nick along with high tech reindeer, whose design should be instantly recognizable to Galaxy Rangers fans. Yea, I know I should have finished the thing, but I guess I was crestfallen when I learned Paul Dini had come up with the same idea. (Except for the robot reindeer of course.)

title: The Other Type of Valkyrie
description: Many clicking on this link may well be expecting another drawing of a Valkyrie Fighter Jet from Super Dimension Fortress Macross/Robotech, which I promise you will see more of eventually. This particular valkyrie is Skogul, the love interest in Mike Avon Oemings "Hammer of the Gods," a comic book I used to color waaaay back when all we needed to research Norse mythology was look out our windows. This character is entirely why, while I was flattered to get an actual drawing published, I still lamented getting tasked with the previously posted page containing the snaggled-toothed Frost Giant Matriach.[link] I should have been clearer when I said I wanted to draw one of the female characters.

title: American Streamers
description: I just found this old Prismacolormarker drawing, mere moments ago, and now, due to my having joined the 21st century and acquiring a modern computer, I am able to post it before I think better of it. The concept behind this drawing is one of my more bizarre one's, so bear with me: After watching way to many anime in which a Nimitz Class carrier is sent to Davey Jones' Locker by a teeny bopper in a sailor fuku, I decided to concoct a story in which super-powered sailor girls become the chief expression of strategic military power. American strategic military power. In the back story, the ability to endow young women with extraordinary abilities is pioneered by the Japanese toward the end of the second world war. Due to the shortages of manpower brought about by the conflict, Japanese scientists desperately work to develop a process that would allow it's remaining female population to defend the home island from the anticipated American invasion. In order to halt the American advance, a means has to be developed to allow a single woman to wipe out an armed division of battle hardened troops. In many ways, this would be Japan's own Manhattan project. As it would turn out, the process proves far too expensive and resource intensive for the Japanese to fully execute, and only a blueprint for it is completed by war's end. Along with the coveted secrets of German rocketry and jet technology, The so-called Tokyo Project comes to number among the United State's many scientific spoils of war. After decades of additional research, a process is successfully developed to, for the price of a mere 1.5 billion dollars each, give a young woman1, the ability to fly, hurl tanks, breath in space, punch through steel, and walk on the ocean floor. Basic "Superman" stuff. Inter-service politics being what they are, each branch of the military wanted their own: Clockwise from the top: Air Force Girl (Blue and White uniform.) Flight Ceiling: 95,000 feet. Top speed: Mach 10. Invulnerability to rigors of unassisted supersonic flight. Hands generate gigawatt-class laser bursts. Limited strength. Navy Girl (Gold and White uniform.) Flight Ceiling : 75,000 feet. Top Flight Speed: Mach 6. Maximum Submerged Speed: 50 Knots. Maximum Dive Depth: 30,000 feet. Invulnerable to rigors of flight and deep sea travel. Hands generate megawatt-class laser bursts. Limited strength. Marine Girl (Black and Red uniform. ) Flight Ceiling: 50,000 feet. Top Flight Speed: Mach 2. Maximum Submeged Speed: 30 Knots. Maximum Dive Depth 20,000 feet. 20 Kiloton proof invulnerability. Unable to generate laser bursts( Uses M256 cannon, coverted for use as assault rifle.) 45,000 ton deadlift strength. Army Girl: (Green and Brown uniform) No flight ability. No undersea ability. Maximum Speed Overland: 110mph. 3 Megaton Proof invulnerability. Unable to generate laser bursts. Uses P.O.R.T.I.A.2 grenades (Pictured.) as well as M256 rifle. 88,000 ton deadlift strength. The Jet behind them is the Battle Queen. Essentially, it's Army Girl's ride to the fight. Together these girls are called the American Streamers. And no, there isn't a Coast Guard Girl. I really liked this idea, but I suppose, the publication fo Image's War heroes has rendered all this pretty much moot. But then, my idea is ten years older and a whole lot less cynical, so perhaps there's a place for it afterall. Who knows? 1.) Why women? Because they're more fun to draw. 2.) Posable Ordinance-grade Resin Ten Inch Action-figure.

comments.body: wow, this one is very old
Yea, it's been a while since I did a marker drawing. Any opinion on the concept? Like I said, discussion may well be moot, but I'm still mildly curious to hear what people think.
well, kinda like captain america only female

Yea,and they can lift aircraft carriers. (Well, Army Girl anyway.) One of the things I wanted was to have them used with military tactics. Instead of having Captain America do his Clark Kent impression by wandering through the war in the guise of Private Steve Rogers, until he stumbles upon the Red Skull's latest weapon, Streamers would be deployed in conjunction with other military operations. Troops would be able to call air support from Airforce Girl, or Marine Girl could go behind enemy lines and snipe out entire anti-aircaft emplacements in advance of a bombing attack. A school of thought or combat doctrine would evolve around the deployment of tactical maidens, like dogifighting for military aviation, or blitzkreg tactics for mobile armor. Rather than telling a superhero story masquerading as a war story, I wanted to tell a science fiction story about how a new technology changes the face of combat. So I guess you could say it's like Captain America if they took the idea as far as it could go, and had more than one super soldier. And they were all cute girls.
ok, now it sounds like Voogies Angel [link] God i love masami Obari Drawings.
Voogie Angel are part of the American military? I have to see this anime?
im sure its not part american, just a part of the earth forces
Well I'll take a look anyway.

Wow, that's actually a really cool idea. I don't think I've ever seen a show/comic/whatever wherein a government actually puts some thought into the tactical implications of having supersoldiers. Of course, in most (of the admittedly very few) shows like this I've actually seen, the rest of the military is shown as being so useless that they might as well dismantle it and use the savings to buy supersoldiers. In real life, your interpretation makes far more sense, and more importantly, it sounds really interesting/entertaining.

Yea, it's usually: The government is a bunch of mell-intended buffoons who only deploy super-soldiers when the incompetant and/or superfluous bulk of their forces are swept aside by the villain of the week; or the government is a creepy iluminati who seeks to use super-powered beings toward some unknowable, sinister end, making it necessary for the requisite super-powered "teens-with-attitude" to take it on the lam. Even War Heroes, which I thought at first would be like Streamers, is just about a bunch of traitorous thugs who sell their powers to terrorists. Perhaps there's a place for this idea after all.
great concept!... stronger sailor worriors... Navi and marine Gilr looks the best...^^ Beside the battle queen
Marine Girl seems to be people's favorite. I actually did another marker drawing of just her for an actual Marine.
YES? Are you going to upload it someday here? Would be cool..
I'll have to do a computer coloring of the lineart. The DI has the original marker drawing, and dumbass me didn't make a copy of the colors.
Oh Too sad... But with computer coloring it will looks amazing too^^

As far as "a story in which super-powered sailor girls become the chief expression of strategic military power", I think that's so awesome! I wish I could think of stories like you do but a storyline just isn't my thing, I guess.

Sometimes the best place to start is to take a look at a story you've read or seen, but were not satisfied with the ending. If you can determine how you would end it, you already are telling a story. Fan fiction, taking the characters and circumstances from a story you enjoy, and expanding upon them is good practice for an someone trying to get some practice. After a fashion you might become comfortable inventing your own characters. Have fun with it. It's not as hard as you might think. ')

Hmmm, thanks for the advice I'll keep that in mind I have the characters, I just never made a story for them...I just do what my friend calls "Mini soap operas", basically when I post pics dealing with my OCs I'll write little scenarios for them.
Sounds like you already have a good start. Soap opera huh? Perhaps I shouldn't be asking you for comments about my "sailor girls at war." Perhaps I should show you a couple of my other characters.

Yeah she calls them soap operas but I'm not really sure. Here's a few examples: [link] [link] [link] You art style is so cool! I'm not sure how to describe it...it's like macho but still sweet, if that's makes sense. It's kinda like a mix of Shoujo and Shonen, I guess that's what I mean?

It seems to me that you're quite adept at making stories. And you really like cross-dressing boys. (Oh Shoujo Manga, you corrupt or youth in ways Frederick Wertham never even imagined. ) "macho but still sweet,"? You know what? That sounds a damn sight better than "Old School", which is what I usually hear from folks. Thanks.

Yeah, I do like those cross-dressing boys! Yeah "Old school" just doesn't work for me, it doesn't do it justice...(niether does "macho but still sweet") It's something else, I just don't know how else to say it. You're welcome
I love this idea. And these designs. And the complicated facts and story that still makes sense even when my head is hurting. And the art, naturally. And aww poor Coast Guard

Well thank you for your kind words. I certainly hope my work is not in any way responsible for your headache, and I hope you feel better soon. I suppose over time, the Streamer program would be expanded, so there would eventually be a Coast Guard Girl. But what would her power set be. She would probably have limited flight, undersea capability, and certain degrees of invulnerability. She really wouldn't need weapons, so she'd have no hand lasers, and it's doubtful she'd be issued and artillery rifle. However, she'd probably have super strength, to allow her to lift damaged ships out of danger. Despite her powers, she's still only Coast Guard. The others will probably give her a hard time.

Want to know why there isn't a Coast Guard girl? It's because she's completely top-secret. She's the ace in the hole if the other four get beat. Her whole mission? Rescue the streamers at all cost, and has an amalgam of the powers of each girl, but doesn't specialize in any one. Thing is because of her role she's kept away from the spotlight. Also usually kept in the dark on things as the US Military doesn't want her flying off on her own saving people, yet she has a superhero complex like no-other. As she's kept as the rescue in case everything goes wrong she's generally "kept in residence" on some ship which is randomly chosen every 6 months, to keep the knowledge of where she is to a minimum. Usually near an operation area where the other streamers are. They hope they don't need her, but all she wants to do is get into the fight...
That sounds more like something more appropriate for Special Forces Girl.
Special Forces Girl is the black-ops, edgy spin-off series.

I just had a thought. In all the shoujo mecha shows, (Which my concept predates my more than a decade, ironically enough.) there is a gateway character, who serves as the lens through which the audience is introduced to the world. She is universally short haired, and lacking in any outstanding talents. She's not as busty as the mandatory bombshell, she's nicer than the mean girl, though not as nice as the gentle shy girl. In general, she's more or less a blank template, there to bring the special traits of the actually interesting characters into higher relief, by means of contrast. This could be an ideal role for Coast Guard Girl. Crap, I may actually have to start doing something with this idea.

I wager she'd be the weaker of the crew, so for instance she needs a life vest as she has a limited period underwater before she has pull the cord and head to surface for air, can't take as much damage, and can't really do the work of her "sisters" alone. If anything she's the Imouto. A rookie who is the one we feel the most connection with, so whenever she's in trouble and the others save her, you begin to like the other four as true heroines. That and every once in a while Coast Guard Girl gets to save the day, which'll really bump up her merchandising. ^_^

Also, Marine Girl and Army Girl will be total Tsundere Friends. They will constantly bicker, leveraging the ongoing Army/USMC rivalry that my service friends and house mates have borne out over the years. They'll always be in one another's face until one of them is in a jam. Navy Girl will be the snotty, rich one. I guess that leaves Air Force Girl as the brainy one. I need to redesign her with some "Royal Smart Person" glasses.

Actually Navy and Air Force compete in one-upping themselves. Navy is more Wise than Smart, always thinking before she acts, and most of the time she's right. Although she tends to be too by-the-book, and that can bite her in the butt sometimes. She might rely on AF to get her out of a fix, but sometimes might lean on CG as well. It's rare she ends up in a pickle, but usually when it happens it's usually something BIG. Navy might look Rich, but she's actually more the mother-hen. Always making sure everyone is geared up right or telling them about some regulation they've forgot, and usually in an embarrassing way. And don't make her angry... you wouldn't like her when she's angry. Her nickname is "Boomer" for a reason. Air Force is impulsive (as the youngest of the girls), but she's intelligent and a gear-head (which helps her get out of most fixes). She knows her way around any machine, and has dreams of being in space. Also probably is the only girl equipped with a parachute, not because she's careful, but because skydiving is freaking awesome! Of course it always ends up being Navy's or CG's job to save her out of her latest misadventure. Devil-may-care. Also unlike Navy, AF is sneaky as a ninja and WILL come out of nowhere. She loves doing that. Also of the girls, while Navy is old-school intel, AF is scary good at computers. To her, any computer is her's to play with. (Cue hacking music)

Now for their foe. Their enemy will be something that conventional forces are powerless against. An evil scientist is trying to force her version of peace upon the world by activating a series of devices, whihc turn everything within their range of influence into the most adorable, chibified version of itself; Houses smiling with winking window eyes, anthropomorphic cars that bounce along on inner tube tires, dancing to songs sung by cherry trees with cherub cheeks. Never ending, sugar coated horror. All armies entering the Quarantine Transformation Zones, or QTZ's, are subject to these horrific, disfigurements, and only add to the strength of forces bent on building new devices and spreading the scourge of super-deformation globally. fav.me/d1q20xc Basically leveraging my love of darwing SD hardware. Only the Streamers can enter the QTZ, destroy the field generators, and free those trapped within. So once again, the worlds holds its breath, and waits for America to come save its ass.

HAAHAH! I LOVE IT! Although toonification is the least of the world's troubles. Deep in the Marianas trench a terrible testament to humanity's ability to destroy itself slumbers. The super-cold water and pressure keeping her asleep... see Japan did in fact create ONE war maiden... but she was too powerful even for Imperial Japan to control. So in a sacrificial effort she was encased in a iron casket filled with liquid nitrogen and cast into the deep. There the iron casket sat, slowly leaking liquid nitrogen into the waters around it, and slowly, painfully, the war maiden thaws. If and when she awakens she will have the biggest, most extreme case of waking up on the wrong side of the bed. With the world she knew long gone, she just looks for any fight, and finds the majority of the world wanting... but she heard of these girls who might give her the fight she's always wanted... thing is she's almost 90 now, and while still in her prime even a super soldier must answer to time's arrow sooner or later.

Hah! Japan's answer to Captain America! This sounds like a menace for "season two". Of course, as far as anyone in the Streamers program knows, Japan produced no working Tactical Girls before turning their research over to the US. I'm sure the revelation of the existence of Shoujo-Zero would be quite an embarrassment for the JSDF. They may even call upon the American unit too late to save a major city of land mark from destruction; opting to attempt to contain her with Anti-Tactical Maid Weapons, developed in secret, as a countermeasure against the Streamers. The Streamers would be livid at the JSDF, right up until the moment they realize that the ATMW's actually came from the US. Starting as an adversary, the Streamers eventually realize that each of them could have been Shoujo-Zero under different circumstances. Of course this revelation will not changer the fact that SZ will have to be put down, for the greater good. Season Two would definitely be darker, featuring more intrigue, and higher character stakes than the QT War story arc.
Well actually it could still be light hearted. SZ is unhinged from her time in the box, so her schemes are cartoon villain at best, DBZ BBEG at worst.
BBEG?
Big Bad Evil Guy (or Girl)...

Ah. I thought this was in reference to a specific anime, as was you mention of DBZ. I'm still brimming with ideas for this concept now. The opening should definitely involve the destruction of a Nimitz class carrier. (The fact that this happens so much in anime is what inspired Streamers to begin with.) A carrier group will be standing off shore in support of a special forces operation, launched to capture a rogue evil scientist. (I was going to call her Doctor Genius, but a quick google search revealed that name was taken. ) The troops dispatch the expected crowd of faceless hench dudes, and proceed to arrest the mad doctor, who is disquietingly okay with the whole affair. Soldier: "Come along quietly, doctor. We've taken care of your private little army. It's over!" Doctor: "Quiet the contrary, soldier. My army's just arrived." No sooner do the soldiers grab the doctor than her first Deformation Generator activates, turning everything, and everyone, on or around the island (Except for the doc herself, who as made herself immune to its effects with a special process that turned her skin a delightful shade of lavender.) into the most adorable, super deformed versions of themselves. Now with the might of history's most huggable carrier battle group, the maniacal doctor moves her plan of global conquest forward. Two additional thoughts. 1.) One of the special forces soldiers initially confronting the dread doctor could be the future Special Forces Girl. Of course this raises the additional question of whether SFG is immune at first, of if she's liberated by the Streamers to later add to their ranks, with a thirst for vengeance created by her time as a super deformed thrall. 2.) After watching Nichijou, I am wondering if I could get away with making the BBEG into a little girl, like Professor Hakase Shinonome from the anime, Nichijou? Would explain the obsession with cuteness.

1) Or even better? Want to know why Special Forces Girl always wears a mask? It's because she was "disfigured" by the Deformation Generator. It's given her the gift of eternal youth, but now she's a 16-year old Kawaii schoolgirl-looking sort FOREVER. Talking Shoujo Manga. It's why she's always in her gear. Thankfully she didn't get hit directly by the Deformation Wave... either that or Deforma Particles do strange things... after all Deforma Particles are part of the process that creates Battle Maidens as well, but there's something extra added... [Insert super-element crystal here] A note on SFG's appearance: She's "Pure". Everything about her. It's why she covers up, if she ever undressed in front of someone, they would either die of a nose hemorrhage from the sheer beauty, or become obsessed with her. She is subtle and sublime perfection. Many think because she's so protective of her identity she's some sort of hideous, deformed freak... the opposite is true. She just doesn't wanna blow everyone's mind. The Streamers are immune because of the PROCESS, and anyone else effected by Deforma Particles. 2) YES. She wanted to first become immortal. She succeeded beautifully in her opinion. Sadly the reason why she's a mad scientist is her frontal lobe was damaged in the process (smaller skull, children have low impulse control). So... she went mad. Mad about ALL THE CUTE KAWAII THINGS!!! Oh, and one thing about the "Toon Fleet": All the big ships look like pool inflatables that are in the shape of ships. Big, inflatable, armored ships, with just one weak point (several actually, since they're chambered)... the valves. Something Navy Girl, Airforce Girl, and Marine Girl are good at finding and popping open. This also means that it's easy to restore the fleet... just find the deflated boat and re-fill it with air. Makes patching easy too if something actually manages to puncture one of those things. Oh one weakness for Army Girl: Of all the things she CAN do...she's deathly afraid of drowning. She passed her water confidence course and everything, but actually BE in the ocean? She'd rather stay on dry land or on deck. In fact, what ARE each of the Streamers' weaknesses?

These are some amazing suggestions!!! It hadn't occurred to me to have Special Forces Girl be covered up in any way, but that would give her that air of mystery like Snake Eyes from G.I. Joe or The Stig from Top Gear, or Raim Kawasaki from Bakuon, and I'm sure there are a multitude of examples I am not aware of. Having a long standing mystery regarding her appearance, before it is finally revealed that see is a survivor of the first deformation, sounds very satisfying, along with the big reveal that she hides herself because of the surreal beauty that is now her eternal curse. The idea of the chibified Aegis cruisers and Nimitz class carriers having the weakness of pool toy valves is inspired. In addition to just being plain cute and funny, it's a nice reference to the mandatory "weak point" that seems to afflict all genre monsters. And yes, the super deformation effect and the Streamer process, do share a similar technical origin. The evil doctor may well have been a high ranking scientist for the program, until her experiments with rejuvenation curse her with a skull too small for her mighty brain. In fact, it could even be possible that our widdle doctor, (Hmmm, Doctor Widdle? ) once cured of her rejuvenation, could revert back to her old self and work towards redemption by helping the Streamers against Shoujo Zero. Army Girl is afraid of heights, actually, but I really do need to give more thought to the various quirks of the other girls.

Ah... Maybe Air Force girl then has the "I don't wunna drown" issue then. Also Doctor Widdle... I like. Thing is I think the change should be permanent. If she turns to the side of good, she needs to bear the mark of her mistake. "To remind me..."
Plus, she stays cute.
Oh yes. Forever cursed... but maybe having eternity to watch is a lesson in of itself.
Finally created her.
I saw! ^_^

title: Skirted Swan
description: Even as rigidly censored as Science Ninja Team:Gatchaman (Gawd, who comes up with these titles?!) was in its Battle of the Planets incarnation, I distinctly remember more than one panty-shot of Princess (Jun the Swan to you purists. ) making through to my very American television screen. comments.body: Actually in the Gatchaman anime the made in the late 90's..you even see her named during her transformation. MT

Truth be told, the story was pretty stereotypical IIRC (and we europeans got the uncersored version) Pretty much Mazinger Z but with Power Ranger heroes. Then again, I watched this years ago and have a foggy memory of it.

We saw it in the US back in 1978. It was marketed as Battle of the Planets to exploit the Star Wars craze. Since it was a full decade and a half before Power Rangers made over to these shores, it was unbelievable fresh and new to us. But they did edit out all the cool violence. Ironic, I was thinking of how back in the 1970's they repackage anime to appeal to Star Wars fans, while nowadays, the folks at Lucasfilm give Anakin Skywalker a spunky teenage sidekick to appeal to anime fans. Hmmm, I wonder exactly when Darth Vader murders Ahsoka Tano? He betrays and kills all the Jedi right? Or are the going to retcon that too.
Well, I can tell you over here they didn't use the Star Wars part, that's for sure.

I do, I do (remember the comic that is). Best american mecha comic ever. Isn`t she that lady from the "Blood nations"? Really nice to see that someone remembers.

Blood Nations. Sounds like a good title for a DJ revival. It annoys me that IDW reprinted just about every First Comic except DJ. I think a new Dynamo Joe comic would be cool. I guess I should chalk that up riht there with new Firefly or Space: Above and Beyond. episodes. No one wants to invest in the stuff I like.

I Love the this illustration of yours pal. Your artwork , specially the coloring of this illustration is pure late 80s and first half of the 90s anime style, style I love and a style you have come to make your own in such a natural and original way. It is so cool to see artwork of this line still today rockin' it!

Thanks for the kind words. It's funny. When I got on DA, and first received comments saying my work was reminiscent of what many identified as "old sküle" anime, I felt defensive and did a lot of research in post millennial animation. I was hoping to somehow "update" my style to be more palatable to a modern viewership. What I found however was that much of the new offerings from anime studios simply wasn't as appealing to me. Perhaps its a product of me being old and becoming "set in my ways", but I just felt that the character and mechanical designs of Japanese cartoons produced since the turn of the century have acquired a bland, flavorless sort of androgyny. Both character and machine alike look to be smoothed down and economized in detail and texture to optimize them for computer rendering. They all seem cold somehow. There's no life in them. The older works have a passion that communicates to me even now. There's something about knowing that every mecha in the most intricate battle scene are all hand drawn and cell painted that heightens my appreciation for the old forms. It's probably just the rantings of an "old sküle" fan, but I simply feel more passion from anime from the era you described. It is the greatest compliment you could possibly pay now to say I have taken this style and made it my own. I'm glad to know that such wonderful influences can be seen in my drawings. (I return the compliment to you as well, btw. You draw Lynn Minmei like nobody's business. ) I find it to be my privilege to spread this influence to a new generation. "Old Sküle" forever.

I can tell you that for me the Old School anime are those days from Ozamu Tesuka to the late 70s. From the 80s until the first half of the 90s it is just THE GOLDEN AGE!!! Yeah men!! I also tried to find anime movies or shows of this new times of anime and I really never found something as original and powerful, except for those rare lucky incidents like the Mad House ( Pure Yoshiaki Kawajiri ) BATMAN short film, it was a breeze of those days. I think just the same as you. The cool full of art and style drawing has being sacrificed to achive a more realistic animation, hence the dilemma: Anime is not just a matter of movement, half of the appeal of the subjet is that what moves are drawings. I feel that is the whole point. If you don`t like the art who cares how realistic the animation is. For realistic movements we have actors and movies. That line without personality, without a drawers style in its core is the theme of now a days animation. All they offer is just the design of characters and mecha, an industrial mass produced drawing with hundreds of different animators making the same thing again and again. I think that that passion we both miss in new anime was possible back then where little art studios produced their very personal works. Now a days si too industrialized, but like someone said, cinema IS industry and animation these days smells more like a business than like an artistic expression. That is why it is so cool to meet an artist that has that blood in his veins. Not just memorized patterns and cliché, someone that has not just learnt to draw watching anime and reading manga, but has also studied art as a whole, that is what I see in your work. That is the only way to continue the legacy of those days, not just drawing like your favorite drawer. I think that not everyone can tell that my illustrations of aircraft like the F-5, f-16, and P-51D are product of my 80s anime influences. All that love for the mechanichal detail was implanted deep in my brain and soul with Macross and so many other great anime movies and of course there is always STAR WARS an influence that runs too deep in me. "Old Sküle" forever TOO! P.D: Glad you liked my Minmey!
By the way, I do remember Dynamo Joe You are not alone
Oh SNAP I loved this comic., Still have every issue plus the Grimjack crossover. It was extremely inspirational to me as a young and aspiring mecha artist!!! thanks for the Flashback

Same here, pal. When people ask me what comic (Invented by someone else.) that I would most like to draw, I say Dynamo Joe. Sadly, I usually get blank stares at that point. I guess they're expecting me to say Spider-Man.

Holy crap! Dynamo Joe fanart! This is like finding a live coelacanth! Hell yes, I remember it. They used to brag that DJ was the only giant robot comic on the stands that was not based on a tv show, movie, or toy.

title: Uzi Cat Colored
description: Colors by Phil Moyer. [link]

comments.body: "Cat and Mouse" got a little unfair.
Yes, but not in the way you might think. The cat works for the mouse. [link]
The tough athletic one working for the meek one. Do they ever get unprofessional and goof around or get in fights and such?

Well not as much fights as arguments. Constant, festering arguments. The cat harbors a belief that all felines naturally deserve to sun themselves in bookstore windows--a tradition dating back to ancient Egypt, and in so doing protect the premises from evil spirits and such. (In cat lore the accidental fire that immolated the fabled Library of Alexandria in 48BC occurred several hours after the attending librarian shooed the facilities guardian cat.) In exchange for this spiritual protection, librarians are afforded the additional privilege of caring for and feeding the cat that has deigned to safeguard their building with its anointed presence. Granted this was all in the ancient past when cats walked on four legs, and didn't talk, but it still mystifies Cassandra why Molly stubbornly insists upon her doing actual work. For Molly's part, it is a constant struggle to suppress her natural annoyance with cats in general, and Cassandra's religious devotion to laziness and rudeness in particular. The mouse frequently contemplates firing the feline, but every time the cat offends a customer with yet another round of "'Help YOU?' I'm a bookstore cat, YOU should be serving ME!" Cassandra turns around and saves the store from alien book thieves, or the firemen from Fahrenehit 451 intruding into reality. In fact there are times when the cat's inexplicable mastery of all known firearms and martial arts, makes the mouse momentarily consider the veracity of the "guardian cat" myth. Of course that still doesn't make Cassandra any more personable.
