title: DMV: All Gangs Here
description: Ugh, This may well be the very first thing I ever colored on a computer, and it shows! The main reason I'm subjecting myself to inevitable torrent of disgusted gaffaws at my protean coloring attempt, is to display some previously unrevealed DMV characters. Long time viewers will recognize meter maid AJ and her lazy sidekick Drew. The white haired woman at the top left top is Roulette, hotshot officer of the GHP. (Global Highway Patrol.) A long time rival of AJ's, she's fond of pointing out that she has worldwide juridiction as well as a self-ware, nigh-indestructable squad car with a high-yield capacitor gun. Secretly, she's annoyed that AJ catches more crooks with just a gun that shoots parking tickets. On the right side of the composition, we witness the unfolding tradgedy of Pool Parties. Fierce battles by armed and armored car pools for territorial gain in the vast Megarage complex. In the silver minvan, we have the Hiocc's, (For High Occupancy vehicle.) who take advantage of the large carrying capacity of their vehicles, and use them as makeshift troop transports allowing them to overwhelm carpools driving smaller vehicles. Toward that end, I model all their SUV's after attack helicoptors. For instance, the silver minivan here is based on a Hughes 500 Littlebird. The green chopper carries two members of Motor Horde, one of the dying breed of biker gangs, who exist chiefly because, even at the time of this drawing I knew I would one day figure out how to do Akira style tail light trails in photoshop. The motorcycle is based on the old Green Machine toy. (If you don't know what that is, then your childhood sucked, and your parents didn't really love you.) Finally, going back to the top, the robot with the beret is a Bot-Nik. Bot-Nik's are fixtures at futuristic coffee houses. For the price of a single $10.00 coin (The not-too-distant-cyberpunk-dystopian-future's answer to the quarter.) they spontaneously generate free-verse poetry. They also serve as emergency thug muscle whenever the DMV or the GHP raid your coffee house. In the NTDCD future you see--coffee is illegal.

comments.body: i think the coloring is great!
Amazing as usual ! Even the colors
You're too kind.
Bot-Nik? Oh, the puns... they're so irresistable. <3
I'm so in awe.

That's Lucille and Bo on the green thing, by the way. Now that I see the design again, I'm going to have to change it. This was back when I wanted a Recumbent Electricycle to look like a "grown-up" version of a Green Machine. (THE collest, baddest ass, most child-endangering evolution of the tricycle ever conceived. ) Unfortunately the "Green Machine" design has no place for the chrome plated "faux-engine" noise maker. Back to the drawing board for me. I may just redraw this whole thing. So much has changed since I first did this.

Clever. I would never have expected nostalgia - and - wait a minute, why is it looking like an oversized tricycle a bad thing? As a kid, everyone wants a specific toy - so why not give it to them as an adult? In this dank and super-consumer future, is it such a bad idea?

Don't worry, I don't want to drop the whole Green Machine aesthetic entirely. But in the design above there's no place for an ostentatious mass of chrome piping, as described in my write up. Trust me, there is plenty of room for venal consumerism in the "NTDDCF".
That van reminds of the truck in the old Highman tv show.

I remember that show! I always loved the picture of a soaking wet Jane Badler that they'd show in the opening credits. Oh yea, and the pre-bunny, Energizer spokesman as the crazy sidekick. I didn't consciously bas the van on that truck. I actually based it on an MH-6 Little Bird helicopter. (I base all the commuter minivans in America Jone's world on attack helicpoters. Wait 'til you see the one I based on the Mil Mi-24. ) But then the Highwayman's truck cab did transform into a helicopter, so perhaps it has influenced me afterall.

title: Visit Scenic Asgard
description: A two page spread I did for Mike Avone Oeming's Hammer of the Gods, of Heimdal the guard of the rainbow bridge of Asgard. That's a female frost giant looming in the background. It's been so long I've forgotten her significance to the story, although I do remember being disappointed I wasn't asked to draw any Valkyries.

title: Who Let the Dragon Out?
description: One of the advantages of being the sole proprietor of N.E. Boox (Otherwise known as The Most Dangerous Bookstore.) is the fact that, when giant fire-breathing serpents* start to terrorize the town, you know exactly where they came from. Someone's going to get a stern talking to when Molly gets back from lunch. * This particular breed of serpent is called a Bookwyrm. FYI.

comments.body: "Oh no, not again... *sigh*" I didn't realize you had a Toriyama-esque setting where normal humans don't bat an eye when anthropomorphic rodent is standing in the middle of the street. Still this is an excellent page, its composition is so superb you can even use it as the first page of the first issue! I'm worried about what worse, though, someone opening a book inside N.E. Boox that came from its Children's Section, Science Fiction/Fantasy shelves, the Occult/Supernatural area, or the Adults Only aisle.
Hmmm adults only? I'm going to have to let that idea percolate.
*high-fives* Perverts of teh intarwebs unite!
Very well done comic page!
Thank you for the kind words.

title: BookStore Pencils
description: Pencils for a Most Dangerous Bookstore pin-up. Don't know why I gave Molly such an actiony pose, I like how it turned out although it seems a bit out of character to me. Yes, that's a shotgun behind Cassandra's back.

comments.body: Come on, as the protagonist of the story, she's entitled to look cute. And the pose is good for her, it lets people see that even if she is a wee little mouse, she be packing where it counts. The pantsleeves rolled up to her thighs is a sexy touch, too. Say, what model shotgun is that in the back? I can't tell.
Rats I used to know which shotgun it was. I had an airsoft model of it that I had to give up when I moved. I'll have to get back to you on that.

title: Wilma Deering
description: I desperately hope I'm not the only one who remembers the old Buck Rogers in the 25th Century television show. To me, it was just about the most awesome thing to be projected by a cathode ray tube! It had laser guns, hyper-tech futuristic cities, and there was no problem that couldn't be solved by an outer space dogfight! And best of all, there was Erin Gray in her painted-on flight suit! Televised gold.

comments.body: *laughs* Ah, waht a show. Definately a product of its times. I don't remmeber much/many episodes, but what little does stick is both the good and the bad. The rather strange talking 'Simon' around the kid in the robot suit fro one of the strange. &#039 Cheers!
Do you remember the episode where Gary Coleman was president?

Um, no, nope, my mind is thankfully blank on that matter. Perhaps a sign of some sort of trauma shock? &#039 The one that does stick is the Buck Rodgers 'bombing run' mission. I just vaguely kind of remember the whole Bad Guy: "Ha! They can never defeat my super weapon here!" Good Guys: "I know, we'll pull these really old bombers out of mothballs. Recruit the old pilots/Gunners that used to fly them and swoop in under their defenses!" Heh, was kind of neat, in a recreate the Death Star trench kind of way. Cheers!
Gary Coleman? President? While I regret never seeing the show, I must admit that is a scary image.
I remember that episode. Horonimus Fox was his name I believe. Definately during the time Gary Coleman's career was at a peak.

Heh, I've been occasionally peeking at the new Buck Rogers comic book, and I am forced to wonder why they don't have the space Lugers, the star fighters, the wisecracking robots, the girls in tights, and the black president.* (The three-foot tall black president.) I wish I could get a job drawing for that publisher. I'd take care of that right away. I mean what's the point of Buck Rogers without Wilma's blue painted on flight suit? * It's funny. Back in 1978, having a black president was how you could tell the story took place in the far-flung future. Now that we actually have one, I guess we'll have to stick with the other tropes like food pils and personal space fighters.

yeah Hieronymous (sp?) Fox was from Buck's time (the 20th century) and was thawed out by a alien race from Genesia, (I believe) and once they realized he was a super genius they elected him president of their planet. It was kinda goofy, but fun.
too bad , im not familiar with this . Ive heard of the name Buck Rogers though.
Get the DVD of the first season. It's like James Bond (Roger Moore James Bond anyway.) in space.

ok, i think i know why ive heard this tittle before. you see, cartoon network had this show called Duck Dodgers. with daffy duck as Duck Dodgers. So im pretty sure its some sort of parody to this in any case i got both name confuse.

The newer version does have that great Tom Jones song, which spoofs his earlier song, "Thunderball," and they add the Martian Queen to the cast , but you just can't beat the old 1950's cartoon short. [link] A little trivia; Originally published in 1928 by Philip Francis Nowlan, Buck Rogers actually predates even Flash Gordon.
I remember very little of of Buck Rogers, just heard of it in passing. Though she looks def tough and yet sexy too. A flight helmet and big hair...yep...it must have been from the old days of sci-fi.

It's from the 1970's. Even though Buck Rogers as a work of fiction originally predates even the venerable Flash Gordon, (I know, I know "Flash!--Ah-Ah!" ) it was resurrected in 1979 as part of the headlong rush to cash-in on the popularity of Star Wars. However, years before Lucas thought to untape Carrie Fisher's bosom and haul out the metal bikini, good ol' Glen Larson thought to play the "sex appeal" card with Erin Gray's wardrobe of "painted-on tight" jumpsuits. Good times.

I thought that the fighters where real cool as a kid. But I always got a kick out of what they thought some 20th century items were or how they were used. But my favorite episode had to be the one with the little guys with the mind powers. Now that was some good television.
Off-think, off-think.
you are not alone she ruled tweekee could be blown out an airlock as far as i am concerned
'Specially season two Tweekee, when they didn't even bother with the original voice. But then, season two mostly stank anyway. 'Cept for the "Off-Think" episode.

Liked the later episode with the dwarf looking guys who didn't know what a female was, so they decided to find out by taking a closer look at Commander Deering... using their slight (individually that is) telekinesis powers. "All Think, all think, all think" and there went her belt, then her top started coming undone (snickering). Ah... see you liked that episode also. You captured the show very well.

Tee Hee. I think it was "Off Think." But whatever it was, the important thing was the result. I saw there was a new Buck Rogers comic book. I guess they were trying to be more like the classic incarnation, because from what I saw, there were no star fighters, no blue tights, and no alien princesses who wore bras as tops. And I'll bet they're even wondering why they aren't selling any copies.

I read a interview long ago with Gil who talked about that episode and how , before the studio execs had a collective fit, that scene was much funnier and a bit more adult. According to the interview, the boys had Wilma cornered and began their "off think" bit, we see her belt pop off and her uniform unzip about 1/2 way. Quick cut to poor sleep deprived Buck walking down the hall when one of the little dudes walks up to him holding a bra. He holds it up and asks, "What's this?" Groggy and clueless, Buck replies, "Looks like a B-cup to me." then the little guy runs off down the corridor waving the bra over his head and yelling, "It's a B-cup! It's a B-cup!" The Powers-That-Be saw the footage and said, "NO!!!" More's the pity.
It's probably just as well. The version you described here would probably end up banned somewhere.

BeDeBeDeBeDee! Tweekie wasn't that bad. It was Mel Blanc after all. I loved Buck. The fighters were way more stylish than original Galactica. Way finer spandex clad sexy woman too. May I color this too?

Sure, just send me a note when you're finished. Curious thing about the fighters. The Starfihters were actually Ralph McQuarrie's original design for Colonial Vipers ( No joke [link] [link] ) The Galactica producers opted for the more "Star Warsy" design we are now familiar with. [link] When Buck Rogers came around, the speed of production forced him to use the previously unused (And in my opinion, superior.) design.

I totally embarrassed for I've to big boxes of concept designs of Ralph McQuarrie's and I had no idea he did both TV series fighter designs. Thank you for the info and more importantly the permission. Laters'
Are you saying you have some of McQuarrie's sketches?
Not originals. Published copies. But wouldn't that be something.
I love this one.
Thanks!

Are you kidding me? Gil Gerard Buck Rogers was AWESOME! Erin Gray in essentially painted on clothing? Don't mind if I do! But don't leave out: Pamela Hensley as Princess Ardala...HUBBA HUBBA HUBBA. Even better, I seem to remember Ardala going anti-heroine near the end of her run in the series...I love it when the bad girls do a turnaround [albeit partial] Anybody with me here, or am I just blowing steam? - JC BTW, sorry I didn't mention sooner, but this picture is excellent; great lines on the starfighter, and great detail and shading on Wilma. I hear she still makes appearances at Conventions and she enjoys spending time with her fans - what a doll!

Yea, BR was most awesome. And I did like it toward the end when Ardala and the Draconian forces joined forces with the EDD to fight off the evil forces of Julie Newmar. She had a set of blue tights like Wilma's only her's had convenient openings to showcase her superior--er--hats. Recently, I saw that Dynamite Comics was doing a new Buck Rogers series, but it simply contained updated versions of 1930's designs. There was no reference to the Earth Defense Directorate, no James Bond style adventures, and unforgivably no Star Fighters. I really have to wonder just what the publisher was thinking. Here they had a grand opportunity to capitalize on 70's/80's nostalgia and pubescent hormones, and they throw it all away to create a product that would only appeal to that handful of readers who were alive when Buster Crabbe was still making movies. Smart move Dynamite. I guess for now we'll have to rely upon our memories--and our DVD's.
I own it on DVD. One of the cheesiest sci-fi tv shows every aired; gotta love it.
Great...now I have the BUCK ROGERS theme music in my head.
"Faaar beyond the world I've known, Far beyond my tiiiiime..."

title: Pool Girls Color
description: Colors by Phil Moyer. [link]

title: Pool Girls
description: Another old commission I did for Phil Moyer. (I did a lot of drawings for my friend, all of which he is gladly making available to post. Many of them you will not see, as the intervening years have rendered them too shudder-worthy for me to admit to.) These are two OC's Ariel and Arisa (Sorry--Don'y who is which.) enjoying a swimming pool that happens to be atop a skyscaper somewhere in the not-too-distant-cyberpunk-future.

title: Galactica the Anime
description: Another commission for my buddy Phil Moyer. As I recall, this was of Kozue Kaori from Revolutionary Girl Utena dressed in colonial battle dress from Battlestar Galactica:TOS, superimposed over the image of the updated Galactica, flying in formation with a squadron of Mark II Vipers. That's a veritech-style Viper in battloid ("Robot" for you laymen! ) mode, pictured right behind Kozue. Fan fiction folks. It's a long story.

comments.body: looks good. Different size of pen nib really make this pic has some depth. i may need to play around whith thickness as well.
Good thing to notice. Took me forever to realize that the differential in linewidth determined the relative depth of elements in a drawing. Embarrassingly enough, I had to have it pointed out to me.
well, at least you did it. i havent done any drawings that involves depth yet. i may need to start doing it.
How can you not draw the Viper as a Valkyrie Battroid (I likes me Macross terminology better, sorry. ) and not show us how it transforms?! Come on, John, where are the goods? *cries*
I bow my head in shame that I didn't think to do that. Bug me latter if I do not rectify this glaring ommision in a future post.
This time I'm going to take you up on that!
Nice Star wars tag on her right arm patch. otherwise excellent job.

Not an accident my friend. Trust me when I say everything in this picture is exactly as requested. And yes, the request for a Rebel Alliance patch on a Colonial battle jacket has me scratchin' my head too.* Now that you mention it, the choice of anime girl, also confuses me. If one was to choose from the cast of Revolutinary Girl, I'd figure Utena herself would make for a more appropriate Viper driver. However, as long as it's not salacious, (And sometimes even when it is.) it's all about what the Commssioner wants to see. * Phil does have a lengthy fan-fic that explains the conext of everything. [link] if you really want to know how everything fits together. Me? I'm just happy I found someone who wants to pay me to draw stuff.

Well disregarding the below critiques personally I really like this concept. This looks more like a genuine Anime/Manga version of BSG then the "Shades of New Caprica" manga that was made a few years back. So in appreciation im givin ya one of my "El Cheapo" Llamas and a recommendation in TrekGalacticaCaprica group for this piece. I hope you can make more works based on this concept in the near future. Thanks!!
"Shades of New Caprica" manga? I'd be very curious to see this. A google search isn't turning anything up. Might you have a link I can check out?

Sorry thats my fault the correct name of the Manga is Echoes of New Caprica and is published by Tokyopop. Here is the [link] . Unfortunately this was the best I could find. Apparently not even Battlestar Wiki has a page for this manga. According to the link the manga (which is a standalone story) takes place in between the season 3 New Caprica story arc (during and after), however even the article agrees that the artwork in this isn't very well made. Hope this helps and Thanks!!
is that kara thrace?
No, it's actually the commissioner's OC. This is Kara Thrace.
who?
A... Veritech Viper. That'd upset Lucifer so much, his frownie lights would be stuck to 'on' even when he wasn't talking.
The Cylons would naturally answer with a mecha Raider Imagine, a cross between a giant Centurian and a Raider. Giant robot gunfight.
AND IT WOULD BE AWESOME! Ahem. Sorry! Giant Robot Gunfight? Where do I sign up?

I look at those three words and am taken aback by their inherent awesomeness. Actually, this reminds me, I am trying to decide what to put on my business cards: Mecha Master Like it says in the ad copy for the upcoming Empowered special? Mech Daddy My Armored Core® call sign? (Still the greatest video game every conceived by man. Whenever any poor fool tries to compare one of the woeful parade of inferior games that followed it to AC's beauteous majesty, I simply ask them, "Can you swap out the main character's legs for tank treads? If not, then shut the eff up!" ) or Giant Robot Gunfight Because it's just cool to say. Decisions...decisions...

title: Tachiku Color
description: Colors by Phil Moyer. [link] This is probably my favorite coloring job of his.

comments.body: Looks fantastic, colors, lineart... everything! How's your Xmas?
It was AWESOME. I got Photoshop CS2, a lap desk and a G.I. Joe Cobra Firebat with figure!! I'll relay your compliments on the color to Phil. How was your X-Mas?

Wow!! Glad you receive some great gifts!! Nah my Xmas was, well... I've better :S, but cannot comply, family and friends are allright so, that's a great thing... no gifts for me, well, yeah I receive news from my friends (you included ) that they we're fine and happy so, that's a great gift! Yes, please send my compliments and regards to Mr. Phil, his color and your artwork make a great mix! Prepare for New Year's Eve!! And keep up the good work! Your friend -JP "Digigato"

The one piece I am missing from the Cobra Terrordome that I bought at a yardsale about ten years ago is only missing the Firebat and a few pieces from the GIJOE Flagship... dang people not respecting their property Although I did find a Dreamcast at local Rental/used video store in my neighborhood and got the six games he had for free... Dino Crisis for the win
Thundershock? Pikachu? Well... Nice concept. Love the suit And most specially the girl wearing it. PS: Loks like the "Major" Makoto Kusanagi.
Yea. Funny that.
The tachikoma won't hate you if you spraypaint Pokemon on them?
It's not like they have serious personalities. Of course annoyance at such a livery could be the reason behind another drawing.

title: Tachiku?
description: Don't ask me why the Tachikoma (Took me FOREVER to figure out how to spell that!) has Pikachu nose art. This was a commission for my friend Phil Moyer. (Phil, do you have a DA username?)

comments.body: "Thunder Shock" written on that assault rifle... Does that stand for anything?? Where did it come from? Argh, must research! @_@
Another Pokemon reference. It's Pikachu's finishing move. I thought it would be appropriate.
*smacks forehead* >.< I shoulda seen that one coming...
Just a random question ... Do you get published at all ? Do you have your own comics ? You produce so much work, it seems you do this as a profession. Excuse my ignorance ^_^

Haw, that's my line pal. Curious thing is, you're the second person to ask me that this week. Really, I've only been published for color work. I've filled in the lines at Insight Studios, Image Comics, and IDW. (Pretty much every comic publisher whose name begins with an "I".) Beyond color work, not so much. A state of affairs I am presently working to change. Now you do work in the industry right? At least some branch of art and/or animation. I seem to remember us broaching the topic in an earlier correspondence.

Thanks for the info. Yeah, maybe we talked about it before. I forget, sorry ^_^ I hope you get to do your own comics eventually. I only work in animation, but I am looking into working in comics as well, but I don't know this field very well. Keep at it my friend. You've got the talent and more

Thank you very much. It seems many supremely talented souls from the animation field are looking too get into comics. I have to admit, I'm a little puzzled by that. Unless you work for a hallowed few companies, the pay is crap, and the jobs are scarce, and those doing the hiring are all aware there are legions of college students who'll do twice the work at half the price. I would think comics would be a step down from animation. Especially considering what you're bringing to the table. You must be a masochistic idiot like me who just loves comic books.

Ah, well ... I guess you're right ! Thanks for slapping me in the face and wake me up Hey, maybe you should try to work in animation ? You have a good sense of movement, you could be pretty good I'm sure. Think about it !

Well I'm wouldn't be one to stop you if you wanted to publish a comic book. I'm confident it would blow most of what's on the shelves--well--off the shelves. As for getting into animation. I actually do want to publish a comics this year, but beyond that, I honestly wouldn't know where to begin to approach the animation world.

That's nice of you to say that. ^_^ Have you ever tried to animate anything ? There's a lot you can learn on your own. The thing is that there are lots of different techniques and nowadays everyone loves 3D. But once again, you can learn 3D on you own with some good tutorials. If you want to learn, then just try and you'll learn ^_^ You have brains and talent, you can do it. It's no rocket science, just experience and mostly : a good sense of movement. And you have that ! If you prefer 2D animation, it's rare nowadays but it's still around ! Now for the work... well it's best to be in a city where there are a lot of studios. In 2D animation, you can easily enter a studio as an inbetweener or an inker, and then you work your way up the ladder till you become animation director, yeah ! In 3D animation, just do your tutorials and make some funky animation bits with ready made characters. Make a showreel and you can find some work I'm sure. Then, same thing, work your way up.

Heh. I actually did once do my own animation. When I was a kid, I used to draw tiny animations in every large book in my parent's house! For while, during my childhood, every paperback novel and copy of Reader's Digest had a crude space battle, complete with the hero doing victory rolls as he flew triumphantly through the expanding debris cloud that was once his opponent, scrawled the upper right-hand corner of every page. I suppose the mere fact that I wasn't spanked within an inch of my life over this lengthy campaign of literary vandalism was my parents' own quiet way of encouraging my creativity. Anyway, that's the extremely long form way of saying I am passingly familiar with some of the principles of animation; although there are doubtless volumes of material I have yet to learn. What you propose here does sound interesting. To be honest, since comic book artists don't seem to have many of the advantages of any mailroom employee in any other type of company in the industrialized world, (Such as bi-weekly pay checks and heath benefits.) it actually does behoove me to investigate the other sources of income that can be acquired through my tendancy to scribble.

Man, you seem to me to be a born animator !! What are you waiting for ? I work essentially in London and there are plenty of studios here. Usually they pay well and in time. Most artists are freelance though because projects are short and work comes and goes but if you work well they call you back. But I don't know how it goes in the US. The thing with animation is that the budgets can be enormous and teams can gather hundreds of artists at a time in one place. It can be very exciting and creative. Take your portfolio. Knock on a few doors. You know, just with your skills you could potentially get some work straight away. I'm not saying your first pay would be amazing but I believe you would learn very fast by being inside the industry. Go on, shop around ! (big and small studios)
You've given me a lot to think about here. Thanks for the encouragement.
Well, hope this helps !

title: I am Vengence, I am the Night
description: I AM... A cute little bat with barn-storming goggles. A commission I did of Barnaby Bat, an OC I created as a child, at the behest of my elementary school principle when he expressed concern that all my sketchbooks were full of war machines and explosions. (I hadn't yet discovered how to draw women at that point.) So while, the drawing is more or less recent the character predates all the other cute bat characters you've seen in lame Disney cartoons. Don't worry, I'll go back to babes and guns in my next submission.

comments.body: Why aren't you drawing Batman? Or Nightwing? Or Batgirl? Or any of the other dozen DC books out there where kids stalk the rooftops of the big city at night?
Really, I'm more of a fan.
hemm, looks like a mix with a bat and that thundercat little.... Boy, even i forgot the name of that little red cat of theirs.
Snarf.
lol. how can i forget . haha
He's adorable! One question though: Do you mean that you created the character on the behest of the principal, or are you saying you could draw like this in elementary school?
On the behest of. Trust me when I say the bat looked considerably different back in elementary school.

title: CoCo Gunbun
description: A convention sketch I did for the amazing , of his OC Coco Gunbun. As I understand it, she's basically Pam Grier with bunny ears. Genius.

comments.body: Because everything is better with bunny ears. Well, everything except those analog TV sets come next month thanks to the digital conversion... Bygones. Back to hating on Ronzo...
You chose the wrong emoticon. For , is totally inappropriate. Try Or
I'd post a more appropriate icon, but dA doesn't have a smiley face for "headlock and noogies"

title: El Hazard Collage
description: Another blast from the past in the prehistoric days before my discovery of Photoshop. I had it in my mind to draw all of the characters from the El Hazard ova. I tried to fit them all in there in this car wreck of a composition, but I still think I missed a couple. I drew this with Berol Prismacolor markers, and colored pencils. (Heh, by the time I was done, the marker fumes in my studio were so intense, a lit match probably would have blown up my entire house.)

comments.body: I like your old stuff better, they have this really great oldschool feeling about them!

Water walking comic book gawd, John Byrne always said, a cartoonist knows he's made it when a fan says, "I like your old stuff better." Sea-parting comic gawd, Adam Hughes says you know you made it when a fan says "I hate your effin' ghuts!! Of the two I'll take the Byrne senario. Thanks for the comment.
You're welcome!
God, I love El Hazard...
Whatahell is doing Shinji Ikari thnere!? XD
I alswys did think the two had erily similar character designs.
XD
Oh yes, Fatora <3

The girl who share a face with the protagonist. That cartoon was my first experience with anime/manga's frequently visited androgyny motif. (Not to mention the even more frequently visited yuri motif.) Who knew that my love of robots would expose me to so much strangeness?
Lovely piece. Funny.. I feel I know you from somewhere else. ^__^ Great work, all around.
I get that a lot.
awsome
Thanks.
you're welcome

title: Biker Babe
description: This is a convention sketch I did some time ago. The commissioner was kind enough to send me a jpeg. In retrospect, I wish I had orieneted my paper in a landscape fashion, as opposed to the portrait orientation I went with here. It would've fit better, and allowed me to include more of the speeder bike. Even so it turned out surprising tolerable for something I threw together while trying to shill BGC posters. [link] And, yes, I'm fully aware that Princess Leia had long since changed out of her metal bikini by the time the speeder bike scene took place in Return of the Jedi, but a man can dream.

comments.body: You know, I would agrue that she should have stayed in that outfit for all of Return of the Jedi...
YES!
Well, we can always hope for that whenever Lucas decides to rework the old movies for no reason. But of course, the next edit probably will have Han Solo NEVER SHOOTING.
Yea, Greedo will spontaneously combust!
OMG.. OMG No wonder You were using Star Wars Refs. "Owww Sweet Mama jump start me" just recently watch the Clone Wars 3d movie..

Heh, I wuz using Star Wars only because it was more universally known than the Green Lantern mythos with follows generally the same rules, only with ring color. (Yea, I a multilingual geek!) Which Clone Wars? The cell animated cartoon? Or the cgi one with the spunky teen sidekick?

CGI spunky teen sidekick. In many sense, i feel it was aimed towards kids. The plot could have been better. I would surely like to see more severed bodies. Maybe they should just rebooted the New Hope till The Return of the jedi match the episode 1 to 3

Well one thing I do appreciate abou the cartoon is the fact that they use fresh new Y-Wings. With as beat up as the look in Star Wars (I will NEVER call it "A new Hope!" ) they clearly should have been around in Anakin's youth. I hate interpretations that portray the beat-up, carbon scored, part-shy rebel spacecraft as stolen new prototypes. Okay I'll stop typing now. Geeking...out.
I was in love of Princess Leia... (of course long before I saw princess Amidala ) but this sketch is superb!!
I still prefer Leia. More curvy.
BEST. SLAVE. LEIA. INTERPRETATION. EVAR. Now if only you could draw Olivia Munn in Slave Leia gear in your style. And then I would spontaneously mindgasm to death.
You Olivia Munn too huh? Yea, she is truly the geek goddess.
I like the idea and the choice of bikini outfit... Just a bit puzzled about the shadow, so sharp and detailed for the bike but almost completely absent for the girl (except her foot)...

Just what I was taught. On machines you try to strike a balance between suggesting as much detail as possible, while keeping the image readable; and with youthful pin-up girls you try to suggest as little detail as possible, while still retaining a sense of comprehensiveness to the image. I've never really thought about the conflict in directives. You're very observant to point it out.
Oh wow, this is gorgeous You did a great job on Leia, her pose and expression (and, of course, the legendary LMB), and the bike looks cool too Put them together and you get awesomeness!

Like I said, this should have gone into the movie. It's a shame they covered Leia up during the rest of the film(s) (Yet another reason I feel 'Trek has the advantage over 'Wars. Every chance they got, Uhura was stuffed into an outfit skimpier that her standard uniform, which was also skimpy!) Could you imagine it if they ran with the scantily clad Leia theme? Leia could show up as the unidentified alien bounty hunter in a shiny leather catsuit! Or, when she befriends the Ewoks(No, it's not what you're thinkin.) they, being little folk, wouldn't have a long flowing dress to give her, but rather a skimpy fur bikini. The money in additional action figure and poster sales alone would probably buy Lucas the American presidency. But I digress...

Very definitely ^^ You've got a point there, Uhura's standard outfit was very flattering and she did get a few other outfits. I can certainly imagine a scantily clad Leia. All of those thoughts are great Should go back to the other two movies and continue it And really considering that the slave girl bikini was virtually Carrie Fisher's idea, it's a shame that they didn't do more with the skimpy-attire for Leia. They do seem to have learnt from this with some of the characters in the prequel trilogy though *cough - Aayla - cough*.
Oh yea Aayla her combined appearances amounted to a rockin' 45 seconds of screen time.
Well better then her not appearing at all, and she was quite memorable. But yeah a lot more work needs to go into seeing scantily clad cuties on screen in Wars
That and other little things like plot and characterization and dialogue.

LOL Well personally I always thought the plot wasn't that bad, but yeah the characterisation and dialogue could certainly use a lot of work. That always mystifies me, cause Lucas worked with Spielberg on the Indiana Jones series and the dialogue in that is so much better.

Okay, I guess the overall plot wasn't bad, particularly when you consider that the plot for all three films boils down to that one awesome monologue given by Sir Alec way back in Chapter IV: When George Could Write. But the plotting, or pacing of how those events would take place was still atrocious.
Yeah I think that's the thing; overall the plot was fairly good, but the pacing, the dialogue, the acting direction let it down in a big way.
And her shadow?
Er ah, she's translucent, because of--the force...or something.
That's neat line-work on the bike. Great pic. Good work.

title: Gundam Girls 02
description: Natarle Badgiruel officer of the mobile suit carrier Archangel from Mobile Suit Gundam: Seed. I always like her character design, but I would pay good money to find out where the creators come up with these outlandish names. Actually, while we're on the subject of Gundam, does anyone know exactly what it is that distinguishes a Gundam above all other mobile suits? As far as I can tell Gundams are the only robots that have two eyes. (At least in the MSG universe.) I'm wondering if it's that what sets them apart.

comments.body: Pegasus.

That's the original MSG.(Actually, it was only called Pagasus in the novelization. In the anime, it called White Base and was as Pegasus-Class vessel.*) In MSG: Seed, the vessel was the Arcangel. *It is I, Dukkeofdeath, master of inconsequential factiods!!

Lol, after reading the novel it took me forever to remember that the White Base was the name in the anime and it was of the Pegasus-Class. I confused a bunch of my friends when I called it the Pegasus. Anyway, I'm not a huge Seed fan. Although I liked Natarle's character. She made the show human by having a flawed character who could not fix all her mistakes... Unlike Jesus Yamato (A little nickname for Kira). Also, digging your art style, like a mix of Western comic book and Anime. It also has the feel of a 80's Anime style, can't really explain too well, but it makes me think of a bunch of old Anime out there.
I'm always impressed by your attention to details. How long did it take you for this ?

Four hours. I actually keep a timer next to my drawing board to motivate me, (Long story.) so I generally know precisely how long it takes me to do a piece. What I don't know is if that's a good time. When I'm at a convention doing skecthes, other artists do multitudes of drawings in the time it takes me to do one. Guess I'm a slowpoke.

Same here. I feel quite slow when some artists say "yeah I made this masterpiece in 30 mn during my lunch break whilst surfing the net, eating my sandwich, and knitting a new pair of socks" I don't know how they do it ! Maybe I'll be a comic artist when I'm 90 years old ^_^

gundams are usually the protagonists' high-end mobile suit projects and the derivative projects from that. "gundam" was the name given to the original RX-78 in MSG, and other suits built off of that technology or in the spirit of the project end up sharing the name. in SEED "GUNDAM" was some goofy acronym for the OS the mech ran off of, and in gundam wing the name comes from some alloy the mobile suits were armored with. so, long story short, "the good guy's mobile suit."
Oh yea, I remember the bit about gundaminium or some such metal. "Good guy's mobile suit." Nice.
I know gundam and something about it but I never knew the diffrenz between gundams and other mobile suits -___-

Gundams are the hero mecha. They look like big blue samurai, and in general have two eyes, while all other mecha in the Gundam Universe have cycloptic single eyes. Gundams are also faster, more maneuverable, more durable; essentially superior in every measurable quality to any mech they're likely to meet in combat. But I guess I already said that when I said hero mecha.
XD YAY! Sounds great and really clear!^^
Wow, this is some amazing artwork! Well done!!!
Thanks
My pleasure!
I love that woman. She has a counterpart in another setting I follow where the soldier girl is torn between her duty to the emperor (a despot) and her duty to the empire (the people).
What is the other setting?

It's actually a role-playing game called Legend of the Five Rings™, specifically this lady samurai called Matsu Tsuko. This was during the Clan War period, and at its height, she committed ritual seppuku to demonstrate that blind loyalty to a corrupted emperor, even if he supposedly the Son of Heaven, is wrong when the empire itself is suffering under his rule. Some, if not most of, her art has been less than flattering, though...

To answer your question: Gundams (specifically the RX-78-2) were made out of the more durable "Luna Titanium" over the Zaku's older "Super Hard Steel Alloy" It was never explained in the original series why the RX-78-2 was named "Gundam," I assume it was just a name picked for the mobile suit like "Zaku" was picked for the famous Zeon suit. As per canon, Luna Titanium was renamed "Gundarium" after the One Year War concluded in honor of Amuro's Gundam. Another variation of the metal's name seen in other Gundam series (like G Gundam and Gundam Wing) is "Gundanium Alloy"

Holy cow, that actually makes sense! Usually, answers to long standing science fiction questions are more confusing than the questions themselves. (Take the latest episode of the once-watchable Battlestar Galactica for instance.) Thank you for the information.
Glad to be of service

Mrm, but I seem to remember that the RX-178 Gundam Mark Two wasn't made of luna titanium (probably because the Titans are Spacenoid-hating assholes). I always defined 'Gundam' as a 'white protagonist MS with two eyes and V-shaped antennae'... which doesn't really account for why some Zanscare MSs aren't Gundams, and why some 00 MSs are.

who really knows anyway? The Gundam franchise has changed so drastically from what it was back in the 70's that it's hard to tell what is what anymore. I just wish they hadn't ruined the original Gundam series with Char's Counterattack...absolute worst ending ever for any anime. But then again, Zeta's ending was pretty bad too but not nearly as WTF.

'Yeah, we weren't really sure what to do with the greatest rivalry in anime, so we went ahead and made both of them disappear in a glowing... something. With a rock.' Riiiiiiiight. The last 'good' Gundam was 'War in the Pocket', so far as I'm concerned.
Damn, that's hot. And very well drawn.
Thank you on both counts.

Heck,one look at the thumbnail and I can recognize your style. :3 Awesome details,and man the perspective.It must've taken you some time to be able to draw mechanical stuff with an angle to it,I assume. Oh,mind if I ask what kind of pens do you use?I have a liking for line arts,and I wanted to learn about some stuff so...yeah...

I ink with Sakura Pigma Microns. I use the 08's for thick lines, and the 02's for the thin ones. One more thing, here's a trick an old college buddy taught me. When inking, place a blotter--a newspaper or a newsprint pad (with the cover removed. )--under your drawing. The cushioning will allow you to vary the pressure you put into the paper, and subsequently control the width of your line. This how you can lay down a single line that tapers at the end and is fat in the middle. You can also do this with a brush, or a pen nib, but this way is quicker and significantly less messy. For perspective, read this book. (Hell, sometimes I think I should work for Amazon.com. ) It shows clear concise tips to help anyone of any skill level raise their game in linear perspective. Doesn't matter if you draw manga-style or not. Regardless of how big your characters' eye are, Filippo Brunelleschi's rules style apply.

Can you be any more compacted with information?!Seriously,I could learn a lot from you! XD But really,I appreciate the tips. :3 Hmm,Sakura Microns...I've only heard of them,I'm not sure about GETTING them anywhere in Malaysia...Except maybe in Kuala Lumpur,but eh...How do you compare the ink and the pen nib with uniPIN pens?I usually stick with uniPIN 0.8,but eh,I'm willing to learn.:3 (I do have some hate for Artline pens,since I do use them,but their ink tend to blotch so damn easily...Is it the ink or the head???) Ooohh,the How to Draw Manga series!I think I've seen them a number of times before!But buying them is still another matter...Maybe I should consider part time jobs...

Since the original series, Gundam was named for "Gundanium", the "Lunar-Titanium" alloy that's processed on the moon and used to armor the Gundam and other high-level mobile suits. The other distinguishing things about the Gundams is that that they always tend to be state-of-the-art, sporting all the newest (coolest) technology (like in "Zeta Gundam", there was the Mark II's magnetic coating and the Zeta's "Wave Rider" mode) Yup, I'm all about the original U.C Gundam.

UC Gundam is where it's at. All the series in that continuity are so dense with a wonderful sense of historical verisimilitude. Very few animated series do as good a job as UCMSG at portray the march of technology and how if affects the course of human conflict. I'll always have a fond spot for the original MSG in how it depicted as new school of tactics emerging around mobile suit warfare. The newer series don't really have that feel. They're more like "In Sync In Space" to me.
The Zeta Gundam box set was the best $160 I ever spent!

Gundam names are left up to the imagination of the respective series staff. Original creator Yoshiyuki Tomino said he picked names for his characters based on Japanese word-play jokes. Nice picture of Natarle, she doesn't get enough fan-love.
Yea, it's always the loli girls who get all the attention. I think its nice to lend support when someone designs up an actual adult woman for a change.
Well, you can also do Murrue-kanchou & Sumeragi-kanchou & Cima-sama as well.

I always had a thing for Cima Garahau. She was a class A babe, a starship commander, and a pilot, with a badass mobile suit to boot. I can't believe I never got around to drawing her. She goes on the list.
Exactly!

title: Gundam Girls 01
description: Mobile Suit Gundam was a defining moment in anime. It was the first show to portray giant robots as mass produce piloted vehicles as opposed one-of-a-kind mechanical superheroes. It's influence is such that, it is often called the "Star Trek" of Japan. I suppose one of the secrets to the original MSG's success was the events surrounding the final fate of the mysterious Lala Sune. Her tragic end ignited an irreparable antipathy between the hero Amuro Ray, and the villain Char Aznable, (Man, these freaky names!)and set in motion circumstances that would lead to show's final act, as well motivate its sequels. But most importantly, without Lala, there was no ethereal glowing chick to turn the end of the story into stream-of-conciousness soup, like there is in 90% of Japanese animated features. She's pictured here with her Tricorn Hat mecha. You have no idea how hard I tried to make that goofy thing look menacing.

comments.body: I wish i could see the old ones. Im not that much a fan of Gundams, although i have 2 of the movies. my brother always pronounce Gun dam as gunedam(following the Japanese pronounce) In any case. I love the chick. If this piece were to be given anime style shading, i think it would look too awesome. I was thinking of blue for some reason, even though i never seen the color refs.

I thought I was giving it anime...style...shading I've been listening to my marvel zombie buddies call my work anime-style for so long, frakly I was unprepared for the barrage of glowing praise for how American my work is. I'm definately missing something. If you could, point out some examples of what you would call anime-style shading. Clearly, I need to study more before I get this right.

hehe, well, mostly, i would go for the coloring style of animes i think would go with this style. I think something like a cowboy bebop coloring might be interesting. Of course, im not into cell shading just yet. But still, i got time to improve my craft. Maybe i should try what i was thinking with this picture..

You're talking about coloring eh? Well, I still get comments on how Marvelly, my colors are. The comments are all very positive which is cool, but I still would like to learn more from Japanese influence.

well, hemm, i thinking Western coloring techniques have many layers and uses more effort. the japanese style tend to use three kind of tones for the skin and cloths and 4 four for shiny things. of course its the anime that's been using this technique the most. If anything, i like hellboy originality, using one color. The comic industry has involve from dot printing to smooth laser printing

I wasn't evolution. It was revolution. When Marvel's six top artist's(Todd McFarlane, Jim Lee, Rob Liefeld, Marc Silvestri,Eric Larson, and Jim Valentino.) jumped ship in 1992, and formed Image Comics, they introduced computer color into the mainstream. Their comics, drawn by the most saught after artists, and sporting obviously superior production values sold in the millions! Marvel and DC Comics were forced to dispense with their decades old printing techniques and completely retool in order to compete with a new demand for more lavish printing and paper. It's true most change the comic industry is downright glacial, but color was revolutionized practically overnight.
awww incredibly nice She's one of those characters appearing in just a few eps, but becoming really popular (someone said Kaworu in Evangelion?)
'Cept that they killed Kaworu and still had the ending ruined by a mysterious ethereal glowing girl. (Thanks for the comment. )
details? (lol)

While I'm not a big fan of Gundam (at least until Tomino got awesome-happy in Turn-A, and later King Gainer), I've always liked Lalah. I don't know if she was an influence on my OC's, it would look that way but to be fair she never came to my mind back then.
Interesting insight into your source of inspiration. Me, I'm all about Universal Century continuity Gundam.(MSG, 0080, 0083,Chars Counterattack, 08MST!!!) If it ain't UC, It don't count to me.

Agreed. I never really liked the feel of any of the other Gundam universes...well, except G gundam, but that was mostly because of HOW insane it was (Anything with giant windmill robots from Holland has to be given some slack).
Preach on bro.
I'm so looking forward to fighting her in Dynasty Warriors: Gundam 2.
Dammit, that was supposed to be a [link] .

A new Gundam game? Let me know if it's any good. I've been let down by so many of them. Actually, has there ever been a mecha game that surpassed Armored Core: Master of the Arena? (And don't say Halo, unless they've made a new version of it where you can swap out master chief's legs for caterpillar treads.)

Well, if you're into strategy RPGs (SRPGs) in the vein of Final Fantasy Tactics™, there's always been the Front Mission™ series by Square Enix, of the same FFT fame. And if you want some over-the-top animated mecha genre-bending "Street Fighter" action, look for Tech Romancer™ by Capcom.

Excellent picture of Lalah. We don't get enough pictures of her in the uniform, it is always in dress mode. I know how you feel about the Elmeth, it has a strange design that doesn't seem right. Looks stupid just sitting there, but take it into action and it's fierce!
I just started watching Gundam Unicorn. I should probably start adding to this series.

I endorse this idea, it should be done! Gundam Unicorn has a few subjects for this series, so you should become inspired. Also coming out sometime in the next 12 months is MS Gundam the Origin, the facelift of the original MS Gundam.
COOL! I am really enjoying Yamato 2199 which is the same thing for Space Battleship Yamato.

It should turn out to be something like that, yes. The original Gundam the Origin manga was created by the character & mecha designers of the first Gundam series. It showcased some ideas for mecha & story details that were not originally adapted, as well as filling out some of the background information that was published afterwards. I am really looking forward to the anime.

title: Ifurita
description: Back to basement mining. This is a very old picture I drew of Ifurita from the old "El Hazard" OVA. As this was before my descent into photoshop dependence, I drew this one in colored pencil. To the probable majority of you to whom El Hazard is "suma dat old school stuff," I recommend you give it a view. It's that rare anime that in spite of the fact that it has time travel, alternate dimensions, and world destroying, glowing goddesses, (Pictured above.) it still manages to have a satisfying conclusion that actually MAKES SENSE! Like I said--very rare indeed.

comments.body: Fine piece !
Thank you.

hemm, actually, your a bit right on that part. i have watch the magnificent world 1 and 2, and the Alternative world. i always like the Priestess bathhouses. some good scenes over there. But more the fact that they had a perverted princess with an underage lover. And i say, for a color pencil pic, you sure choose very bright colors.but it really works. Mind telling us how you plan the shadows and all before shading in the color pencils?
You do an underdrawing in brown for warm areas, and blue for cool areas.
i see, meaning the flesh gets the warm colors and the cloth cool colors? hemm, im not sure my interpretation is correct, but i think i have to learn this myself

More or less. Here, let me speak geek for a moment: Warm Colors are the colors of Sith light sabre blades--red, yellow, orange; Cool Colors are the color of Jedi light sabre blades-- green, blue, and violet. (That's right, Mace Windau's blade falls in the correct part of the spectrum.) Star Wars helped me through color theory. (Star Blazers helped me through astronomy. )

I love those colors- and Ifurita's looking great. I'm more of a Shayla-Shayla fan myself, but comfort myself that even though she didn't get Makoto, that means that some other dimensional jumper has a chance with her.
Heh, you said jumper.
Destro, is that you? I thought I told you to go clean out the Viper pit!
I'll deal with the Baroness later! We've got world domination to plan!

You did this in colored pencil? Unreal. I have a hard enough time controlling the graphite smear on my normal pencils. I can't even imagine the amount of effort and skill that goes into making something this pretty. This looks like *days* of labor.

You know at this point, I wish I could roll out and say, "Oh really? No it took me ten minutes, with one hand. The other was busy undoing the straps on my model's bra." But yea, it did take days of labor. Colored pencil looks great when you do it right, but doing it right takes commitment and planning.

Art in the physical medium seems to becoming a lost skill over time. I have a few commissions from people who still do art with good old fashion pens/markers/pencils and colored pencils, but with the advent of Photoshop and Painter, it seems like everyone has moved on into the digital realm. I enjoy both, though it's really a shame that we've lost the former in favor of the latter. I admit though that I can't stand working in some mediums. Charcoal drawing gives me conniption fits.
gee, you are so fast amazing coloring. i like the style of older animes
Really I'd like to be faster. That's a way cute avatar, incidentally.
you are very fast anyways, whats the hurry? you got the 'live fast, die young' motto as well? thanksss
wanna see a lot more coloured work! I lovvveee it!^^
Thanks for the love.

I would like to see you draw this again, using the same medium (colored pencils), but this time giving Ifurita the evil treatment ala Sephiroth at Nibelheim. She was portrayed as a demoness after all, and similar to an Asian belief (forget which at the moment) that gods and higher spirits had peaceful as well as wrathful aspects, I think the proverbial "other cheek" would like a good turn right about now.
Hmmm, sounds like it would be good practice. I may just take you up on that.
Ok you are INSANE!!!!! Sorry but this is just heart stopping, jaw dropping good. My word your pictures always leave me craving more.

Yea I know it does take some work to color in pencil. A friend of mine told me there are color lead sticks for layering color over wide areas. I'm going to have to check those out, before really do go insane, working with regular sized pencils.
lovely piece!^^ Loved El hazard
Thanks for remembering.
This is the best Ifurita I've ever seen! Hnnnngh!
Thank you.

title: DMV: A New Breed
description: Oh, color this time. This was supposed to be the penultimate introduction strip, capturing that, "The Navy Calls it 'Fighter Weapons School'. The fliers call it..." sort of moment. I guess this would be for the Sunday paper.

comments.body: I likes the parking meter badge.

Thanx, it took me a while before I was happy with the badge design. I always loved how elmblematic the badges were on the old Galaxy Rangers animated series, so I wanted to design something similarly symbollic, but comedic at the same time.

You *watched* the Galaxy Rangers™?! [cue fangirl squeal] I always thought they were the most badass cartoon produced in the 80s, without trying too hard to be badass that it became lame. Well, with the exception of Doc Hartford, but that's just me.

Yea, Rangers wrawct! I do wish Doc Hartford was even 1% more badass though. He was always bumbling around, and he had the lamest power. He was the Ranger's equivalent of the heart kid from Captain Planet. Heck, it was his voice that sang the theme song, but in the Rock Concert episode they have Shane sing it! Doc got no love. Hmmmm. Drawing idea: Niko, in a girl's version of Shane's black "Galaxy Stranger" Uniform. Gotta remember that.

title: DMV: The Balding Blade
description: I'm sorry to have to post this strip so heinously out of sequence. I thought it was lost to the mists of time. Turns out it was just under the dust of the basement. Happily, I found it while looking for something else. This was supposed to be the big moment where Blading Blade announces his famous cliche before claiming the "prize"--er--parking space.

comments.body: Ah, there's the missing scene. I like how the guy's snarl transfers from the right side of his face to the left side.
Eh, nobody's perfect. That's doubly true for artists.
I actually meant it as a compliment. Some would pundit the facial movement, but I think it's a nice touch. It makes the comic look more animated that way.

title: Cold Weather Gear
description: After watching WAY too much Trigun I designed badass duster jackets and ticket rifles for my heroic meter maids. I don't know if it works to have them look so cool though. There's a little more humor in having them fight road raging hordes in short shorts.

comments.body: well, There are some designs that even skirt can be warm in winter

I'll take your word for it. Whenever I see women standing on those windswept subway platforms in the dead of winter with skirts on, long or short, (Especially short. Brrrr!) I am overwhelmed with sympathy. I likes me some gams, but I can wait 'til the spring.
true, but if they wearing one of those long panty hose, that might be interesting.
Those are actually warm? I find that hard to believe.

haha, japanese wear their long skirts during these season, and some with bolder look, skirts with long spats(i think)im not sure myself. Im still envy my sister and brother because they got to visit the land of the rising sun.

Yeah, but a common practice is to also wear their gym pants under their skirts; only tucked up so you can't see them so they can still meet the dress code. They don't do that because the weather is warm, my friend. :3
yeah yeah i was refering to what they wear for casual.but sokay
HAH! when I saw the thumb before clicking on it I thought "Man that looks like something out of Trigun" at least I'm not totally losing my mind ^^ Vash is my hero

Hmm that reminds me. Have you noticed all the good westerns are coming from anywhere but the US? Cartoon Westerns are done by the Japanese (Case in point, the aforementioned Trigun), Western comic books are done by the Europeans. Even Modern Western movies like 3:10 to Yuma star English and Australian actors. I really find that odd. Well the was that excellent sci-fi western Firefly, but they were in all kinds of a hurry to cancel that.

yah, and created by Whedon no less. Considering the rest of his work I found it a little odd. And that's very true! i could mention Brokeback Mountain, but we're talking about *good* westerns so ignore that ^^ I miss Trigun...I hope they don't muck up the movie too badly
They're making a movie? Please tell me its an animated movie and not some horrifying Hollywood cash in. (I still wake up screaming at the thought of DiCaprio's Akira.)

.....Please tell me the DiCraprio Akira is a joke. Yah it's been in the works since he announced it in 2006. Madhouse is making it, animated, and all the people working on it are well known. Google Trigun Movie and the first post has most of the info. I'm excited ^^
I'm glad to hear that the Trigun is animated. I wish I was joking about the Akira thing though. [link] I just hope it gets lost in "development hell."
...I don't even have the words.
OMG you aren't kidding!! Why Akira, WHY???
Because they like the sound we make when we scream.
I dunno, there's just a lot of silence on my end. they like the horrified expression I gave the computer. DBZ...Akira...when will it end...

Well then they probably just like yo watch you squirm. (If you bear any resemblance to your avatar, I wouldn't mind seeing that myself. But I digress.) Sadly, it will only end when they stop making money. Ironically, that'll be about the time they start actually making good movies.
nah, I'm blond ^^ [link] gave up being a red head loooong ago They'll make a good one some day.... *so I'm naive, oh well*

Hah, you weren't kidding when you said you loved Vash. Curious thing, I was flirting with the idea of making AJ's partner female. (Since I'm not yet published, I can change the cannon at will. I know it doesn't necessarily mean I should, but I can. ) Anyway, these pictures are a strong argument in favor of a female Drew.

o.o You realize if you were to do that I'd be a brat and go around saying "She was inspired by MOI!" like the devil I am. And yah, I adore Vash. I had a photoshoot in San Diego with this costume and wanted to make it as realistic as possible, so I went through the pain of supergluing his metal stitches so I wouldn't have any "accidents" [link]
So no wardrobe malfunction. Boy, you really went all out for this costume. I was already impressed by the jacket.

I didn't want to half-ass it. Cosplaying makes you look like a freak to begin with, I might as well be a god among the freaks^^ ...That wasn't meant to sound snotty though it kind of did. The buttons on the jacket were a bitch.
I dunno. "A god among freaks" sound pretty fun. And instead of half-assing it, you're half-chesting it. (Pun -1,000 HP) Okay, I'll stop.
HAH! yah that might be a good idea hehe

This really gives them a wild west feel, which in think helps the mood of the whole project. It's pretty telling that the world of parking garage law enforcement is a dangerous and untamed frontier. I mean, they're even wearing chaps. :3 For some reason, AJ's coworker there reminds me of Egon from Ghostbusters, with the light hair and the glasses.
I was thinking more Vash. But Egon works too.

title: DMV: Enter the Heroine
description: Enter AJ, fully grown and at the height of her powers. The two former combatants cower obsequiously, confronted by the threat of debt. (I imagine her "Dragonball Z" eyepiece should win me a few more props for bein' all "retro". )

comments.body: Haha, The 'Scouts' are in season again.
The Dragonball thing you mention. Its called "Scouts' if i recall.
Hmm, learn something new every day.

They're called 'Scouters' to be exact. A funny play off the ol DBZ could be had here. They could measure a person's parking debt based off a central database lookup, in which case you can rebirth the oft played '...it's over nine-thousand!' or simply have the eyepiece self destruct.
well, scout was use in the dub version of the DB manga, so yeah, different region. haha, over nine thousand is soo over rated
Ha! That's an awesome idea!

title: DMV: There Can Be Only Wha?
description: In the heroic nick of time, the Balding Blade is prevented from beheading Shades Wearing Business Suit Guy by having his Dragon's Head Katana plucked from his hand by an expertly launched adhesive parking ticket. Who could be behind this? Aw come on, the movie opened in a parking garage! You really think I was going to let that lie?

comments.body: Thank gods you didn't make her stick her pinky out as she held that pistol. What do you call that sidearm, by the way, the "Violator"? I've already seen your pacing and dynamism at Marilith, so I'm very confident with your storyboarding.
The pistol is called a Dispenser. I wanted an ironic, innocuous sounding name. (Although, oddly you're by no means the first person to suggest "Violator.")

I'm curious how the gun works, considering that you usually take the time to think about your weapon designs. It's hard to figure out how such a wide shot comes out of a tiny barrel on the sidearm. Also, is that a clip or an ink cartridge?
I was always considering a clip full of tightly folded tickets that are printed upon as the exit the muzzle, but your suggestion of an ink cartridge is downright inspiring. May have to reconsider.

Well, I figured it was loosely based on the tagging guns that are used so commonly in retail stores. The 'ammo' is a roll of adhesive price tags and you dial in number on the gun for the price. One motion takes care of both the printing and the fixture of the tag. In this case, her scouter eyepiece could scan a license plate number or facial registry ID and lookup previous infractions and the transmit updated data to the gun, which then takes care of the printing and the delivery of said ticket. But I'm still curious how those things get out of the barrel without jamming it. o.O

title: DMV: Dueling Swords
description: Flash forward ( Past all those years of pesky charcater developemnt.) to the present. The most valuable thing in the DMV not-too-distant-cyberpunk-dystopian-future is a 6 x 3 meter rectangle of road surface watched over by a cycloptic parking meter and delineated by two inch wide lines of paint. These simple slivers of territory are easily worth a man's life --especially during rush hour! When the spaces become scarce, ritualized combat begins.

comments.body: Ah, Toledo steel versus Tokyo katana... Doood... ritualized combat over parking space. Food, water, natural resources, I can understand, but parking space. Makes you wonder if anyone helps the old lady across the street anymore... As always, love your ideas and your art!

The thing is, you can earn your share or food water and natural resources--you just have to show up on time for work. Thus the demand for parking space. And believe me, the combat is going to get a lot uglier. I'll tell you about old ladies later.

title: DMV: Whale Tale
description: Young AJ notices the brake lights aren't even lit on the classic IROCZ Camaro, as it peels away from nearly crushing the young girl beneath its wheels. She memorizes its distinctive aftermarket "Whale Tail" spoiler, (Bet you were wondering what the title of this post had to do with the strip. ) to recall later in the inevitable rematch, and looks in speechless awe upon her positive black role model. A meter maid is born.

comments.body: "That car's behind was this big..." Now why is it that any reference to a body's rear end always makes me think of that song "Baby Got Back" by Sir Mix-A-Lot?? Dayum. Action lines!

I was talking to a friend earlier this week about military jets, and that song came in my head when I thought the F-15 Eagle. Since its engines are so ungodly huge for a fighter plane, they had to build these big ol' bumps into its rear fuslage. Coupled with the subtle hour glass shape of its area rule supersonic airframe, the Eagle always reminded me of a woman with a big ol' padonkabomp rump. [link] So you're not the only one. Of course that could just mean we both need therapy.

title: DMV: The Rescue
description: Young AJ is deftly plucked from the front bumper of death by a quick thinking meter maid. (Technical Note: To help ground this in a previous era, I decided to give the older meter maid, actual wheels on her scooter.) I think you see where this is all going.

comments.body: yeah, still loving the scooter.
Still appreciating the love.

title: DMV: Killer Camaero
description: Last week a curious poster asked if I could show him some actual narrative art to some of these high concepts I've been so liberally hurling about. Thanks to my celebrated bachelor's inability to throw away anything, I am able to oblige. While I do have pages in the traditional comic book format, I thought it would be fun to start out with this origin story for the character AJ. Be advised, this is back when I was crazy enough to try this out as a comic strip This first strip shows a seminal moment in AJ's childhood, when she wandered into the mighty Megarage and was menaced by an antique Camaro IROCZ. ( Keen eyes will notice the mass of unpaid tickets accumulated on the car's windshield.) Those decrying child endangerment please calm down. As you can see, AJ's deliverance is at hand.

comments.body: did this run as a Newspaper comic strip or something? the action scene looks good. And i love the scooter.

It would be great if it did. I can tell you with absolute certainty, newspaper comic syndicates have no interest in action/adventure comics. The last new action comic that was of any worth was Gil Kane's Star Hawks and that was printed in 1979! If you want a comic strip with action. (Or original thought, or intelligent humor for that matter.) you have to look online. Glad you liked the scooter.

i see, but in my country, spiderman still running, even brenda starr. aldo most of the other comics like Garfield, Hagar the horrible, and the Tiger and the kid strip, There are some local comic strip with action, but i myself dont read it, why? because we gotta buy the newspaper to read, and that a real pain. And other thing, if you start reading it in the midle, it doesnt make sense.

Yea the only action strips are holdovers from the golden age of comics like Dick Tracy, and Brenda Starr: Reporter. But even those are watered far down from their former glory. Have you ever read early Dick Tracy? In the first week, crooks kidnap his fiance', shoot her father in the chest, steal his life savings, and sucker punch Dick with a billy club when he tries to intercede. (This all happens on the page, no chickenshit cutting away or hinting at violence!) When Dick comes to, he surveys the wreckage of his life and swears a bitter oath on his fiance's father's corps that he will become the scourge of crime. (Yea, I know, it sounds all Batmanny. Hey, it was the 1930's. A lot of people were swearing vengeful oaths against crime back then. Every other alley was full of kneeling dudes, cradling a recently deceased loved one in one hand and raising their free hand toward the heavens begging the almighty for the power to lay their enemies low. You couldn't sleep with all the oaths being belted out.) Anyway, if someone approached a modern comic syndicate with Dick Tracy's exact premise and events, he'd be shown the door. Newspaper comics are in a sad state, in my opinion.
tough business. better of with a one shot manga.
Probably right.
An origin story? My, my!

Yup, of course I've since decided to change the car from the Camaro to the fictional Red ICE, (Still working on the name, but I want the letters ICE in it to signify Internal Combustion Engine. ) That way, I avoid any potential licensing issues with General Motors, and I get to have fun making up another car.
Heh. Clever. You seem to have a lot of car concepts. Have you ever seen any of those other retro-futuristic designs - the dream cars of the 40s and 50s?

I'd be legitimately surprised to meet anyone who didn't have at least a passing familiarity with the bold concept cars of that era. Cars such as the infamous Lincoln Futura. (And if you can't tell me why that particular car is famous, you have to turn in your fan girl membership card.) I have one minor point to make. Forgive me, it pains me to respond your endearing conversational overture with an anal-retentive correction, but I'm afraid that nerd OCD has taken control of my typing hand. "Retro-Future" is a term used by modern designers to describe designs that are inspired by the aforementioned concept vehicles. Cars of the 1950's, many of with were inspired by jet aircraft, were described at the time as simply being "futuristic." Retro-futurism harks back to past generations' visions of the future. But anyway, yes I am familiar with Art-Deco, Jet-Age and Modernist design.
