title: Golden Boyfriend
description: Pictured above are evil prom queen Lori, and OttØ, her motorcycle riding bad boy boyfriend bot. (Alliteration x4 combo! ) As you can tell, this is one of many variations1 I experimented with our resident mechanical bully. I based this version's face loosely on Gort from "The Day the Earth Stood Still.2" You'll note that the top of his brain case has a paisley pattern. This is to simulate the bandanna that all worthless teenage reprobates are required to wear 3. The rest of his chasis is electroplated with gold as the ultimate expression of "bling.4" I never quite figured out OttØ's backstory. Is he a high-end military prototype, expertly programmed to piss off the fathers of rich young women and steal the lunch money from weak young men.? Is he teenage boy, disfigured in a horrible accident? Is he teenage boy who had his brain swapped into a killer robot chasis beacuse he thought it would be a "bitchin' thing to do"? Or is he simply a mass produced personal computer/cell phone, like out of Chobits ? No one possibility has stuck out to me as the most fun, so for now I simply work from the fact he's a menacing mechanical miscreant (Moar Alliteration! Gimme pointz!) who is inexplicably enrolled in a private high school5. Also, he has gyroscopic equilibrium, so he can strut in perfectly simulated slow motion while blasting badass music on his internal sound system. Oh, and lastly, he never leaves Lori's side6. Loriand OttØ expend much energy in cynical attempts to prove how little they care about each other. (Protesting too strongly, as is the cliche'. ) As a couple, they're very much like The Baroness and Destro; only--you know-- in high school. SELF-INDULGENT FOOTNOTES 1.) I'm guessing this would be "OttØ 5.0. 2.) Not the Keanu Reeves version! 3.) It's part of the bad boy code--look it up. 4.) Something he thought was cool, until the ever maligning Lori started calling him "C3P0"! 5. Yea, yea I saw Cromartie High , but I'm hoping girls will find OttØ sexier than Mechazawa. 6 Well, mostly never. there are alot of other pretty girls around, and like an FA-18, OttØ can track 24 targets at once!

comments.body: Haha. So, basically, Golden boy [link] im soo clueless of this characters. maybe i need a short synopsis on emm

It's from a defunct old project set in a futuristic high school, ruled absolutely by an evil shrew and her minions. The various characters are spread about my gallery. I invite you to peruse at your convenience and comment, should you feel so inclined.
sure, i am visitting each one. Funny how i stumble upon this gallery Actually, i have been searching some of your works, but i only found a few. well, didnt stop me much
Glad you did. I've been enjoying your comments.
She looks so mean (and HOT!!!) ahhh memories of high school.... (unfortunately I wasn't the Otto type who scored with Lories :'( ) Great work!
I hear ya. Me, I was a permanent resident of "Friendzoneopolis."

Say, now that you've added tones... is Lori one of those kogal types?? Hmm, the way those two items just behind Ott0's brain case is asymmetrical in direction, I'd think they were movable sensors of some kind. Excellent attention to detail, as per the usual, and as per the usual, an unusual yet fresh twist in your concepts. I love seeing new submissions from you after a hard night's day answering calls in tech support. <3

I hadn't divined their purpose exactly, they were meant to mimmick the fin-like folds you get when you tie on a Bandana. Since they follow the back of his head I guess they could be a means to allow him to check out other women when he's hanging out with Lori. On a side note, voyeurs no longer sell candid snapshots of girls in this future. They priavtely broadcast videos in exhange for electronic fund transfers. Very convenient for one with the future equivalent of wi-fi in his head. (I imagine Lori is going to have to start carrying a signal scrmbler--Not that she cares what Ott0 is looking at.) I'm not familiar with the term Kogal, but if it has anything to do with a spoiled girl who sports a punctiliously maintained 365 day tan, in its own way every bit as vain and artificial as Ott0's auric veneer, then yea Lori's a kogal. (BTW, cancer was wiped out a generation prior to the beginning of the story so smoking and recreational tanning are rampant in the future.)
great couple, I hate them already
Odd thing is, these were the characters I most enjoyed coming up with ideas for among the characters in the romance concept. The people folks were supposed to like--[i]bored the hell outta me![/i]
Yep, I can see your point !
lol, I love that backstory. Very unique and quirky. Real cool.
Thanks,
Gosh, Otto is made of bling. He's 90% bling, in fact. Does he have a label to that effect on the back of his neck, as per dolls and 'Made in China'?
Nah, it would probably say something to the tune of "Property of Lori Richmond." She'd place it there when he wasn't paying attention.
What sort of regulations are there on robots in high school? What about being a foreign exchange student?

I'm taking a page from "Cromartie High's" Mechazawa, and saying there are no regulations. But I reserve the right to make up some if they add spice to the story. I will say that there is a separate class meant for robots called Hum-Ed or "Humanity Education." I get this idea from watching all those episodes of Star Trek TNG in which Mr. Data stumbles his way about in clumsy, futile attempts to grasp the subtleties of human interaction--never getting the joke, unable to call a bluff, or act on a "gut feeling." I figure if robots are integrated into society to the point that they're dating prom queens, an effort would be made to formally educate them in the particulars of human customs. The robots in my story however would have long ago adapted to human interaction, so Hum-ED class would be a redundant exercise in which human teachers condescendingly tell the robots in attendance information they already know. Teacher: "Today class, we are discussing the concept of the "Joke." A "joke" is a story or anecdote whose purpose is to elicit laughter. Can anyone here give me an example of a "joke"?" OttØ: "Yea, this class." In addition to the bored teen-aged robots, passing flash-encrypted transmissions that poke fun of the teacher's poorly matched follicle-swap, human students are attending HuM-Ed class. Most of them are the school nerds, hoping the tips on human interaction will help them improve their social lives.
There's something I really like about this picture...hmmm I just can't put my finger on it- Oh yeah! The whole pic!

Glad you like it. Usually I like war stories, or super-hero stories--which are basically war stories, except all the combatants are wearing tights. After watching Peach Girl I came to realize that soap operas were actually really vicious war stories in which the combatants were school girls and the contested territory was social standing! "It has been said that social occasions are only warfare concealed. " Khan Noonian Singh That's what sort of got me on the idea of doing something like a romance. I have to admit I found the prototypical bad girl waayy more interesting, and so Lori was born. And yes, I do watch entirely too much Military Channel on cable.
Yeah I noticed that too about soap operas. My mom watches them enough to where I can't help but notice
He's hot!
Thanks. *Lori stares jealous daggers at you, and plots your fate.*
*notices Lori* <__< I-I think I'm gonna, um, walk over here...away from you...far away from you. *slowly walks off screen*

*You receive a Site-to-Sight text message on your contact lenses. (Contact lenses are only used for communications now, since optical surgery is mandatory.)* Ott0: "Wink, wink, baby." (As you probably guessed, Ott0 can fire off such messages at will from his internal comm array--even as he assures Lori that he's not checking out your a$$. )
(lol oh my~ Me like Ott0 xD) *I leave the room, but come back up to the door and hold my hand up to my ear like a telephone. My lips read "Call Me" then I bolt off giggling like a school girl* xD
You're fun, but you know robots are only after one thing.
Science projects! xD ;]

Yea, science projects. That's the ticket. *Later, as you sit in the locker room getting ready for gym, you feel a cold presence behind you, accompanied by a colder voice, "You know, Ott0's sensorium is actually tactical grade. He can track an estimated 200 independent targets of a predetermined profile simultaneously, and disseminate unique telemetry to each one. And those are just his market specs. So I suppose you could say he was designed to have a roving eye. Me? I'm only human. I don't have all that, so what that means is I can only track one target at a time. But that target gets all my attention."*

*I'm startled a bit by the presence. I turn around to face the woman behind me. "Oh, I uh didn't mean to intrude on your target. Female instinct tends to take over when someone has "interest" in me. It uh doesn't happen often...hahaaa..." I laugh awkwardly and close my gym locker, which suddenly pops back open. This time I slam it close and give her a fake smile. Geez, her presence nerves me.*

*Lori looks you up and down with undisguised disdain. "It uh, doesn't happen often? No surprise there. And just so you know, it will never happen here." The prom queen walks past you without waiting for a response. As you try to shake of the afternoon's unexpected chill during the long walk home, your thoughts are drowned out by the unmistakable whine of the drive turbine of a recumbent electricycle. Ott0 is at the wheel, and Lori is conspicuously absent from her usual perch on the passenger seat? "Goin' my way?"
* My reaction was probably a dead give away. There was an awesome bike and an Ott0 without a Lori...and he asked if I'm going his way? Respond you idiot! Respond! "Uh, y-yes! I am actually." *

"Well hop on. And hold on tight." *You sit behind Ott0 on his fancy steed, and wrap your arms around his literally chiseled frame. You are surprised to feel that his metallic carapace is actually warm beneath his school uniform. A second later the wind is slapping your face and your thighs are vibrating with the banshee scream of the electricycle's ravenous motor.* "Where to, babe?"

*I've never been on an electricycle before. I wrap my arms tighter around him, hoping my petite body doesn't fly off the bike. "Just the next street over!" I try to speak up over the sound of the electricycle.*

*It's a good thing you tightened your grip, because suddenly Ott0 veers the electricycle to the right. The machine is well balanced, and Ott0 is running top-notch driving subroutines, but you're still unprepared for the sudden motion, and you almost wrench your neck. It's too soon for your turn, so you look around for the cause of the unexpected motion. In the next instant, a cherry red Pegasus zooms into view, big as life. The crimson coupe passes through the area of space occupied by Ott0's cycle, scant seconds ago. Either the bad boy robot saw the machine in his rear view mirror, or his proximity detectors alerted him to its approach. You don't have to look in the driver's seat to know who's behind the wheel of the hovering projectile. Everyone in school knows its the prized possession of that douche bag Patrick. However, from your vantage point behind Ott0, you notice a stream of lilting blonde hair from the occupant of the passenger seat. "Oh, this shit definitely does..not..compute!" Ott0 guns the motor of his bike. The 1.2 Megawatt battery obediently surrenders raw voltage into the electricycle's roiling turbine and vehicle rockets in pursuit. You thought you were going fast before.*

*I cling to Ott0 even tighter and bury my head into his back for a brief moment. My head then moves to the side of him and I see the cherry red vehicle. Isn't that, Patrick's? I then briefly noticed the girl in the passenger seat. And is that...? I hear Ott0 speak and then... "Eek!" He suddenly guns the eletricycle and hold onto him for dear life. Holy crap! How fast does this thing go!?*

You leave your intersection and your home far behind as you pursue Patrick and Lori down the road. After a few more nerve racking miles, the coupe flashes its "up" blinker and climbs effortlessly into the sky where the electricycle can't follow. Ott0 slows down as he tracks the trajectory of the repellor car. When the car banks out of sight behind a distant parkology the robot brings his own vehicle to a stop.Showcasing the functional subtleties of a high end synthetic sentience, he talks to himself. "Lori hates that jerk almost as much as I do. She's totally just trying to make me jealous."

I frown and loosen my grip as he speaks. "M-maybe it's best if I just walk." I don't want to because I really like being in Ott0's presence, but being around him was just ruining his relationship with Lori. "Lori really doesn't like me being around you."

*Ott0 steps off his bike, and walks over to you.* "So it's not all about you is it? I don't see that attitude much in humans--or machines for that matter." *He leans over and gives you a peck on the forehead.* "Why couldn't I have fallen for someone like you?" *The gold robot in the boy's school uniform gets back on his electricycle and peels out. You notice a litany of air foils and exhaust vectors deploy, changing the profile of the small vehicle to something even meaner. Relieved of a human occupant, Ott0 is able to really open up his steed. He bolts off with a speed that makes your previous harrowing ride look like slow motion. But why is he heading back in the direction of the school?*

Or he could be the creation of a madscientist, oh say the Mad Doctor Killigan, who wanted to create the ultimate robot killing machine. But one day his no good thug of a nephew, Otto, snuck into his lab with then girlfriend to make out with his gthen grilfriend. But due to the usual series of convoluted anime style circumstances he wound up merged with the killing machine, becoming the ultimate bad boy school bully and good girl magnet.
I just wanted to say, that is a GREAT idea. I've got to think on this.
So, the 'schoolgirl' costume on the left? It's to die for. It's certainly no uniform I've ever seen - and I absolutely adore it.
Glad to hear that. With so very many school girl uniforms to choose from in anime/manga, is next to impossible to design something that hasn't been seen before.

title: T-Bird
description: Last picture for this week is of the character Yeager from 801TTS Airbats. Yeager was pilot for the USAF Thunderbirds, the Air Force's aerial demonstration team. Actually while I'm on the topic, and while I've got more or less a world venue in which to vent my curiosity, I have a question to pose to knowlegeable fighter fans. Years ago, I saw a documentary comparing the T-Birds with the Blue Angels. During said documentary, one of thr T-Bird drivers betrayed some envy that the Angel drivers were pushing two engine aircraft around. That got me thinking. If twin engined aircraft are superior to their single engine counterparts, and the T-Birds are supposed to demonstrate the best the USAF has to offer, why are the birds driving F-16's instead of F-15 Eagles? Does anyone have an answer for this?

comments.body: The reason the Thunderbirds fly F-16s is because the F-15 is more expensive, more complex and more maintenance intensive. The F-16 is relatively cheaper, simpler and easier to maintain. Plus the F-16's design makes it much more maneuverable in the low altitude, low speed regime (which is where most of the Manuvers they demonstrate take place); the F-15 handles better at high altitude around the +400-ish knot regime. Plus, since tubofans perform much better at sea level, the thrust is enough to give the lightly-loaded F-16s a Thrust to weight ratio of parity or beter.
Wow, that was prompt. Thanks for the info, friend. BTW, looking at your avatar, you might want to check out this picture. [link]
Yeah, I fav'd that a week or two ago...
Man, I thought I must have been damn near the only person to have seen that show. Sweet pic.
Yea, I lament the fact that we see less and less hardware heavy anime these days.
I only saw the first three eps of the 801st TTS, and that was because I somehow found a VCD of it in Malaysia for fifteen ringgit. Still got the disc too but no VCD player now.

Talk of Thunderbirds and Angels makes me think of the "Supermarionation" series of the 60s, Thunderbirds: [link]) and Captain Scarlet: [link] Oh, and the Beach boys. "She'll have fun, fun, fun, 'til her daddy takes the t-bird away". /geek

Nah for me whenever I hear "Thunderbirds", I'm a kid again sitting atop my father's shoulders in the memorial weekend heat on the crowded flight line at Andrew's AFB, straining to get a better view of the pilots as they climb into their T-38 Talons. Yea, I'm dating myself. It's been a while since they flew those.

title: Street Sweeper Antoinette
description: Another original character. Sorry, she's got nothing to do with the High School romance. Or anything else really. I just felt like drawing a French maid with combat boots, a sniper rifle, and a flying car. I could see her in a movie preview: Panel One: Antoinette stands at a skyscraper window with a rifle at her shoulder. (No sniper would actually stand to take a shot, but how else are we going to show off her gams?) Panel Two: Scope-eye view of a distant office widow. The gun sight reticle rests squarely on the head of some overweight, vaguely Italian looking mug. Narrator: This maid does do windows. Panel Three: Close up on Antoinette's face as she pulls the trigger. SFX: BANG!!! Narrator: And takes out the trash! HIT MAID!!! I have no idea what the story would be though.

The interesting thing that always struck me about this particular picture was that the gentleman's face imprint reminded me somehow of Adam Warren's work. It's hard to pin point exactly, but the burliness of the chin and the use of hatching reminds me of his technique. Hatching isn't something you do very much, your lines tend to be super crisp and animation cel-like. Do you ever use hatching as a shading style in other pictures? It might be an interesting experiment of organic pencils in your technical style.
I'd hate to see what she whips out when you park on the *Disabled* space!
I've got to find the pics of the ticket rifles now.
Ah! I remember this drawing now! Wow, you've been sitting on this idea a long while Just like everything, looks fantastic!

Thanks for the kind words. Yes, I've been sitting on a lot of ideas for quite some time. I think most artists have those "my baby" projects waiting in the wings of their portfolio for the publishers and the public to be "ready for them." I find it amusing, with all the unpublished concepts and all the brilliant minds, languishing in various forms of obscurity, that the established publishers always seem to struggle for new ideas. There are radical ideas and original characters populating the cheap booths in the back of every convention, big and small, yet publishers still insist upon changing the color of a super-hero's tights and calling it revolutionary. Par for the course I suppose.

*laughs* aren't you glad you have weirdos like me to push ya to get those ideas out? *laughs* I know what you mean about really good ideas being wasted because the artists can't catch a break. I see it every year at Wondercon. Lots of fantastic artists with great ideas that are trying to get them off the ground and no one gives them a shot because they're afraid to take a risk. The comic book industry really has become a corporate business hasn't it?

title: Evil Prom Queen
description: Back to the high school romance project. Pictured above is the inevitable queen bee, Lori: A young dainty flower whose remarkable physical beauty forever rescues her from the burdens of empathy or compassion. Lori glides through her average school day, effortlessly utilizing the collective fear and admiration of her classmates like the tools of a master inquisitor. The social standing of the student body, ( As well as the faculty.) exist at her whim. Lori and the evil quarterback, Patrick, coexist tenuously, like dons of rival mobs. She also maintains an on again /off again liaison with the school's resident robot bad boy, OttØ. At least, until the equally inevitable normal girl shows up---

title: Space Girls
description: No you're not in the wrong gallery. I did draw this picture. It's yet another prehistoric piece of art that I wrested from the grasp of the silverfish in my basement. Some pieces are older than others. I drew this with colored pencil, and just enough photoshop airbrush to give it that sleazy veneer. These are even more OC's that have yet to see the benefit of print. They are GELF's; Genetically Engineered Life Forms created for the rigorous task of piloting combat space robots. (Can't you tell? Why else would they look like blue skinned strippers with pointy ears?) Anyway, one is good, questioning the need to shoot folks down and bomb stuff, and the other is evil, liking nothing better than to dance to the symphony of screams that she passionately conducts. Bet'cha can't tell which is which,

I looked into it and it doesn't appear to be a fake. The clip is a large capacity drum magazine for 5.45mm RPK-74 Kalashnikov light machine gun. what serie is?

Holy cow, feedback! How'd that happen? Thank you for, asking but I'm afraid this isn't a series. This was something I was proposing to Tokyopop®, before they tragically went belly up. (The series was likely not to get a green light anyway, as it was felt my character designs were to sexy for the teen girls audience they were trying to attract. ) Anyway the story is, or rather was, Raised by Robots. A high school romance in a school in which teenagers attended school side by side with robots. In the future, artificial intelligence is so advanced that it is deemed to hard to program complex human social interaction into robots on a mass production scale. Robots coming out of factories are either hopelessly naive, like Data from Star Trek, or dangerously crazy like Skynet. The only solution is to get machines accustomed to interacting with humans is to send them to school. Of course, in addition to their other lessons, some of them fall in love. The characters: (Assuming you're still interested after that infodump. ) Trina: (This is actually the character pictured above--I just redesigned her.) Trina's parents died in an accident when she was an infant. Unfortunately, no one knew of this, so the robot maintenance staff of her automatic home continued their programmed directives and care for her for ten years, before she was eventually discovered. She attends a robot/human school now, but is enrolled in the robot curriculum, since she has much to learn about humanity. Ott0: A robot that has come to the conclusion that it's superior strength and speed would allow it to procure lunch credits from weak, frail humans. In other words, a school bully. (Pictured above giving flowers to Trina) Lori: Evil prom queen. Awarded a scholarship to the school because census calculations deem her to to be a perfect educational template for self-actualized social integration. Robots are encouraged to observe and imitate her as a better means of leaning to flourish in human society. In reality, she is a crafty and conniving social climber and user. She is a poor example for the machines who imitate her and the humans who adore her mask of civility. She dates Ott0 so she can keep him around as a symbol of both status and general intimidation, and is of course jealous of his fascination with Trina. Just an idea I had. Let me know if you have any questions.
omg...this needs to be a comic! I was stupid enough to look it up when I realized it doesn't exist T.T

Wow, sorry for the wild goose chase there. I might have to revisit this idea. Of course as I recall, the nice girl here was pretty hard to write. The evil prom queen was so much more fun. [link] Don't know why that is.
I agree! I am a writer myself so writing about nice people is so...hard...

When I think about it, nice characters are mostly defined by what they wouldn't do, and the depths to which they wouldn't sink. They are satisfied with their lives and maintain a positive outlook on life. It doesn't seem like they have ambition or drive. The bad characters are capable of anything, and are usually motivated by some desire or goal that arbitrary rules or imposed values will not keep them from. I'd rather know or meet Trina here in real life, but I'd pay to watch the antics of someone like Lori. She's much more driven and far more entertaining.

Sorry, but the answer is nowhere; at least for the time being, after getting rejected by a publisher. However, following the reactions I've gotten here, I may want to dust this off and try somewhere else. In the meantime, here's the flower recipient's rival--the evil prom queen. [link]

Actually, for most of it, it says stuff that basically translates to this: "asijjdfhHOUSEodjflajdFOODaoodjfdDAYaldjjf1743253dsflj" so it's okay There aren't really any cuss words in Japanese, anyway, besides maybe sexual body parts. I've lived in Tokyo for like four years now, so I immediately recognized where this was supposed to be (Shibuya, in front of the 109 department store) but giggled at the Japanese not actually saying anything ;D Also, I didn't check out your other stuff, but it looks like you don't usually draw in the anime style. I mean, your inking and some of your details and coloring techniques looked like stuff my Marvel-loving artist friends would use. Which kinda gave this a cool, fresh look, actually

Yea I am trained in good ol' American "Pseudophotorealism" (Term coined by Chris Clarement I believe.) As a matter of fact, I used to ghost pencil the backgrounds for certain Marvel Comics. I have been attempting to adapt the the Japanese style of late. I could care less that it's popular actually, I just like the fastidious attention to detail and concrete sense of place that most of their comics seem to possess. (Something that domestic comics lack more and more, I'm sorry to say.) I thought I had the hang of the Asian style, but a multiplicity of accolades congatulating me on the Americaness of my art indicates that I've still got quite a bit of studying to do. On the bright side, in my inholy misegnation of the two schools of cartooning I may have stumbled upon a unique look. Indeed wonders never cease.

I don't think adopting a totally convincing anime style is a career booster, as a lot of my friends tried doing that and they are mostly still unemployed. I used to try to get it really Japanese looking, but when I came to Japan and studied at a manga school here they told me I had a very American look in the way I drew expressions and in having slightly more... what's the word... well, Japanese art can be very stiff, and since I'm influenced by the old WB style of animation, they said it was weird that a character could be standing still and still kinda look like it was in motion. Which, you know, is kinda like Bugs Bunny or something. They said that was actually a real draw for them when reading, so I eventually stopped trying to look totally authentic and have added more and more cartoony elements to my work. So having a mix is definitely a good thing While I think American comics look better than ever now with the way they are coloring these days (slicker paper and photoshop can do amazing things to a comic), I hate the process and how they use like 8 people to complete one issue. I think more than the art and the style (Japan is also in an art slump, I think, and doesn't have some of the beautiful artwork it had in the 90s), it's the attention to story and characterization I like. I think American comic producers try to concentrate too much on the physical look rather than the actual content of a comic.

What li'l "corner-ee thingies" are you talking about? If there's a new drafting tool out there that I can use, I'd definitely like to know about it. In the mean time, there are a couple of books you might want to check out. The first is How To Draw Manga Volume 29: Putting Things In Perspective. It's a nice straight-forward book, that's meant for all ages readers, so it gives easy to understand tips on how to create the illusion of environmental complexity. I highly recommend it. Regrettably, the more advanced volume, How To Draw Manga: Sketching Manga-Style Volume 4: All About Perspective, appears to be totally out of print. It's sad because that was an amazing follow-up to the first volume, (Don't know why it has an earlier number. ) Even if you can track down either of the Graphic Sha texts, there have been books published on the topic of linear perspective since Filippo Brunelleschi invented it in 1425. It should be no problem to track down a serviceable text at a local bookstore or library that should have the information you need. And last but not least, you might be able to get some tips from a website or two. So about that "corner-ee thing"...

The thing that you can make by taping a vertical ruler in the middle of a horizontal one? you don't have one? O-o waaaaaaah. all my school's books are like FROM 1425. I found a GREAT book at Borders but alas, I didn't have 25 dollars to spare. (la~ I owe the school 500 dollars O0O) anywho thanks.

"all my school's books are like FROM 1425." It would probably be even funnier if it weren't probably true. Taping a vertical ruler to a horizontal one? That's sounds clever actually, but I have never had need to do such a thing. When I was little, and I drew my first flip cartoon in the corners of one of my dad's old copies of Analogue, the old man went out and bought me something called a T Square. (I honestly thought it was some new means of corporal punishemnet, until he showed me what the thing was for. ) When I started taking drafting classes, we were all required to buy Triangles, which looked like so many ninja weapons to me. On a side note, one day when drawing, I accidentally broke a triangle. Feeling particularly mischievous, I put the longer of the two broken sections in my chest, as if I had been stabbed, and stumbled down the stairs like a murder victim in front of my mother. The antics of the two siblings before me had anesthetized her to such buffoonery. She told me to get up off the floor without so much as looking up from the dinner she was preparing. You should be able to acquire these implements without going that much deeper into debt. And if cash is a problem, then definitely make use of your local library.

During a critcal WWII battle, an American paratrooper unit was surrounded by a German tank battalion who lay siege to the strategically important village of Bastogne, Belgium during the winter of 1944. Winter weather had grounded the US Air Force, and America's own tanks were days away. The outnumbered paratroopers had to hold the town against a technically and numerically superior force for an entire week. The German's, confident in their position, offered the paratroopers an opportunity to surrender, to which their commanding general responded "Nuts." Emboldened by the defiance of their commander, the now legendary 101st Airborne paratroopers held the village, until American tanks finally arrived to repulse the German offensive. Many historians look upon Bastogne as the place where the Third Reich's back was finally broken. History's greatest nut shot.
and WHY is this not a movie? but instead there is a movie about a failed attempt to kill hitler with a suitcase?

Okay this requires a reply in three parts: First, more war trivia. The attempt to assassinate Hitler was in part because many of his best, most senior generals disagreed with his plan to attack Bastogne. They felt the resources used in that offensive could have been put to better use in a defensive position, protecting their capital city, Berlin. With the loss of the Ardennes forces, Germany lay virtually prostrate before the allied juggernaut. The officer's responsible for the "suitcase plot" felt that Hitler's poor judgment was becoming a tactical liability. Second, There WAS a movie. It was called "Band of Brothers." Well actually it was an HBO miniseries--an emmy winning mini-series, that was produced by the makers of "Saving Private Ryan." In my opinion, it was even better than the latter, because it was made using testimony from the surviving members of the 101st Airborne. It may well be the finest WWII drama ever produced. FYI, the title "Band of Brothers," came from William Shakespeare,s "Henry V". In that play on the eve of battle, facing a technically, and numerically superior foe, the king of England encouraged his troops with an immortal speech: "And Crispin Crispian shall ne'er go by, From this day to the ending of the world, But we in it shall be remembered- We few, we happy few, we band of brothers; For he to-day that sheds his blood with me Shall be my brother; be he ne'er so vile, This day shall gentle his condition; And gentlemen in England now-a-bed Shall think themselves accurs'd they were not here, And hold their manhoods cheap whiles any speaks That fought with us upon Saint Crispin's day" Third, what they really should make is a new movie about the Battle of Britain.

Well it's quite a bit older than that my friend. The ship is the titular vessel from the animated series Space Battleship Yamato, a seminal show that premiered in 1974, before George Lucas even started filming episode IV. [link] Yoshinobu Nishizaki was the first guy to do fighter planes in space, not George Lucas. He was also the first to come up with adorable garbage can bodied robots. [link] And once again Analyzer was on Japanese television screens three years before anyone ever heard of R2-D2. (Didn't I hear that George Lucas visited Japan around 1974? Very interesting.) On another curious note, they just did a live action Space Battleship Yamato movie last December. [link] It's available on DVD through Amazon. I recommend it, it's very entertaining. Way better than Star Wars episodes 1-3.

It's funny you should mention homework. I got an "A" in as astronomy class because of the knowledge gleaned from Space Battleship Yamato. Question 27.) "What substance is Saturn's rings composed of?" Hmmm, in season two, the Earth fleet defeated the alien invader's Magna-Flame Gun by retreating to Saturn's ring. As the alien captain was blown apart by Earth forces he lamented, "I should have known the Magna-Flame Gun wouldn't work in the deadly cold in Saturn's ice ring." [link] Answer: Ice. Fun and educational.

Whew. Thank you very much. This feels much better to hear than "Way to jump on the bandwagon and cash-in on the manga fad, you soulless, Gaijin hack!" Not to say that I ever heard that.
...Well, that was oddly...specific.
Sorry about that. Here let me try this again.: Thanks for the kind words, they really mean a lot.
Hahahahaha!
I couldn't imagine someone saying that, but it sounds like something a friend would say. So...did you draw all the art for the "Manga Mania: Video Game"? I know that blond girl anywhere! XD

title: Bad Boy bot
description: More from the doomed high school romance concept. Wallowing in teen drama tropes as I was during the conception of this ill-fated project, I realized that I needed a motor bike riding bad-boy with a heart of gold. (In other words, he only looks evil, while the handsome quarterback actually is.) Being me, I decided to make the dude a robot. I like the way the motor bike turned out here (May use it somewhere else.) But the bully? Man what was I thinking? He just doesn't come together as a design. BTW, that's milk he's drinking. Stealing all that milk money, he might as well.

comments.body: Wow, very cool, nice work!
Thanks a bunch.
Now if only the guys at Orange County Choppers can replicate that bike. That'd be so sweet.
Haw. That would wrahk!
woot there's so much 'flow' in this picture! And, as always, great use of black ;D I love it ^^ *faves*
*Thanks*
This is soo out of Akira. Tetsuo Riding his bike with no hands but this one might be a bit interesting. Growing milk for bad boys.

I like the bike here but I wasn't satisfied with how Ott0 turned out. Admittedly, this design having an actual mouth gives a purpose for all that stolen milk money. And yea, I was thinking about the clown boss from Akira. I like how he was riding the bike like a mobile throne.

title: Sometimes a Ship is justa Ship
description: Freud would have a field day with this picture.

comments.body: Prepare to fire the wave motion gun!
Funny on so many levels.
I never could work out why the wave motion gun whinnies like a horse
Huh, now that you mention it, that is a good question.
EPIC! "OUR STAR BLA-ZERRRRS!"
Check out my most recent journal. you'll thank me.
I've known about the movie for quite some time, but I've only seen the first trailer. This is beyond awesome. I...I think...I've got something in my eye.
Those are the kisses of little Cherubs. They manifest into tears of shear joy.

...Sexy. XD The ship really does remind me a little of Space Battleship Yamato,with dat awesome wave-motion gun thing.And man,for some reason I look at the water splash and the sun ray more. .__. Probably because they look easy enough to draw,but I never get them right.

Yea, organic stuff can be such a pain precisely because you can't get out a triangle, and conquer them through sheer force of draftmanship. I have a friend who can draw trees and plant life as if his mother was the Batman villain Poison Ivy. He just dashes it out without thinking. What's worse, he can't even explain what he's doing. Personally, when drawing natural phenomena1, I tend to sketch as loosely as possible, just working in general shapes, until what I want coalesces on paper. Once I have the general shape of the three--or the crashing waves, in this case--I trace over the form with my tight pencils and inks. I guess what I'm saying is that when drawing objects in nature, just loosen up! Hmmm, I guess abstract expressionists2 are good for something afterall. 1.) Wow, my spellchecker didn't flag it--"Phenomena" is the plural of "Phenomenon!" And here, my dumbass was just guessin' 2. In my perfect world, abstract expressionists are banished to Antarctica, along with the Twilight fans, and people who talk on their cell phones in the theater.
Excellent Drawing!!! I like it! Maybe I will colour it if you allow it (and if I able to ) I assume she is Yuki

Yes it is. I know they gender swapped many of the women in the 2010 live action movie, and added female shipmates in the 2199 remake, but when I was watching classic Yamato/ Star Blazers it seemed that Yuki/Nova was the only token girl on the ship. If Yamato was the Star Trek of Japan, then Yuki was Lt. Uhura, Nurse Chapel and Yeoman Rand rolled into one set of sprayed on yellow tights. And feel free to take a shot of coloring. Just link me the results when you're finished.

Indeed! But I think I remember another female sailor abroad the Yamato in the original anime in first episodes when they loaded her with the crew. But I did not liked how the Gamillians were shown in the live action action movie though Of course I give credit

Yea, I remember another woman in the crew talking commenting on how she was going to break the news she was going to marry a shipmate during her last communication with Earth. Beyond that I don't recall many other women aboard ship.
Probably she was that, I have to rewatch it

Okay Started colouring this, but I've encountered a problem: Between Yuki's right arm and head there is a thing I cannot identify. Is that little piece is part of the Yamato's Superstructure or her laser pistol? Above her shoulder and maybe insignia part?
If you mean the little pointy thing next to her check, that's some of her hair. Behind that is superstructure.
Ahh Superstructure, thanks, Sometimes it's hard to see things like that
I've finished adding colours, Hope you will like it!
Thanks.

title: Repobot
description: Another DMV piece. The big red guy behind our meter reading heroine is a Repobot.(My clumsy attempt to come up with a pun for "Reprobate") Essentially, it's a glorified car thief that snatches vehicles by simply picking them up, tucking them under its arms like a football, and running off. Quite a problem for a cop whose gun shoots parking tickets!

comments.body: niiice XD
Thanks.

title: Chibi Tomcat
description: Another Super-Deformed picture. Sorry, no maiden this time. Just an F-14 Tomcat.

title: Most Dangerous Color
description: Second Most Dangerous Bookstore picture in color. Here you can better see one of the robotic dalmatians, described in Fahrenheit 451. Granted, I think my design is slightly less intimidating than Ray Bradbury intended. I wish I could make it an ongoing character, the idea of one of those horrifying, eight-legged monstrosities loping around like some dumb, happy family pet brings me no end of joy.

comments.body: must be really deep pockets ha ha love it
Exactly. She has a nigh-infinite arsenal hidden in the pockets. She hasn't figured out how to get a tank in there though.
Well... that's different. Perhaps I should read that book finally.
Do it fast, before they try and make another movie. Could you imagine what Michael Bay would do with the concept?

Le gasp. The colors, so rich. The Lines so crisp and clear; the bookstore owners' professional clothes so suave (though the red eyes did make me scratch my chin contemplatively)... this is such a delight! The hammerspace pockets, while never 'real', have a great sense of verisimilitude. The bookstore has a great mahogany feel that feels more like a gigantic public library - something I've always adored. What's that device in the lower-left?

"Le gasp." Very cute. Hammerspace. Never heard that term before. I'm guessing it factors into some obscure British science fiction show, or fantasy novel series. One of the problems with having a vivid imagination is that so many things I think up have already been done. If I was less honest I would try and pass it off as an homage, "Sure I meant it as a tip of the hat to that pretentious British science fiction show with the bad acting, turgid pacing, and amateurish production design that you apparently like for some reason." Molly (The owner) has pink eyes, being a little white mouse. Cassandra (The clerk) believes it is her divine right as a cat to sun herself in the window of a bookstore and claims that Molly's store chose her as it's cat in some inexplicable celestial fashion. Despite the fact that local males are quite enamored of the sight of Cassandra sunbathing in the store's picture window, Molly takes considerable issue with the idea of a cat lounging in her shop. Realizing over time she was unable to get rid of the feline, she put her on staff. As it would happen, Cassandra is a former superspy, and adept at all forms of armed and unarmed combat. This fact becomes apparent during the first weekly ninja raid on the bookstore in which the wily cat dispatches the assassins who were seeking a forbidden tome. Finding the ability to gun fight, knife fight, and kick box, an asset to a store constantly under siege by book thieves, ancient cultists, and rogue literary figures Molly decides to keep on Cassandra. Although she may have to invest in a spray water bottle to keep her from sunbathing in the window. The robot on Cassy's bask is on of the killer robot dalmatians that served as firehouse dogs in Fahrenheit 451. It totally loves Cassandra, refusing to return to the copy it escaped from. I'm thinking about having it have puppies.

You had never heard of Hammerspace before? As an animation and animanga afficionado, I figured you would have heard of it. [link] Oh, as an avid reader, the very concept is both adorable and fascinating! It has many mysteries and many many intriguing concepts. I would -love- to stop by. I wonder if they sell graphic novels? The anthropomorphized 'cat and mouse' is a very cute idea; much more true than a lot of other visualizations. After all, to a cat, what purpose do we serve other than to provide for our feline companions? Heh, I envisions Cassandra happily snoozing on a warm computer monitor, soaking up the sun in the display, or trying to find a comfortable way to nap on top of a warm, floating space probe. In short, it seems very cute. I do love the reference to the robotic dalmatians; it looks a lot more benign than my prior visualization when I had read it.
hey is that a high caliber machine gun in your pocket or are you just happy to see me?

title: Most Dangerouse Book Store II
description: Another N. E. Boox piece. At the time of this drawing, I had decided to dispense with the bear, the sheep and the rabbit, and just have a surly, cranky cat-girl be Molly's (Whom I've tries to make look more professional.) sole employee. Cassandra, the cat girl, (You can tell, because her name begins with a "C". ) holds the ancient belief that the universe assigns every book store a solitary hallowed cat to watch over it while sunning itself in its picture window. (This is a belief held over from the ancient times when cats walked on four legs--long story ) No way a mouse is going to let a cat, two legs or no, lounge around all day sunning herself in her window, so Cassandra actually has to work in order to enjoy her position as book store cat. Fortunately, as luck would have it, she's a combat trained veteran of the extra-special forces. Hence the heavy weapons she keeps in the bottomless pockets of her shop apron. In retrospect, if I ever revisit this project, I may bring back (And finally name!) the bear, the sheep, and the rabbit. Having only level-headed characters around when armored skeletons on cavalry steeds leap out of dictionary podiums can get a little tiresome.

title: Most Dangerouse Book Store
description: You are looking at another shining example of my ability to invent fresh original concepts and the do absolutely nothing with them. (It's not "self loathing", it's "ironic, self-deprecating humor", which is way more hip.) In the center of this picture, we have our heroine Molly Mouse the owner and sole proprietor of Anybooks; (Or N. E. Boox. I could never decide.) a book store, so full of rare and esoteric tomes that it is actually several times larger inside than out. (As is the case with any piece of comic book real estate. ) Because of the store's unique stock it is always the center of danger and intrigue; both from ne'r-do-well customers who won't take "no" for an answer when it comes to obtaining books with knowledge the world isn't ready for, and the occasional creatures contained within the books themselves. Ms. Mouse, along with her many talents, has such a complete knowledge of bus and subway schedules, she is able to travel the world, often beating local authorities, to massive literary disturbances through use of public transportation alone! (She doesn't have a driver's license. ) She has three hapless employees that I never got around to naming: A cowardly bear who is forever one spindly-legged Lovecraftian monstrosity, spilling out from between the pages of a dusty old folio, away from taking a vow of illiteracy, and a sheep girl who never says anything but always nods in curt agreement when the rabbit girl says discontented statements containing the word "we". Sorry folks. Just a concept.

comments.body: interesting.i would probably do a fan comic of it if you had some clear outlay of the charactyers
A fan comic before the original creator gets off his duff and creates the original? I'd never live it down. But I am flattered that you'd be interested, based on what little you've seen.
the girl is a chipmunk? chipette?
Wait, a sheep girl? wow that some book store. Must be a Sesame street fan
Eh heh She's a mouse. Thus her name.
This might fun to watch (if it ever came out/ was made in to a series )
whats this book thing you mentioned, can it run Window's 7 or at least chech emails... or maybe a food, can you eat book?
Sorry, it can't run any software, or check emails. On the bright side, it does possess infinite battery life. Also, I suppose you could eat one, if so inclined, but I wouldn't recommend it.
but there full of fiber and essential vitamins

title: Takin' the Train
description: This is something I did for an anime convention. Don't bother trying to prove your mad language skillz by trying to read any of the signs. They're all gibberish. For all I know, I've offended someone's dear old mom.

comments.body: Don't worry, the signs are mostly gibberish, at least the ones I checked out closely. The pic itself is neat- I really like your use of perspective in the scene, and the action and motion of the girl (esp. her hair and camera).

Thanks. Oh wait, that means you read Japanese. Cool. I may have to hit you up for future drawings, like how to write jokes like "Why are you trying to read this, look at the action." into the background. That would rock. And thanks for the comments.
Hey, you're welcome. I'm a bit rough with my Japanese, but I can read it alright.

I'm just glad the signs aren't in Greek. I have spent plenty a time trying to read signs with Greek characters in rpgs only to find out they're all gibberish. Sometimes they make some funny words though lol. Anyway, back to the point... your environment drawing skills are top, dude. I'm envious!
Heh, It's all greek to me. And thanks for your kind words.

title: Gridiron Man
description: A surprise from my old drawing collection. A character I made up myself! Years ago, I was collaborating with a studio mate on a graphic novel proposal. At the time, I recall being irrationally obsessed with "Tengo Tenge", so I demanded the story be set in some cut-throat fist fight filled bastardization of high school life. (My collaborator wanted to write a romantic comedy, so as you can guess the project eventually flew apart under the shearing force of two such utterly incompatible ideas. ) One of my contributions was the villain of the piece, Patrick. Since in the world of comics, the quarterback of the high school football team is pretty much responsible for all the evils in the adolescent world, I decided to wholeheartedly embrace the cliche', and make the antagonist the school's resident field general. Of course, in this story, football is played in giant robots, and the ball is a ramjet engine. (Another of my contributions to the story. I made him all boy-band-pretty in deference to my collaborator. But then now that I think of it, many anime/manga villains are similarly androgynus.)

comments.body: Such clean lines, love it!
Much obliged.

That's it. As soon as I amass 100 billion U.S. Dollars *does the Doctor Evil pinky thing*, I'm going to find you, then fund your own studio so you can make this anime high school of yours. Sounds a hell of a lot better than a ton of animation that's found on both sides of the Pacific.

That would be awesome, Then I could at last fullfill my life long dream of buying a mansion, complete with a swimming pool full of easy women, each carrying a silver plate full of cheeseburgers. It would be like a aquatic "Deal or No Deal" with chlorine and a lot mor cholesterol.
hemm, You know, i think ive never seen a fight pages from you. that might be interesting to look at. or not
I'll see if I can find some old DMV strips for you when I post next week.
sweet.

Do you recall a little fighting game series called Rival Schools/Project Justice? The football player was the dick in that series too! He also liked to dress fancy like your guy does. I don't know about robot football, but I understand robot basketball is set to become the next big thing... [link]

Holy cow, man. You were actually serious about that robot basketball thing! That trailer looked awesome. I going to have to get the DVD. Wow, street ball, meets lowriders. But yea, high school football players have been comic book foils since Flash Thompson gave Peter Parker his first purple nurple. I didn't know the concept had made it to fighting games however. (Then again, the last video game I played was Armored Core: Master of the Arena.) I never figured the Japanese new anything about American footbal. Sounds like something I should check out. Thanks for the link, btw.

There's been a few football players dotting the japanese fighting game scene... very sporadic sightings. Of course, the one from the particular game I mentioned didn't actually do much football-ing at all. He was more a poor man's Terry Bogard... oh wait, are you familiar with Fatal Fury at all? It's a video game, but it had a really spiffy series of OVAs and a motion picture "back in the day"...

title: Have I Posted a Catgirl Yet?
description: Another basement find. You know, I was all ready to write a few paragraphs in tribute to the great Ben Dunn, the creator of the character pictured here, but I think it would be more amusing to reveal how this picture actually came to be. A short time ago I was contributing to a series of How to Draw Manga books. (Forgive me--I was young and I needed the money. ) As you can guess, one of these dealt with how to draw cat girls. One of my assignments was to draw a cat girl washing her car. (Unfortunately for me, and the supposedly "G-Rated" nature of this proposed book, I had just seen "Cool Hand Luke" for the first time shortly before being tasked with this picture.) Anyway, this is what I sent the editor for the first drawing. He was quite pleased with it, and asked me to move on to my next assignment. I was half way into drawing that, when I got the following two phone messages: Message 1: BEEP John, about the car wash picture. My kids just took a look at it. There might be a slight problem. Message 2: BEEP John? Uh. Yea. My wife just saw the picture. There's a problem. Needless to say, the drawing was summarily un-accepted. So, deciding to cut my losses, I changed the cat-girl to Asrial, and made the car into her signature Corvette. Accidental fan art.

comments.body: Hhm, No tail... ah yes she must be from the rare short tail/no tail bobcat species of catgirl!
Eh, can't say I was ever a fan of tails. Can't say why. I'm fine with cat girls, tails just don't suit me.
Hence why I said she was of the short tail bobcat type catgirl... still it is nice!
THIS PICTURE STINKS! ... See, because Asrial's not a cat. She's a skunk!

Curses. You're right of course. Not about the picture stinking, (Actually, it has a faint, peaty aroma. With a touch of lilac.*) but I forgot about Asrial true form. Might have to edit my artist comments. *Free single character pin-up if you can guess the reference.

Not that I wouldn't kill to see you draw some things... but your reference was a little bit outside of my geek-o-sphere... so I google-fu'd it up. Of course, I won't spoil it, because I couldn't guess it outright.

What is 'O'Brien and Bashir teasing Worf about the way he smells?' If only to get you to draw some Empowered fan-art which I *know* you're a big fan of. There's so many hawt babes in that series that it's a real shame that none of them have graced these talented pages.
Amazing outline

title: Lovely Angels
description: Another artifact from my bout with basement archeology. Long before the recent proliferation of graphic novels inspired in every 16 year old, a fierce desire to draw his own "manga," one immensely talented artist was ripping off the Japanese cartooning style without even the slightest hint of shame. That artist, as you know, is Adam Warren, and waay back before entirely too many of you were even born, he drew the ever drool-worthy Dirty Pair. (More recently he is known for his series of Empowered tpb's from Dark Horse--Bondagey Goodness. ) Continuing the tradition, I have tried to co-opt his style, with equal shamelessness, and draw these two wonderful characters myself. I know I suffer mightily in comparison to the awesome Mr. Warren, but I hope you'll find it in your hearts to enjoy my offering nontheless.

comments.body: Ka-boom! I love that inkwork, lots of energy and character in it. It sets them off quite nicely. It's a nice interpretation of the dir... lovely angels.
Careful, you know they hate the "D" word.

BTW! I got a great idea... I always wantedt Rachel and her team to have a great JET...^^ And you asked me about Rachel having tec-wepons or something... maybe if ya want to... you could draw her/the teams jet^^
Hmmm, sounds like a plan. Do you have any particular jet in mind, or would I have a free hand in coming up with something?
free hand my friend!! Surprise me! You got the best taste in planes I know!
Glad to hear it.

I like these two a lot. Is this a copy from a DP manga? Heh... I was careful in not to type the full name, as I too, know they hate it. Regardless, it's very well drawn. Keep up the good work! You read the graphic novel A Plague of Angels?

Is there an actual manga? I'm familiar with (And obviously inspired by.) Adam Warrens comic, but I was unaware that the Japanese had ever done a DP comic of their own. As for the picture being a copy? No. It's an original composition. I paid slavish attention to Mr. Warren's designs, but I came up with the drawing on my own.

No, I was on about Adam Warren and Toren Smith, don't worry. Their style is like manga and they are known to use Japanese names and all. I don't know myself, if a Japanese manga artist did a manga for these two. As for the pose? Sweet! I love it when folks make up original poses, rather than copying from the original pic in their style.
Yea, if you just copy the pose, you may as well fire up your light table and trace the bloody thing.
dirty pair was not japanese?

Yes and no. Originally The Dirty Pair was a series of Japanese science fiction novels by Haruka Takachiho. In the late 1980's the animation rights were sold to a Japanese company, who produced the famous anime; and the comic rights were sold to Studio Proteus and American company.(Who would later be bought out by Dark horse.) Adam Warren produced that comic. So really it's both, but it's origins are Japanese.
Nice buns... I mean GUNS!
*thumbs up* Very cool.
Thanks. Hey, is that Gendo Ikari from NGE in your avatar? I think you may want a look at this picture. [link]
Yup. I bear a slight resemblance to him in real life, so... LOVE the Rei/Asuka pic!
Haw, I thought you might.
This is a stellar piece of DP artwork man. Faved.
Thanks. Or "Domo Arigato Mr. Robaato!" I'll bet that's the first time you've heard that. But seriously, thank you very much. I am impressed by your art as well.
Dude, we gonna have to do a collab in the future.
Sounds awesome.
Looking good.

title: The Cat and the Gat
description: One last basement cleanage picture. This is of two characters I made up just to have an excuse to draw a leggy cat-girl and a robot. The robot's details are kit-bashed from an F-15 Eagle and a fighter pilot's flight helmet. There's no story beyond that really, so if anyone has any ideas, it would be fun to hear them.

comments.body: Easy. They're both artificial life forms, the cat girl's the latest gen model of them helpful crossing guards in Tokyo City, while the little guy's a portable SWAT unit, deployable via a catapult/launcher from the nearest police station, hence its transform-iness. Crossing Guard Neko-Chan™'s ears and tail are actually receivers and a transmitter, respectively, that she uses to summon SWAT-kun whenever other AI or piloted-mechs go nuts, which is usually whenever the Knight Sabers are on their day offs and the Special Vehicles Division 2 guys are stuck in traffic somewhere else (and don't get me started on them SPD Rangers, they *always* have their hands full... ).
Oh yeah, forgot to mention: Natsumi and Miyuki can't be bothered because they're always on APB duty looking for Strikeman. Man, that guy always gets away!
Hey that actually works. And SPD is my favorite ranger series. I have lurid unmentionable fantasies about that yellow ranger chick.
Dude, I need to take a crack at coloring some of your stuff. This has been my second outing into your gallery and yet, I'm finding more awesome things to look at. Excellent work John.
I am very glad to hear this. Feel free to color anything you wish. Just let me know when you finish, as I'd be very curious to see the result. Thank you for the kind words.

title: Red Prowling Devil Girls
description: I'm fairly confident I'm the only one who read the manga "Red Prowling Devil." Which isn't entirely surprising when you consider that the translators working on it thought the father of modern rocketry's name was Fon Brown.* It's still a shame so few read the manga. How can you go wrong with cute anime girls and fourth gen fighter planes? * FYI the rocketry guy was Wernher Von Braun.

comments.body: I love it, and the lines are SOOO classic. A shame this style has vanished... got me into anime/manga in the first place and for me totally kicks the ass of all these weakling-emo-boys and underage-girl crap of today

I salute you sir!!! I too long for the days when anime was about hot babes who fan serviced, and "Gainax-Bounced" across the landscape. When anime became popular, little did I suspect it would usher in a torrent of willowy girly men and prepubescent femmes. (I mean Gundam with girly men? WTF?) I feel like my genie screwed up mish third wish.
Can't go wrong :0) Lovely Mig 29 and F-15 :0)
Soemone who recognizes jets. At last!
ok, the thing that attract me was the boobs, maybe better put, the folds on her breast. Looks really nice. The jst might as well be starscream and thundercracker
I know Starscream was an F-15, but I did not realize Thundercracker was supposed to be a Mig-29.
lol my bad, i thought the jets were the same, looks like i missed a few details. Maybe Astro Plain fit the bill..

Well they are both high wing, bubble canopy, twin engined forth generation air superiority fighters with dual vertical tail fins and a thrust-to-weight ratio approaching 1:1. So I suppose I could see where one could mix them up. But one is American and the other Russian so be careful which one you mistake for which in to company of each countries resident fighter fans.
Love this manga series, but I haven't finished it yet XP. great fan art btw.
Thanks.
"Red Prowling Devil."? Never heard of it. Actually there's a huge load of animes and mangas I've failed to read. Nice work on the Su-27 and the F-15. They're very detailed and shape is accurate.

Sadly, ComicsOne the domestic publisher of RPD "stopped paying its bills and disappeared." according to Wikipedia. As far as I can tell only a couple of volumes are available through Amazon. But is is new, and cool mecha stuff should be supported in any form.
I'll check it out
sweet

it was bon brown (godart like herman overt were pioners but von borwn was the one that make it work for the first time whit the a4-v2)

I have NEVER heard of this manga. Where can I find it?!! Is there an F-14 in it?!!!

There were eight volumes in total, but unfortunately its publisher/translator, Comics One has been defunct for quite some time. I saw that it is possible to order one or two of the volumes on Amazon.com, but I don't know where to direct you to get all eight. (The wholesaler I occasionally, get back issues from, Mycomicshop.com, only seems to carry Comics One's martial arts titles.) I don't recall ever seeing an F-14 in any of the volumes. Mostly they featured aircraft that don't get a lot of attention, like the French Rafale, and the Russian built Su-27. Doubt they would have gotten to it if they were able to go beyond eight volumes. Oh well.

Came out in 2002.
mmmmmHM Less well known, ya say? What about the Mig-1.44? I oughta go find it and buy it myself.
No, but they do appear in the Wonder Woman DVD.
eYEEEE, REALLY? You mean the new live action movie? SRSLY?!!
No silly, the animated one that came out last year. [link] There is no movie in the works any more. Although I have heard they're trying a new TV series. You probably won't see any Migs in it.
Dagnab. I WILL go look at that, though How many are there? What's the scene with 'em like?!

There's only one DVD. Warner Brothers animation has gone to a mostly "Original Animated Video" format of late, making one-shot "Straight-to-DVD" animations like this one. The video recounts Wonder Woman's origin, which involves combat pilot Steve Trevor getting shot down and landing of Paradise Island, and as the first male setting foot on the sacred island setting in motion the chain of events which sends Princess Diana to man's world. The origin is updated from its original WWII setting, so instead of a P-40 versus an Me-109, we see a bunch of F-23's against a mess of Mig-1.44's. If you're a classic aviation buff, you'd probably be disappointed, but I don't think you'll have that issue. Steve Trevor is voiced by Nathan Fillion--the guy who should have played hal Jordan.

title: Robotech Rook
description: Rook and her Cyclone from Mospeada/Robotech:Invid Wars. I discovered Robotech late in its initial run, so Invid War was my first exposure to cartoondom's most famous kit-bash1. Sure, it depicted a post apocalyptic future, which deviants sharply from my preferred not too distant dystopic cyberpunk futures, but all the heroes in it roamed the battered landscape in fighter jets and motorcycles that all turned into robots! Really there should be something like that in every cartoon. 1.) What we know, and revere, as Robotech is actually an aggregate of three separate Japanese animated series: Super Dimensional Fortress Macross, Southern Cross and Genesis Climber Mospeada. I used to assume every anime fan knew this, but I meet more and more whippersnappers who stare blankly when I bring up the glory of Robotech. It's sad really.

I watched Robotech: The Macross Saga in '88 or '89, but never saw the end (guess because there was a REAL war going on in my country at that time, I think TV station lost licence to broadcast it). SO, recently I downloaded ALL episodes, with "The sentinels" movie, and "The shadow chronicles" movie, and watched it day by day, from crashing of SDF1 'till the last appearance of Rick Hunter, and his ship heading towards the black hole... Now (20 years after), I'm waiting for the sequel :0) My favourite character-Lisa Hayes.
Hmmm, never drew her. She is underrated. I may have to rectify that. If I may ask, what country are you from?

I'm from Serbia (Yugoslavia 'till recently). Heh, and half of my ability to recognize jets (you mentioned it in the other comment) was learned "the hard way", since NATO bombed my country for 78 days (year 1999.). Some trivia: it was the first time ever that B-2 bomber was used, and also the first time f-117a was shot down. I encourage you to draw Lisa asap :0)

Today, compared to it's descendants, like Macross Frontier, SD Macross/Robotech appears antiquated and even quaint, but back then, it totally blew the doors of my mind.

title: Planet Girl
description: This is an illustration I did to accompany an article published in a programming guide to an anime convention a while back. Oddly enough this picture reminds me of the art of the Late Great Mike Weiringo. I wasn't going for that look, as I was trying to be more "anime-esque". But there are worse things than unintentionally imitating the work of an admired professional.

comments.body: Great job on the piece. I do enjoy your spacecraft (normally, people try to build them like your generic cone-style rocket, but I'm glad you used a fighter craft as a basis for a single-occupant vessel). And the girl in the piece is lovingly drawn, and I enjoy the detailing on her suit. Plus, that shot of her looking at that planet (which's landmasses resemble a smile) is well-constructed. I love it!

Yea, I easily could've gotten away with a simple V-2-style rocket, or a conical Apollo-style capsule for the spaceship in this drawing. But as a devout fan of the wickedly awesome Shoji Kawamori, it was more or less my duty to draw in a high tech space fighter--albeit a chibified version.
Yeah, you could've gone the generic V-2 rocket approach, but I'm glad you went with a high-tech vessel. But, since she's the only occupant, it makes sense for it to be "chibified".
Cool!

title: Autumn Walk
description: This was something I did for a convention a while back. I totally balked at the assignment, as it was for a pretty anime style girl walking fully-clothed down a path on a beautiful autumn day. ACK! No robots? No explosions? No cleavage? Well grave misgivings, I drew the piece, and ended up really liking how it turned out. Don't expect to see a lot of pictures along these line though.

comments.body: It's beautiful ^.^

Thanks, I'm used to hearing words like "Destructive" and "Disturbed", when my work is discussed. I'm forced to admit "Beautiful" is a welcome change of pace. Maybe I will draw more stuff along these lines. Maybe.
Never hurts to try new things Btw, what program did you use? I've dabbled with colored anime styles but I have a hard time with coloring my line-art. Think you can critique some of mine?

I use Adobe Photoshop 5.0 to color. I know that makes me a caveman considering the state of the art; but it works for me. Someday though I fantasize about being able to upgrade to a computer and a graphics program that was made this century. Sure, I can take a look at your stuff. Just drop me a note with the appropriate attachments.

Caveman, haha. Yeah, I really would like some good tips because I'm trying to get into an art college, but it's expensive, so I'm working on a portfolio for a scholarship. Keep in mind, I'm self taught and need some serious tips! My small gallery is all I have on the comp right now: [link]

title: Bubblegum Girls
description: I'm told that a "Bubblegum Crisis" is a situation that one comes upon when one is blowing a particularly large bubblegum..er..bubble, and discovers to their chagrin that if they let the bubble burst it will get all over their face and make a big mess. So one blows more air into the bubble to keep it supported, in the process increasing the size of the bubble, and subsequently the size of the mess it makes when it inevitably does burst. What that has to do with a female Tony Stark wanna-be and her rock star pal (And the two other chicks.) going all Iron-Manny on killer robots in the Not-To-Distant-Dystopian-Cyberpunk future, is beyond me. Anyway, I drew this picture. Let me know what you think.

title: Daughter X

I'm pleased how the city turned out and I'm relieved to have overcome a life-threatening battle with debilitating procrastination to complete it. Lucky for me the good friend who commissioned it; much like the character portrayed therein; was possessed of an uncanny mutant power: Saintly patience.

comments.body: Wow; amazing work john ^^

i always wanted to see some kids of them... did you knew there is a comic-issue where the adventures of the X-kids are shown... I mean the REAL childeren of Rogue and Gambit, Storm and ?, Colossus and ?

Yea, I think it's called Generation Next. And I believe it's written by the same gent who wrote "New Mutants" way back in the day. One thing that interests me about it is the fact that Ororo's daughter Becky, seems to grow fangs and have uncontrollable moments of beastial savagery. hm... Don't know... Can't remember if the first one was a regular plane :/ sry But It's great that you know everything about becky and the genneration next^^ I am still wondering bout becky abilities^^

One of the great things about the late Dave Cockrum, the artist who introduced Storm the comic universe was his devotion to technical accuaracy. When, in a comic script, a villain made a getaway on a jet that hovered vertically, Cockrum would draw the real real vertical take-off jet the AV-8A Harrier. [link] And when the script called for the X-men to have a large jet that could travel the world quickly, he drew a real jet that could do the job. The fastest non-rocket powered moving object built in human history, the SR-71 Blackbird!! [link] The SR-71 was a spy plane that was retired after the gulf war in favor of spy satellites, but it put up speed records that stand TO THIS VERY DAY!! Mig-25 [link] pilots who tried to shoot down Blackbirds during the cold war confessed, after the Berlin Wall fell, that their Mach 3 interceptors could only fly high enough and fast enough to see a Blackbird pulling away. The SR-71 is an astounding testament to American aeronautical engineering, and ingenuity, and most importantly. It's a real plane! Why doesn't every living American child know about this plane? Sorry about the rant. I guess that's what happens when retired USAF NCO's take their kids to see the SR-71's at Andrew's AFB every summer for 16 consecutive years.

you are amazing! You know some great suff! Are you a fan of things like that, or is it because of x-men!? But 4 ral, I am glad to know now, that the good old blackbird was a real plane! thx 4 telling me all that!

title: Future of Parking Enforcement
description: Another picture from my DMV concept. The vehicle in the air above our heroine is a tow truck believe it or not. The thought behind this picture was a possible slogan for the story: "It's 2076, do you know where your car is?"

comments.body: Fabulous piece. I really like the drawing style you work with - there's a lot of detail there, but the line-art still remains clear and clean. The contrast between the cel-style shading of the foreground, and the more traditional colouring of the sky is really novel, too.
Thank you. God willing, there will be more examples for you to look at soon.

Yea, to me Anime=Cyberpunk. I loved Akira, Dominion, Ghost in the Shelll, BGC. Where the future isn't full of magic technology, and there's still problems ordinary bums have to rise up and solve, in the absence of glittering perfect idealists. Despite the fact they're full of cyborgs and sentient machines, those futures always felt more authentic.

hemm, the cars are kept like pokeball, or like the Dragon Ball series, In capsules, but even if the size is reduce, the weight is still a factor, so they need to be kept in meter style parking meters. of course, that would be senceless
Yes it would, and it would negate the need for 50 story parking garages. Not to mention it's far beyond the technology in the story.
Nicee
Thanks.

What a cool image! I love the thick and pervasive colors. It really does blow me away; looking at all those shades and layers... it really strikes me as downright jaw-dropping! I definitely like the 'quad-boot' on the aerial tow truck, as well as the super-impressive 'DMV' logo. Much more impressive now.

Thanks, I've really got to get around to doing newer color images for AJ. (Side note: She gets really pissed when you call her "Miss America". ) FYI, I designed smaller, cuter boots since I drew this.
What was her first name, again? Is "Columbia" too cheesy?
It's America. That's why she gets pissed when she's called "Miss America". Anyway, with "Columbia", it would be CJ.

*Facepalms* I should listen better. Well, I just recently learned that the iconography of 'Lady Liberty' was originally called 'Columbia' in the sense of a female personification - and wanted to use it somehow. [link](name)
The glowing engine rings remind me of those new "Dyson" fans. [link] I do not suppose that is intentional?
Dyson is totally my hero , but this drawing was done years before he invented his critically bad ass fans. Still it would work as a explanation behind the bladeless "hover hoops."

No? Well, who knows? Maybe he was inspired by you. Either way, it's such a cool concept. I do not know how they work, but I know that my attention span is intensely attracted to the shiny and futuristic look.
COOOOL! I wantt onnnneeee!!
A levitating tow? Sure thing, just wait 66 years and park illegally.
KEKEKEKEKEKE. I will. WATCH ME.
suddenly getting a traffic ticket isn't so bad
