Wow, she looks good

It's funny. I didn't design AJ with a mind toward drawing "the sexy girl", but folks react to her as a sex symbol nonetheless. Guess I'm just too used to drawing "that kind of girl." Of course, I may have overcorrected with Repeat.
I think there's something about a fairly attractive woman in a uniform, particularly a fairly figure-hugging one, that just ratchets up her appeal. lol, maybe. Hehe "cooould be".

Could be huh? Back to the drawing board it is. I'm not really interested in having jokes where folks call Repeat "sir" by mistake. I was going for more of a Lina Inverse, small chested woman aesthetic.
Heh. Aha I see, yeah Repeat does look just a bit too boyish.
A woman named America working for our most despised law enforcement branch on the nation's tri-centenial; it reminds me a bit of Martha Washington in Frank Miller's Give Me Liberty.
Finally someone notices the tricentennial connection. I'm familiar with Give Me Liberty, but I've never actually read it. Never figured I'd be doing an accidental homage to Frank Miller.
Ah jus' luvs her! (no really I does!)

It's funny. Unlike with the Hench Bunnies, I didn't create America Jones with the intent to create a sex symbol. I just wanted to create an ass kicking meter-maid. I suppose it's ultimately my fault for using Nichelle Nichols as the physical template for the character.
What's sexier than a thickly-stacked, heavily-packed, foot-to-ass-chick?
I would LOVE to see this as a regular comic series.
Glad to hear that. Workin' on it.

title: DMV a' la FLCL
description: Yes I shamelessly swiped the composition of FLCL poster, but these are in fact my own characters. The woman on the futuristic scooter if AJ, the not-too-distant-dystopian-future's toughest, most badass meter maid. The red headed dude is her partner, Drew, the not-too-distant-dystopian-future's least badass meter maid. They patrol the Megarage, the pinnacle of multi-level parking structures, and almost a city unto itself; and they fight ( At least AJ does.) corporate sponsored youth gangs; highly armed criminally, desperate car pools; and coin operated robots who dispense the not-too-distant-dystopian-future's most pernicious illicit substance--coffee. Anyone whose ever known me has suffered under the extremely long held belief that I will eventual produce a comic, or cartoon, or series of cocktail napkin sketches containing this bizarre concept. To date I mostly have a lot of promisory notes in the form of drawings like this one.

comments.body: I've seen some of these DMV themed pictures before and I've always been marveled at how unique the concept was. The art is stunning and the characters already looked like they had fleshed out personalities. I agree with every one else, I hope that this becomes a series at some point.

Holy cow! an actual reply. Thank you for replying, and thank you for every word you've written here. It's very encouraging to have a positive response to my wonky concept. Yea, I'm not attention starved. Not one bit.

I'm serious, the other stuff I've seen was fantastic. I recall a group pic with the cast on a car in the parking garage and another with an unfortunate victim of the apparent citation flinging sidearm. The concept is a neat twist on the usual world ending epic conflicts that are present in most of the usual cyberpunk stories. Instead, this was about those folk that never really get attention, but have to deal with a very real and very important part of city life. Can you imagine the difficulties of controlling and monitoring a futuretech city's traffic? Good god. With the population density and the new throughways that said cities would bring? It's practically a whole world of stretching concrete spans and elevated highways. Now of course, this is partially exciting because one of your strengths is technical design and layout, so you'll be one of the few people who could fully explore that world. I wouldn't want to do it myself, but I look forward to seeing you do it. :3

title: Chiroines 01
description: Just about everyone I know is continuous in their extollment of the virtues of displaying artwork on a deviant art account. Well I will give into peer pressure this once and start posting a few pieces. A couple of years ago I started a series of chibi-styled super-heroine drawings, with the eventual ambition of publishing a sketchbook with said illustrations. Here is the first chibi-super heroine Wonder Woman. I hope folks like it. Perhaps enough postve comments will move me to get off my considerable duff and finish this series.

comments.body: so cute!
Huzzah, a comment. A positive comment no less. Thank you very much.
Ha, nice. In fact I like all of them so far. Unfortunately I never read or saw any Wonder Woman so I'm gonna have to ask this: why the Starfighter? Please forgive my ignorance

Wonder Woman used to have an invisible jet plane, that she would use to get to the scene of whatever villain or disaster she had to fight. It was trasparent, radar invisible, and looked a little like an F-105 (At least it did in the 1970's Superfriends animated series. [link] ) So I decided to use an actual F-105 as my model for this drawing. They wrote the invisble jet out of the comic book in 1985. Wonder Woman's publisher, DC Comics, reasoned that if Wonder Woman was supposed to be the female equivalent of Superman, then she should be able to fly like him too. [link] Since that time, Wonder Woman had flown under her own power, just like the man of steel, so it's little surprise that fans today aren't aware that she used to need a ride to get around. I suppose I didn'treally need to draw the jet in my picture since I have her flying under her own power next to it, rendering it redundant; but I wanted some gadget to fit in with my Girls and Gadgets theme, and Wonder Woman does have some history with the jet.
Hey, girls and gadgets: always good Ah, that explains it then. I must admit I never got much further than Batman and The Phantom when it came to superheroes.

It's an American thing I guess. Here in the States, every kid is innundated by a wide variety of super-hero fare. (Mostly to sell toys and underwear, I guess.) Plus, there used to be a live action television show about Wonder Woman where the actress playing her looked like this. [link] So trust me when I tell you, every man in the States knows who Wonder Woman is.

Looking back at this post, it seems a tad jingoist, which was not my intent. I was trying to make a joke about how attractive the actress who played Wonder Woman was, and how the character's mainly imprinted on us by our lust for Linda Carter. Hope I didn't offend.

another sweet piece....did i mention i'm loving these to death. there's still batgirl and her cycle....huntress and hers, valkerie and her pegasus, medusa or crystal and lockjaw,invisible girl and fantasticar,gwen stacy and green goblin glider...ok ,ok, rip gwen.wasp,ms.marvel, scarlet with,black widow,tigra,jocasta,spider woman,sersi...... and the avengers quinjet,harley quinn and the jokermobile ( does that even exist? lol)

Ok, reading the other comments I see this was intentional and I am not the only one.
You focus on it because your an observant viewer. I was totally thinking of the Starfighter when I drew this! I always thought the invisible jet from the old Superfriends cartoon resembled it.
Ah yes! For me it was always the "rockets" on the wings... so many beautiful aircraft from the 50s/60s. Oh as well, really lovely ink work.

I don't know if t was intentional or not, but there was a real flair and beauty to aircraft like the B-58 Hustler, or the SR-71. They look like they were forced into existence by the sheer power of our collective imaginations. I think some of the modern designs are increasing soulless, like the F-22 Raptor. It may be radar invisible and cook a 20 lb turkey in under 20 minutes, but it still looks like a paper airplane to me.

I hear ya, my favourite aircraft design is the B-52b. I mean, it's a work of art, like something Zeus would throw to the skies. Modern stuff just doesn't have the same design flair. It seems everything is designed by computer to either look like a folded cardboard box or a jelly bean...
